docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:1:# FFI & iOS Bridge Debt Inventory — Detailed Findings (M10.5)
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:10:### Finding F1: Unsafe FFI Pointer Dereference — `ffi.rs:75`
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:24:**Classification:** **ACCEPTABLE**. Standard FFI ownership transfer.
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:58:**Severity:** Low (standard C FFI pattern, validated downstream)
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:117:visible (FFI clamp). No overflow risk.
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:138:### Finding F13–F14: C FFI Bounds Casts — `ffi.rs:94,296`
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:147:**Classification:** **ACCEPTABLE**. Standard FFI. Clamp bounds the second.
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:167:## Doctrine Audits D0–D5
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:169:### D0: Kernel Never Grows App Nouns — COMPLIANT
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:178:### D1: Best-Effort Rendering with Placeholders — COMPLIANT
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:186:### D2: Reactivity Contract (Composite Reverse Index) — COMPLIANT
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:195:### D3: Errors Never Cross FFI — COMPLIANT
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:197:- All FFI exports are void-returning C functions.
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:198:- No `Result` types or error enums in the FFI surface.
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:204:### D4: One Writer Per Fact — COMPLIANT
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:213:### D5: Capabilities Report, Never Decide — COMPLIANT
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:215:- iOS FFI bridge exposes capability requests as simple commands with no decision logic.
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:217:- No policy decisions in `KernelBridge.swift` or the FFI wrapper.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:31:├── nmp-app-<name>/                # generated; concrete AppAction/AppUpdate/ViewSpec + FFI
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:67:- Desktop: direct path dependency; no FFI.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:85:| §7.3 Cross-FFI subscription protocol | Single update stream (`AppReconciler.reconcile`) carrying `AppUpdate`. Platform shims (generated by CLI) adapt to `@Observable` / `Flow` / signals / iced subscriptions. | §6.1, §6.6 |
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:120:| 6. v1 release | Publish primitive kernel crates, minimal examples, migration notes, explicit future extension points | A developer can build a small event viewer without writing cleanup, dedup, replaceable handling, or FFI batching |
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:168:- **Action.** A `uniffi::Enum` variant + an async fn in `nmp-actions` that implements it. Fire-and-forget at the FFI boundary.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:170:- **AppState.** A `uniffi::Record` carrying everything the UI needs to render. Cloned across FFI.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:174:- **EventStore.** The reactive single source of truth for all Nostr events. Owned by the actor; not exposed at FFI.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:181:- **Best-effort rendering.** Doctrine D1: render what's available, refine in place; never withhold cached data; never block on fetches.
docs/plan/subsystem-matrix.md:10:| **NDK-style subscription aggregation** | [M2](m2-subscription-compilation.md) | Per `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7, the planner becomes a subscription compiler. Logical interests → per-relay plans → wire REQs, semantics-preserving merge/split. |
docs/plan/subsystem-matrix.md:13:| **FFI hardening + empirical iOS proof** | [M10.5](m10.5-ffi-hardening.md) | Dedicated stress harness, real-device measurement, simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI suite; hard gate before M11. |
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:1:# Cardinal Doctrines D0–D8
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:7:**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:9:## D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:17:- Closed FFI enums that prevent modules from contributing typed views, actions, updates, capabilities, or identity scopes.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:21:## D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:34:## D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:40:## D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:53:## D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:57:## D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:59:What crosses FFI is the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store. `AppState` carries small screen-shaped data plus a map of `ViewId → ViewPayload` for views currently in use. Closing a view evicts its payload from the snapshot. The event store itself never crosses FFI. See §6.2 and the FFI architecture appendix (§A1).
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:61:## D6. Errors never cross FFI as exceptions
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:63:Operational failures surface as `toast: Option<String>` state fields, never as exceptions or `Result<T, E>` across the FFI boundary. Long-running operations expose `busy` flags that clear on completion regardless of outcome. Native `dispatch` calls never need `try` / `catch`; native reconciler callbacks never receive a Rust error type.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:68:- Per-operation error-type proliferation across UniFFI.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:73:## D7. Capabilities report; never decide policy
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:85:## D8. Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:87:The substrate that delivers D1, D2, D4, D5 in practice. Every inserted event participates in a composite reverse index that names the views interested in it; view recompute is bounded to ≤60 deltas per second per view; the working set is hot-resident with claim-pinned overlays. Allocations after warmup are linear in active-view count, never in cached-event count. False-wakeup rate ≤ 0.10 candidates per delta.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:89:The idle-tick emit path is gated on `kernel.changed_since_emit()` — idle ticks that produce no state change must not emit snapshots (D8 regression guard).
docs/plan.md:5:> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → WoT + cross-platform + release (M13–M17; M12 Wallet deferred post-v1 per [scope-adjustments-2026-05-18](plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md)).
docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D8): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 negentropy first, REQ second · D3 outbox routing automatic, manual relay is the opt-out · D4 single writer per fact; caches derive · D5 snapshots bounded by open views · D6 errors never cross FFI as exceptions · D7 capabilities report, never decide policy · D8 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60 Hz/view, working-set bounded). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/plan.md:44:| [M10.5](plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md) | FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof | Hard gate | |
docs/plan.md:49:| [M14](plan/m14-uniffi.md) | UniFFI migration | Arc 3 — Release | |
docs/perf/firehose-bench/1779049355-replay.md:24:- This is a deterministic prototype harness. Real relay sockets, LMDB/SQLite writes, UniFFI marshaling, platform wrappers, and NSE calls are modeled because those runtime pieces do not exist yet.
docs/perf/firehose-bench/1779049355-replay.md:31:- The highest-risk unmeasured surfaces are durable storage latency, real relay burstiness, UniFFI/platform-shadow memory, and actual NIP-77 support.
docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md:1:# M14 — UniFFI migration
docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md:5:**Demo product:** iOS app, podcast app, and (incoming) Android/Desktop/Web shells all bind to the kernel via UniFFI-generated bindings produced by `nmp gen modules`, not raw C FFI.
docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md:7:**Scope.** Replace the current raw C FFI surface in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs` with the per-app generated `nmp-app-<name>` crate per ADR-0010. The iOS app stops importing `NmpCore.h` and instead imports the generated Swift module.
docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md:11:- `nmp-codegen` extended to produce UniFFI scaffolding in the generated per-app crate.
docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md:19:- iOS app builds and runs against UniFFI-generated bindings; no raw C FFI in the app target.
docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md:20:- Cross-platform consistency test (next milestone) is unblocked because the FFI shape is now identical across platforms.
docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md:24:**Runnable artifact.** iOS Twitter + iOS Podcast apps both using UniFFI. Report in `docs/perf/m14/uniffi-migration.md`.
crates/nmp-testing/tests/store_insert_path.rs:1://! §2.1 Insert API single path tests.
crates/nmp-testing/tests/store_insert_path.rs:3://! See `docs/design/lmdb/tests/insert.md` §2.1.
docs/product-spec.md:17:- Appendices: [FFI details and NIP glossary](product-spec/appendices.md)
docs/perf/firehose-bench/1779051817-replay.md:24:- This is a deterministic prototype harness. Real relay sockets, LMDB/SQLite writes, UniFFI marshaling, platform wrappers, and NSE calls are modeled because those runtime pieces do not exist yet.
docs/perf/firehose-bench/1779051817-replay.md:31:- The highest-risk unmeasured surfaces are durable storage latency, real relay burstiness, UniFFI/platform-shadow memory, and actual NIP-77 support.
docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:81:The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.
docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:103:- **Make `AppAction` / `ViewSpec` extensible via downcast / `Box<dyn Any>`.** Rejected — kills type safety at the FFI boundary; degrades into stringly-typed dispatch.
docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:104:- **Vendor in app-specific modules as cargo features on a monolithic `nmp` crate.** Rejected — explosive feature matrix; entangles compilation; doesn't solve UniFFI typing.
docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:113:| 3. UniFFI for app-level enums across crates | Resolved in `kernel-substrate.md` §5 — per-app `nmp-app-<name>` codegen crate owns the FFI exposure |
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:1:# M10.5 — FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof *(hard gate before M11 starts)*
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:5:**Demo product:** The iOS Twitter slice from [M1](m1-twitter-slice.md)–[M10](m10-blossom.md) subjected to a published, exhaustive stress harness on the iOS simulator and a real iPhone 12. The kernel↔FFI↔SwiftUI path is proven, in measured numbers, to be **rock-solid and demonstrably performant** before a single line of the podcast app is written.
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:7:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:18:  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:24:- **All M1–M10 perf reports re-run** on the final FFI surface to confirm no regressions.
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:25:- **FFI surface documentation audit** in `docs/ffi-surface.md` — every exported type, function, capability trait, and ownership/lifetime invariant called out; reviewed against `RMP-ARCHITECTURE-BIBLE.md` commandments and ADR-0010.
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:32:- `docs/design/ffi-hardening.md` — design doc enumerating every FFI failure mode and how the harness exercises it.
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:33:- `docs/ffi-surface.md` — the canonical FFI surface reference.
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:43:- Doctrine review (D0–D5) signed off on the FFI surface in writing in `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:141:Action authoring contract for the framework's own contributors (not exposed at FFI):
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:224:`TimelineItem` is a flat summary. The full event content, raw tags, signature, and provenance live in the event store inside Rust and do not cross FFI. This matches the precedent set by the bible's reference implementation (Pika): chat list is summaries; current chat loads full content on demand.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:316:Conversations are derived views; the conversation list and conversation views above (§7.6) are the user-visible surface. Decryption happens inside the actor (or inside the NSE crate, §7.14, when triggered from background). Plaintext never crosses FFI other than as fields of `ConversationView`.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:397:- FFI calls per second (dispatch / reconcile).
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:398:- FFI payload size histogram (bytes per `AppUpdate`).
docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:9:The concrete FFI API is per-app generated. `nmp-core` defines kernel primitives and extension traits; `nmp gen modules` composes the selected kernel, protocol modules, and app modules into a generated `nmp-app-<name>` crate that exposes closed typed enums to Swift/Kotlin/TypeScript.
docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:23:    /// feature flags. Infallible at the FFI boundary; catastrophic failure panics.
docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:52:`AppState` is a `uniffi::Record`. It is the entire UI's source of truth. It is cloned across FFI on every `FullState` update.
docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:78:**Platform shadow is reorganized by domain key, not `ViewId` (ADR-0005).** While the FFI delivers `AppState.views` as a `HashMap<ViewId, ViewPayload>`, the per-platform wrapper layer (generated by `nmp gen`) reorganizes the shadow into typed domain-keyed dictionaries — `profiles: [PubKey: ProfileView]`, `reactionSummaries: [EventId: ReactionSummary]`, `conversations: [PubKey: ConversationView]`, etc. — so components read by domain concept (pubkey, event id) rather than by framework handle. `ViewId` remains an internal token used by the FFI; component code never sees it. Refcounted wrappers (`useProfile`, `@Profile`, `rememberProfile`) manage subscription lifecycle behind the domain-keyed API. See ADR-0005 for the per-view-kind cache-key table.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:482:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:486:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:242:17 `ViewModule`s. Each in `podcast-core/src/views/<name>.rs`. All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:495:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:51:- **Why**: `AVAudioSession` interactions with the kernel's actor crossing FFI on every `Tick` event can introduce jitter. The reference Swift app handles this in-process; we cross FFI ~4×/s plus on every state change. On a battery-throttled iPhone 12, this is non-zero.

docs/product-spec/appendices.md:5:## Appendix A. FFI architecture in detail
docs/product-spec/appendices.md:9:The bible mandates snapshots over FFI. For a Nostr client with timelines of thousands of events, naive full-snapshots are wasteful. We deviate as follows:
docs/product-spec/appendices.md:17:The event store, gossip cache, sync watermarks, working set, and signer state all live in the actor and **never cross FFI**.
docs/product-spec/appendices.md:21:**The planner batches at ≤60Hz.** 500 reactions arriving in 100ms become ≤6 batched deltas, not 500 callbacks. Bible commandment 9 (no high-frequency FFI loops) is honored by construction.
docs/product-spec/appendices.md:33:| **Shared memory + signal.** Rust writes to mmap'd or shared heap; platform reads via raw pointers; FFI carries only "key X changed." | Game engines; Flutter+Skia for graphics state | Memory safety across FFI is hellish. Unsuitable for Swift/Kotlin idioms. Not portable to web. |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:17:A Cargo workspace shipping a Nostr-native **app kernel** (`nmp-core`), reusable **Nostr protocol modules** (`nmp-nip01`, `nmp-nip17`, `nmp-nip65`, etc.), app-owned extension modules, a codegen tool (`nmp gen modules`) that produces per-app concrete FFI enums/wrappers, FFI bindings for Swift/Kotlin/TypeScript, a wasm target, a scaffolding CLI, and reference platform shells.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:23:The framework treats common Nostr-correctness failures (stale replaceable events, lost subscriptions, mis-routed publishes, double-publication, multi-account desync, leaked secrets across FFI, naive cache invalidation, withheld cached data, blocking-on-fetch UI patterns) as **product defects in the framework** rather than as developer mistakes. The public API is designed so that the wrong thing is hard to type.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:27:## 1.5 Cardinal doctrines D0–D8
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:Summary: D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime implementation constraints). Both are equally binding. Every API decision answers to at least one; conflicts resolve in the order D0 > D1 > … > D8.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:35:| D0 | No app nouns in `nmp-core`; test surface gated behind `test-support` feature | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:36:| D1 | Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:37:| D2 | Negentropy first, REQ second | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:38:| D3 | Outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:39:| D4 | Single writer per fact; caches derive | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:40:| D5 | Snapshots bounded by what's open | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:| D6 | Errors never cross FFI as exceptions | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:42:| D7 | Capabilities report; never decide policy | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:43:| D8 | Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded; idle-tick emit gated on `changed_since_emit()` | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:102:### 3.3 Bug class extinction
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:142:| Crate | Role | FFI? |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:146:| `nmp-ffi` | UniFFI building blocks used by generated app crates | UniFFI |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:168:Generated bindings are **checked into git** under `bindings/{swift,kotlin,typescript}/`. Developers consuming the workspace as a path dependency do not need a host build to regenerate. The CI lane regenerates and diffs on every PR touching FFI-exposed types; binding drift fails the build.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:252:4. `just gen-bindings` after changing FFI-visible types.
docs/perf/firehose-bench/1779046847-capture.md:24:- This is a deterministic prototype harness. Real relay sockets, LMDB/SQLite writes, UniFFI marshaling, platform wrappers, and NSE calls are modeled because those runtime pieces do not exist yet.
docs/perf/firehose-bench/1779046847-capture.md:31:- The highest-risk unmeasured surfaces are durable storage latency, real relay burstiness, UniFFI/platform-shadow memory, and actual NIP-77 support.
docs/design/nip29/kinds.md:21:### 2.1 User-sent group events
docs/design/nip29/kinds.md:218:- **kind:11 with `h`** (both `t=discussion` and artifact-share variants) → `nmp-nip29` per §2.1.
docs/design/nip29/kinds.md:219:- **kind:9 with `h`** → `nmp-nip29` per §2.1.
docs/design/nip29/kinds.md:227:**Custom kinds with `h` tags** (livestreams, polls, files, future NIPs) follow the generic `GroupContextEvent` fallback per §2.1's "Future / extensibility" note — apps that ship custom group event kinds layer their own DomainModules without modifying `nmp-nip29`, because the `h`-tag-is-the-ownership rule is structural and works for any kind.
docs/decisions/0005-domain-keyed-platform-shadow.md:8:The platform shadow holds the data components actually read. The reconciler writes incoming `ViewBatch` payloads into a platform-side reactive structure (`@Observable` on iOS, `mutableStateOf` on Android, signals on web, iced state on desktop). Components read from that structure via the platform's native idiom — no FFI on the read path, only on subscription lifecycle.
docs/decisions/0005-domain-keyed-platform-shadow.md:14:The per-platform wrapper layer organizes the platform shadow as **typed domain-keyed dictionaries**, not a flat `[ViewId: ViewPayload]` map. `ViewId` remains an internal framework token used by the FFI but does not appear in the component-facing API.
docs/decisions/0005-domain-keyed-platform-shadow.md:64:- **Per-component FFI subscriptions** (one subscription per UI component). Rejected in the prior turn — multiplies FFI surface and pushes subscription lifecycle into native component code, which has known race conditions across fast remount/unmount in all three platforms.
docs/decisions/0005-domain-keyed-platform-shadow.md:72:- **Performance tests**: an avatar component reading `profiles[pubkey]` does not generate FFI calls after the first subscription; only payload updates flow back.
docs/plan/m15-cross-platform.md:11:- Kotlin bindings via UniFFI; cargo-ndk + Gradle pipeline.
docs/plan/test-pyramid.md:9:| Cross-FFI | UniFFI binding round-trip tests | Bindings stability, rev ordering, callback delivery | `apps/<name>/nmp-app-<name>/tests/` (post-M14) |
docs/plan/principles.md:8:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/plan/principles.md:9:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the [M11](m11-podcast.md) podcast-app proof.
docs/plan/parallelization.md:10:- **[M10.5](m10.5-ffi-hardening.md) (FFI hardening)** is itself parallelizable: the stress harness, the iPhone-12 perf rerun, the UI-script Sonnet-agent fleet, and the FFI surface audit are four independent workstreams.
docs/plan/parallelization.md:12:- **[M15](m15-cross-platform.md) (Android + Desktop + Web)** is three parallel tracks once [M14](m14-uniffi.md) (UniFFI) lands.
docs/plan/m9-messaging.md:19:- DM round-trip in `MockRelay` (alice ↔ bob): content matches; no plaintext crosses FFI other than as `ConversationMessage.body`.
docs/plan/decision-log.md:16:- **ADR-0010**: Per-app concrete enums generated at the FFI boundary. Codegen is critical-path v1 infrastructure.
docs/plan/decision-log.md:29:- **Runtime evidence.** Live mode (or replay mode with real adapters substituted for modeled segments) runs against actual LMDB, actual WebSockets, actual UniFFI marshaling. Passing here is real evidence.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:19:Verify all 3 are addressed. Doctrine D0-D8 per docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md §1.5. File size 300/500. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any remaining concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:722:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:35:| D0 | No app nouns in `nmp-core`; test surface gated behind `test-support` feature | Policy |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:779:     1	# Cardinal Doctrines D0–D8
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:785:     7	**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:787:     9	## D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:795:    17	- Closed FFI enums that prevent modules from contributing typed views, actions, updates, capabilities, or identity scopes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:799:    21	## D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:812:    34	## D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:818:    40	## D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:831:    53	## D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:835:    57	## D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:837:    59	What crosses FFI is the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store. `AppState` carries small screen-shaped data plus a map of `ViewId → ViewPayload` for views currently in use. Closing a view evicts its payload from the snapshot. The event store itself never crosses FFI. See §6.2 and the FFI architecture appendix (§A1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:839:    61	## D6. Errors never cross FFI as exceptions
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:841:    63	Operational failures surface as `toast: Option<String>` state fields, never as exceptions or `Result<T, E>` across the FFI boundary. Long-running operations expose `busy` flags that clear on completion regardless of outcome. Native `dispatch` calls never need `try` / `catch`; native reconciler callbacks never receive a Rust error type.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:846:    68	- Per-operation error-type proliferation across UniFFI.

Both are equally binding. Every API decision answers to at least one; conflicts resolve in the order D0 > D1 > … > D8.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:871:1890:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:14:| 2026-05-18 03:45 | 6 | Heartbeat #6 (autonomous). MAJOR: T1 m1-hardener landed 701d0e5 — M1 PASSES empirically. Live firehose-bench cold_start 258ms first-item / 3587ms filled-timeline; profile_thrashing 0 leaked subs over 10min. M1 ✅ DONE. Two deferrals named (memory RSS → M10.5, dispatch-rate platform gate → M14) tracked as T22. T21 meta-subscribe-analyst landed b0ae439 (substrate-fit analysis: build as reference view module, ~30 LOC compiler change for address-pointer support); two own follow-up commits 5b86de4 + 6782b97 codex-cleaned. T13 m2-codex-fixer landed 5ff0ac5 (M2 design fixes round 1). User landed PD-001 RESOLVED option (b): expanded canonical doctrine D0-D5 → D0-D8 in product-spec/overview-and-dx.md §1.5 (added D6 errors-never-FFI / D7 capabilities-report / D8 reactivity-contract; two-kind classification: D0-D5 policy / D6-D8 substrate). README aligned in ea3d40e + aef42c9. Codex review of cumulative HB5 wave (b0ae439.md) flagged 5 issues — biggest is D0 violation: public ActorCommand exposes domain nouns (OpenAuthor/ClaimProfile/etc.) from nmp-core. Tracked as T23. Worktree cleanups: T1 (ad49d31a), T21 (a5a7f37a), T13 (a67512cc) removed. T22 + T23 dispatched this tick. Pending: none. 5 agents in flight: T14 m3-codex-fixer, T18 highlighter-explorer, T19 framework-magic-reconciler, T22 m1-deferral-folder, T23 m1-codex-fixer. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:872:1892:./README.md:29:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D8).** Canonical wording from [`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5](docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected. Conflicts resolve in the order listed. **Two kinds:** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime / FFI / hot-path constraints). Both equally binding.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:873:1895:docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:7:**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:874:1899:./docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:14:| 2026-05-18 03:45 | 6 | Heartbeat #6 (autonomous). MAJOR: T1 m1-hardener landed 701d0e5 — M1 PASSES empirically. Live firehose-bench cold_start 258ms first-item / 3587ms filled-timeline; profile_thrashing 0 leaked subs over 10min. M1 ✅ DONE. Two deferrals named (memory RSS → M10.5, dispatch-rate platform gate → M14) tracked as T22. T21 meta-subscribe-analyst landed b0ae439 (substrate-fit analysis: build as reference view module, ~30 LOC compiler change for address-pointer support); two own follow-up commits 5b86de4 + 6782b97 codex-cleaned. T13 m2-codex-fixer landed 5ff0ac5 (M2 design fixes round 1). User landed PD-001 RESOLVED option (b): expanded canonical doctrine D0-D5 → D0-D8 in product-spec/overview-and-dx.md §1.5 (added D6 errors-never-FFI / D7 capabilities-report / D8 reactivity-contract; two-kind classification: D0-D5 policy / D6-D8 substrate). README aligned in ea3d40e + aef42c9. Codex review of cumulative HB5 wave (b0ae439.md) flagged 5 issues — biggest is D0 violation: public ActorCommand exposes domain nouns (OpenAuthor/ClaimProfile/etc.) from nmp-core. Tracked as T23. Worktree cleanups: T1 (ad49d31a), T21 (a5a7f37a), T13 (a67512cc) removed. T22 + T23 dispatched this tick. Pending: none. 5 agents in flight: T14 m3-codex-fixer, T18 highlighter-explorer, T19 framework-magic-reconciler, T22 m1-deferral-folder, T23 m1-codex-fixer. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:875:1901:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:Summary: D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime implementation constraints). Both are equally binding. Every API decision answers to at least one; conflicts resolve in the order D0 > D1 > … > D8.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:878:1956:docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:110:**Doctrine review (D0–D8, policy doctrines D0–D5 + substrate invariants D6–D8):**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:879:1964:./docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D8): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 negentropy first, REQ second · D3 outbox routing automatic, manual relay is the opt-out · D4 single writer per fact; caches derive · D5 snapshots bounded by open views · D6 errors never cross FFI as exceptions · D7 capabilities report, never decide policy · D8 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60 Hz/view, working-set bounded). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/c077825.md:930:2216:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:811:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/firehose-bench/1779046850-live.md:16:- This is a deterministic prototype harness. Real relay sockets, LMDB/SQLite writes, UniFFI marshaling, platform wrappers, and NSE calls are modeled because those runtime pieces do not exist yet.
docs/plan/m10-blossom.md:23:- Profile picture update through compose → kind:0 republish with new Blossom URL → in-place refinement across all open Profile / Timeline payloads (per doctrine D1).
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:3:> Sub-file of [`../nip29-crate.md`](../nip29-crate.md). Covers the routing inversion that NIP-29 forces on the M2 subscription compiler + publish planner.
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:18:1. **D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders** — the diagnostics lane (ADR-0007) wouldn't see the wire activity because it didn't go through the compiler.
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:26:Today the compiler reads a `LogicalInterest`'s filter and dispatches to one of two lanes (paraphrasing `subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3 step 2):
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:39:The `pin_to` annotation is not a regular Nostr filter field — it's an out-of-band hint carried by the `LogicalInterest` type itself, **not** sent on the wire. When a `LogicalInterest` arrives at the compiler with `pin_to: Some(url)`, the compiler skips lanes A + B entirely and produces a one-relay plan targeting `pin_to`. The `#h` value is *also* on the filter (relays expect it), but the pin is what determines routing.
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:44:LogicalInterest::new()
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:115:- For cross-protocol "publish in protocol X, then host-pin share into a group" flows (e.g. publish-and-share-highlight per `feature-inventory.md` §2.1), the *composing action lives in `highlighter-core`*, not in the X protocol crate. `nmp-nip84::PublishHighlight` stays group-unaware (it constructs a `PublishPlan` with `pin_to: None`, routing per author write relays). The composing action `highlighter-core::PublishHighlightAndShareToGroup` invokes `nmp-nip84::PublishHighlight` for the kind:9802 leg, awaits its `ActionId`, then invokes `nmp-nip29::ShareEventIntoGroup` (which takes a typed `GroupId` and sets `pin_to: Some(host)`) for the kind:16 host-pinned leg. **No protocol crate ever imports another protocol crate's `GroupId` or any other NIP-specific type; sequencing happens at the app layer.**
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:138:`nmp-nip29` declares all its `RelayPinnedInterest`s + publishes as **auth-mandatory** (a new annotation on `LogicalInterest` and on `PublishPlan`). The auth-paused relay state (`docs/design/subscription-compilation.md` §10 open question 6) applies: if the host relay's auth state is `ChallengeReceived`, all `nmp-nip29` activity for that host is paused until auth completes, then resumes.
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:149:4. `nip29_pin_to_blocks_filter_merge` — two `LogicalInterest`s with identical filter shapes but different `pin_to` values do not merge into one wire-frame.
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:152:7. `nip29_unauth_host_relay_pauses_module_activity` — a host relay in `ChallengeReceived` causes all module activity for that host to pause, surfacing in the diagnostics lane as `LogicalInterestStatus::AuthPaused`.
crates/nmp-testing/tests/store_domain_migration.rs:1://! §2.12 Domain migration success + failure tests.
crates/nmp-testing/tests/store_domain_migration.rs:3://! See `docs/design/lmdb/tests/migration.md` §2.12.
docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md:7:**Scope.** Per ADR-0006 + ADR-0008 + ADR-0009: kind:0 Profile path end-to-end against a real relay, on iOS, through real FFI. Seed-driven discovery (union of follow lists from pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55). Refcounted claim/release pattern per ADR-0005 (profile interest commit `23ae829`). Diagnostics surface per ADR-0007.
docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md:14:- ✅ Best-effort rendering (D1): placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival.
docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md:37:The `profile_thrashing` and `mount_unmount_churn` scenarios note "process RSS ≤ baseline + N MB" as assertions, but M1 has no OS-level instrumentation to measure them (requires `mach_task_basic_info` on Apple, `/proc/<pid>/status` on Linux). M10.5 is the dedicated FFI-hardening + iPhone-12 measurement pass; it adds scenario S11 to capture RSS via `mach_task_basic_info` during the canonical workflow on real hardware. The numeric gate (≤ 100 MB simulator / ≤ 150 MB iPhone 12 at 100 active views per ADR-0003 working-set gate) lives in M10.5 S11 / gates.md §G-S11.
docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md:40:The exit gate above requires "OpenView/CloseView dispatch rate ≤ 60% of mount rate (grace-period absorption working)". This validates ADR-0005's platform debounce layer — specifically the generated wrapper's 30-second grace-period absorption. The current M1 bridge is raw C FFI with no generated wrapper, so the gate cannot be measured here. M14 (UniFFI migration) produces the ADR-0005 refcounted component wrapper; the dispatch-rate gate is exercised there at 1000 mount/unmount/sec for 60 s. See T22.
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:39:- Profile placeholders → in-place refinement (D1; already done)
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:78:M10.5 FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof                             — design done
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:83:M14   UniFFI migration                                                — pending
docs/perf/firehose-bench/1779050935-replay.md:24:- This is a deterministic prototype harness. Real relay sockets, LMDB/SQLite writes, UniFFI marshaling, platform wrappers, and NSE calls are modeled because those runtime pieces do not exist yet.
docs/perf/firehose-bench/1779050935-replay.md:31:- The highest-risk unmeasured surfaces are durable storage latency, real relay burstiness, UniFFI/platform-shadow memory, and actual NIP-77 support.
docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:173:- M10.5 (FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof) must have shipped: M11.5 cannot afford to surface FFI bugs on top of the architectural rewrite.
docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:178:Per `scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md`, M9 (DMs) and M12 (wallet) are deferred to post-v1. M11.5 takes the slot the prior M12 occupied because the kernel-boundary proof case is the load-bearing v1 evidence — two app-rebuilds (podcast + Highlighter), one Nostr-shaped and one not, both proving the substrate handles arbitrary domains without growing kernel nouns. After M11.5 the ladder continues to M13 (WoT), M14 (UniFFI), M15 (cross-platform), M16 (CLI), M17 (v1 release).
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:1:# ADR 0010: Generated per-app concrete enums at the FFI boundary
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:12:The framework's existing FFI surface (UniFFI on iOS/Android, wasm-bindgen on web) expects **concrete, closed types**. Open enums don't exist in UniFFI. Two paths are possible:
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:14:1. **Generated app enum.** Each app's build runs a codegen step that produces concrete per-app `AppAction = KernelAction | Nip01Action | Nip17Action | TwitterAction | ...` enums by composing variants from the kernel, the chosen protocol modules, and the app's own core crate. The FFI exposes the per-app concrete types. Compile-time type safety end-to-end.
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:15:2. **Type-erased registry.** Modules register handlers keyed by string namespace; payloads cross FFI as `Vec<u8>` (or `serde_json::Value`). The kernel doesn't know module types statically; it dispatches via the registry. Plug-and-play module loading; loses type safety at the FFI boundary.
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:21:**Generated app enum.** Each app's build produces concrete per-app `AppAction`, `AppUpdate`, `ViewSpec`, `Capability*` enums via the `nmp gen modules` codegen step. UniFFI exposes the per-app concrete types via a thin per-app FFI crate (`nmp-app-<name>`).
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:124:Each module registers itself with the kernel at startup, providing the `dispatch` closure that knows how to deserialize its own action type. The action bytes are produced by the generated FFI layer (which has typed access) and consumed by the module (which has typed access). The kernel in the middle is module-agnostic.
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:126:### What UniFFI sees
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:128:UniFFI sees only the generated per-app `nmp-app-twitter` crate. From its perspective, `AppAction` is a normal closed enum. Bindings regenerate deterministically.
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:146:- **Cleaner FFI surface.** No `Vec<u8>` blobs across FFI. No JSON parsing on the platform side. No string-keyed switches.
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:152:- **Per-app FFI crate adds a build step.** Each app has its own `nmp-app-<name>` crate. Slight build-graph complexity. Mitigation: scaffolded by `nmp init` and `nmp add module`; developers rarely touch it.
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:159:- **Loses safety where it matters most.** The FFI boundary is exactly where strong typing has the highest payoff. Swift / Kotlin / TypeScript consumers benefit from idiomatic typed enums. Vec<u8> + opaque dispatch reproduces JavaScript-shaped fragility in three statically-typed languages.
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:170:- A per-app FFI crate becomes the canonical FFI surface; raw `nmp-ffi` is for the kernel only.
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:178:| Trait object dispatch over `Box<dyn Action>` | UniFFI doesn't support trait objects across FFI cleanly. Same root issue. |
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:180:| Procedural macros generating per-module FFI | Tried in head. Inverts the dependency direction — modules would need to know about FFI exposure. The codegen tool generating an aggregating crate is cleaner. |
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:185:- Phase 1a.2 (Profile module on desktop) generates `nmp-app-twitter` with `nmp-core` + `nmp-nip01` + `twitter-core`. UniFFI bindings compile.
crates/nmp-core/src/actor/tick.rs:2://! the `emit_interval` utility.  Separated from the main loop so that the D8

crates/nmp-core/src/actor/tick.rs:97:            "D8 regression: actor emitted {idle_count} snapshot(s) without any \
docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:17:Per doctrine D6: "errors never cross FFI as exceptions." The corollary is that the *store itself* must never silently produce an incorrect answer because of partial writes. Any scenario where two separate `lmdb::Environment` handles could each commit independently would violate this — we would have no way to roll back one side when the other fails.
docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:19:**Why this matters (D6):** if the store returns wrong data because of a partial write, that wrong data eventually reaches FFI as an `AppUpdate`. There is no way to signal "this snapshot is partially corrupt" through a `Result<T, StoreError>` once the data is already in the `AppUpdate` struct. The only safe design is to prevent partial writes at the store layer.
docs/design/view-catalog/template-and-enumeration.md:9:> **Status:** Rev 2, reframed per ADR-0009. These view kinds are not in `nmp-core`; apps consume them by adding the owning module crate to `nmp.toml` and regenerating the per-app FFI crate.
docs/design/view-catalog/template-and-enumeration.md:82:`ViewId` is an internal FFI token. Wrappers refcount per key, dispatch `OpenView`/`CloseView` to Rust, and enforce the same warmth/eviction policy as the kernel.

docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:31:Swift `ViewModels/` **disappear** — they become Rust ViewModules whose output crosses FFI as typed ViewBatch deltas.

docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:11:ADR-0006 chose a deliberately narrow demo target — kind:0 profile metadata on desktop iced — as the Phase 1a vertical slice. The point was risk reduction: prove the architecture works in code with the fewest variables, no FFI, one view kind, read-only.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:41:| Reference platform | Desktop iced (rlib-linked, no FFI) | Maintained alongside iOS as a UniFFI-free diagnostic target — if something breaks on iOS but works on desktop, it's an FFI/Xcode issue, not architecture. |
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:45:| FFI tool | UniFFI (per spec §3) | Already specified; this commits to it. |
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:47:| Storage backend | In-memory for 1a.1–1a.2; LMDB from 1a.3 | LMDB is real enough to expose persistence bugs; in-memory keeps the first two sub-phases focused on actor+FFI. |
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:68:Includes ADR-0007 diagnostics minimum: one `RelayStatus` row, one `LogicalInterestStatus::Profile { pubkey }`, one `WireSubscriptionStatus` for the kind:0 REQ.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:81:**Exit gate.** iOS simulator launches; avatar component renders against primal exactly as on desktop; cross-platform consistency check (same scripted action sequence on desktop and iOS produces byte-identical `AppState` JSON at each checkpoint); UniFFI bindings regenerate deterministically.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:94:**Exit gate.** Cold launch with primed LMDB renders the seed-driven timeline in ≤ 1.5s, showing fresh content from hundreds of authors followed by the seeds; tap an author → profile screen → back works; kind:0 arriving mid-scroll updates all author rows in place per doctrine D1; updating one seed's kind:3 mid-session re-resolves the timeline's author set without manual intervention; reactivity-bench `--standard` continues to pass at the larger author-set size; firehose-bench `live` for `sustained_firehose` (running at the real seed-author scale, not modeled) lands within budgets.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:131:- Diagnostics screen: opens `ViewSpec::NetworkDiagnostics` per ADR-0007; shows RelayStatus + active LogicalInterestStatus + WireSubscriptionStatus.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:142:| 1a.2 | UniFFI workflow concrete recipe; iOS `AppManager` reference pattern (50 lines of Swift). Both mostly mechanical. Optional: `keychain-rs` integration plan. |
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:150:Approximately **8 weeks for one experienced developer**, less with prior Rust + iOS experience, more if UniFFI / Xcode toolchain bite hard (they will at least once).
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:156:The desktop iced binary built in 1a.1 stays alive throughout 1a.2 through 1a.6. It links the rlib directly (no UniFFI). It is not a shipping product, but it acts as a non-FFI reference:
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:159:- When a behavior is right on desktop but wrong on iOS → UniFFI / SwiftUI / build issue.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:169:- **iOS toolchain risk is front-loaded.** UniFFI surprises, xcframework build, Xcode versioning — all happen in 1a.2. If they don't resolve cleanly, we know early and the desktop reference target is the fallback validation path.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:176:- **Skip the desktop reference target.** Rejected — loses the UniFFI-vs-architecture debugging shortcut.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:177:- **Start with iOS directly (skip 1a.1 desktop slice).** Rejected — UniFFI noise in the very first runtime would conflate architecture bugs with toolchain bugs.
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:13:The classic failure mode at this stage is **horizontal expansion** — building "the EventStore" comprehensively, then "the planner" comprehensively, then "the views" comprehensively, then finally stitching them together at the end, only to discover that the FFI surface or the relay adapter or the storage backend doesn't actually compose the way the model assumed.
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:25:│  Desktop iced shell (no FFI; direct rlib link)               │
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:37:                       │ AppAction / AppUpdate (no FFI for slice;
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:69:- **FFI.** Desktop slice uses direct rlib linking (per spec §4.3.1, this is bible-canonical for desktop). UniFFI is wired in when porting the slice to iOS/Android.
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:99:- UniFFI marshaling cost (slice is desktop-only).
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:112:- **Subsequent expansion has a working substrate to build on.** Adding LMDB is a `Box<dyn EventStore>` swap. Adding multi-relay is a planner change. Adding negentropy is a planner change. Adding iOS is a UniFFI wrap of the existing actor. None require redesigning the architecture.
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:119:- **Slice through FFI to iOS first.** Rejected — desktop's direct rlib linking lets us validate the architecture without UniFFI noise. iOS wrap comes after the actor's API is stable.
docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:56:- **Computed-from-native fields.** All formatting, all derivations live in Rust per doctrine D5.
docs/plan/status.md:10:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/plan/status.md:30:- UniFFI bindings (current iOS bridge is raw C FFI).
docs/plan/status.md:43:- **No dedicated FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof gate before the kernel-boundary proof.** The prior M11 implicitly assumed the FFI surface was ready; this rewrite makes it a separate milestone (M10.5).
docs/plan/status.md:46:The plan below is a single ladder of eighteen milestones (M0–M17, with M10.5 inserted as the FFI gate), each producing a runnable artifact, ordered so that each milestone strictly adds capabilities to the prior demoable product.
docs/perf/m1/build-verification.md:139:- Simulator chosen: **iPhone 17 / iOS 26.5** (UUID `C380BA01-AD22-4F4A-8524-A309DA15144C`)
docs/perf/m1/build-verification.md:170:1. **firehose-bench is a prototype harness.** All scenarios run deterministic simulations — real relay sockets, LMDB/SQLite writes, UniFFI marshaling, and NSE calls are modeled, not measured. Gates validate planned budgets, not real I/O behavior.
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:15:Both surfaces are easy to get wrong. If relay status becomes raw socket callbacks, platform code starts owning protocol policy. If every diagnostic transition crosses FFI immediately, the status UI becomes its own firehose. If non-Nostr data is forced into fake Nostr events, the event store becomes polluted with records that do not obey Nostr semantics.
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:119:pub struct LogicalInterestStatus {
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:121:    pub state: LogicalInterestState,
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:139:pub enum LogicalInterestState {
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:180:- One `LogicalInterestStatus::Profile { pubkey }`.
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:185:This validates the shape without requiring the full outbox planner, NIP-77 sync engine, or FFI.
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:222:- Debug UIs can inspect relay and subscription state without raw socket callbacks crossing FFI.
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:230:- **Raw callback stream for relay events.** Rejected. It creates a second reactive system, leaks protocol details into native code, and can exceed FFI budgets under firehose traffic.
crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:149:struct LogicalInterestStatus {
crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:219:    logical_interests: Vec<LogicalInterestStatus>,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/afc5475.md:11:- **ffi-stress/main.rs:1** — FFI stress is an exit-1 stub, but justfile:52 advertises it as a pre-merge gate. Implement or remove the gate recipes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/afc5475.md:12:- **kernel/mod.rs:286** — D4 not actually enforced; new `store` is dead while `events: HashMap` remains the live writer at line 304.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/afc5475.md:14:- Debt markers present: "skeleton", "deferred", "not yet implemented", "stub" in LMDB/store/FFI paths.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/afc5475.md:23:FFI surface: no new exported C symbol in ffi.rs; no ADR needed.
crates/nmp-testing/bin/firehose-bench/scenarios/timeline.rs:48:        vec!["Modeled cold start is comfortably under the proposed gates; real storage open and FFI startup still need measurement.".to_string()],
docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:103:- **Bare pubkey path.** If no kind:0 exists in the store yet, `open()` returns a placeholder-filled payload and the reverse-index dependency triggers `on_event_inserted` when one arrives. Doctrine D1 prohibits returning `None` here.
docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:234:- **Author kind:0 not yet loaded.** Per doctrine D1, render the item with placeholders. The `by_author` reindex on `on_projection_changed` updates the item in place. **The post is rendered immediately.**
crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs:75:// does not distinguish between `extern "C"` FFI boundaries and ordinary Rust functions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b4b6afa.md:3:**Merge:** docs(m11): fix codex feedback (file sizes, harness, D5, D4, rig.rs gate, OPML, counts)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b4b6afa.md:13:No extra TODO/FIXME/unimplemented found. No file crossed 500 LOC hard limit. capabilities.md and podcast-core.md remained over 300 soft limit but this predated the merge. No new kernel/FFI ADR required.
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:110:**Doctrine review (D0–D8, policy doctrines D0–D5 + substrate invariants D6–D8):**
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:111:- D0 (kernel boundary): `ActorCommand` and `spawn_actor` are now gated behind
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:113:- D1 (best-effort rendering): unchanged — still one actor thread; no render path touched
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:114:- D2 (negentropy first): no subscription policy changed
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:115:- D3 (outbox automatic): no relay code touched
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:116:- D4 (single writer per fact): the poll only calls `pending_view_requests()`,
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:118:- D5 (snapshots bounded): no new view kinds added
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:119:- D6 (errors never cross FFI): no FFI surface changed
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:120:- D7 (capabilities report): no capability decisions added
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:121:- D8 (reactivity contract): `emit_now` is now gated on `changed_since_emit()`
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:131:**Doctrine review (D0–D8):**
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:132:- D0–D5: Same reasoning as above.  `maybe_open_timeline()` is idempotent; it
docs/perf/m1/baseline.md:134:- D6–D8: No FFI, capability, or reactivity-budget changes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:17:Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:18:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:19:- D1: best-effort rendering
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:20:- D2: negentropy first
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:21:- D3: outbox routing automatic
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:22:- D4: single writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:23:- D5: snapshots bounded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:131: - Android / Desktop / Web targets — post-M14 (UniFFI migration) and M15 (cross-platform).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:206:-Per `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("the developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does") and ADR-0007's domain-of-diagnostics separation, end-user product UIs do not show "relay X is serving 12 authors." That is operator/debug surface. Normal apps consume the `LogicalInterestStatus` summaries; `RelayCoveragePayload` is for the diagnostics screen (proof iOS app screenshot in `docs/perf/m2/outbox-routing.md` per `docs/plan.md` line 139).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:207:+Per `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("the developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does") and ADR-0007's domain-of-diagnostics separation, end-user product UIs do not show "relay X is serving 12 authors." That is operator/debug surface. Normal apps consume the `LogicalInterestStatus` summaries; `RelayCoveragePayload` is for the diagnostics screen (proof iOS app screenshot in `docs/perf/m2/outbox-routing.md` per [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:219: - **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:260: - The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:281: ## 9.2 What the test asserts
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:302: (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:326:-- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:346:-- UniFFI bindings (current iOS bridge is raw C FFI).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:359:-- **No dedicated FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof gate before the kernel-boundary proof.** The prior M11 implicitly assumed the FFI surface was ready; this rewrite makes it a separate milestone (M10.5).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:362:-The plan below is a single ladder of eighteen milestones (M0–M17, with M10.5 inserted as the FFI gate), each producing a runnable artifact, ordered so that each milestone strictly adds capabilities to the prior demoable product.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:380:-4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:381:-5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:409:-**Scope.** Per ADR-0006 + ADR-0008 + ADR-0009: kind:0 Profile path end-to-end against a real relay, on iOS, through real FFI. Seed-driven discovery (union of follow lists from pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55). Refcounted claim/release pattern per ADR-0005 (profile interest commit `23ae829`). Diagnostics surface per ADR-0007.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:416:-- ✅ Best-effort rendering (D1): placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:587:-**Scope.** Per spec doctrine D4 (single writer per fact) extended to account scope:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:623:-- DM round-trip in `MockRelay` (alice ↔ bob): content matches; no plaintext crosses FFI other than as `ConversationMessage.body`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:651:-- Profile picture update through compose → kind:0 republish with new Blossom URL → in-place refinement across all open Profile / Timeline payloads (per doctrine D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:657:-### M10.5 — FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof *(hard gate before M11 starts)*
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:659:-**Demo product:** The iOS Twitter slice from M1–M10 subjected to a published, exhaustive stress harness on the iOS simulator and a real iPhone 12. The kernel↔FFI↔SwiftUI path is proven, in measured numbers, to be **rock-solid and demonstrably performant** before a single line of the podcast app is written.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:661:-**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:672:-  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:677:-- **All M1–M10 perf reports re-run** on the final FFI surface to confirm no regressions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:678:-- **FFI surface documentation audit** in `docs/ffi-surface.md` — every exported type, function, capability trait, and ownership/lifetime invariant called out; reviewed against `RMP-ARCHITECTURE-BIBLE.md` commandments and ADR-0010.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:685:-- `docs/design/ffi-hardening.md` — design doc enumerating every FFI failure mode and how the harness exercises it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:686:-- `docs/ffi-surface.md` — the canonical FFI surface reference.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:696:-- Doctrine review (D0–D5) signed off on the FFI surface in writing in `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:730:-Swift `ViewModels/` **disappear** — they become Rust ViewModules whose output crosses FFI as typed ViewBatch deltas.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:851:-### M14 — UniFFI migration
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:853:-**Demo product:** iOS app, podcast app, and (incoming) Android/Desktop/Web shells all bind to the kernel via UniFFI-generated bindings produced by `nmp gen modules`, not raw C FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:855:-**Scope.** Replace the current raw C FFI surface in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs` with the per-app generated `nmp-app-<name>` crate per ADR-0010. The iOS app stops importing `NmpCore.h` and instead imports the generated Swift module.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:859:-- `nmp-codegen` extended to produce UniFFI scaffolding in the generated per-app crate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:867:-- iOS app builds and runs against UniFFI-generated bindings; no raw C FFI in the app target.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:868:-- Cross-platform consistency test (next milestone) is unblocked because the FFI shape is now identical across platforms.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:871:-**Runnable artifact.** iOS Twitter + iOS Podcast apps both using UniFFI. Report in `docs/perf/m14/uniffi-migration.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:883:-- Kotlin bindings via UniFFI; cargo-ndk + Gradle pipeline.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:964:-| **NDK-style subscription aggregation** | M2 | Per `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7, the planner becomes a subscription compiler. Logical interests → per-relay plans → wire REQs, semantics-preserving merge/split. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:967:-| **FFI hardening + empirical iOS proof** | M10.5 | Dedicated stress harness, real-device measurement, simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI suite; hard gate before M11. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:1016:-- **M10.5 (FFI hardening)** is itself parallelizable: the stress harness, the iPhone-12 perf rerun, the UI-script Sonnet-agent fleet, and the FFI surface audit are four independent workstreams.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:1018:-- **M15 (Android + Desktop + Web)** is three parallel tracks once M14 (UniFFI) lands.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:1035:-| Cross-FFI | UniFFI binding round-trip tests | Bindings stability, rev ordering, callback delivery | `apps/<name>/nmp-app-<name>/tests/` (post-M14) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:1080:-- **ADR-0010**: Per-app concrete enums generated at the FFI boundary. Codegen is critical-path v1 infrastructure.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:1093:-- **Runtime evidence.** Live mode (or replay mode with real adapters substituted for modeled segments) runs against actual LMDB, actual WebSockets, actual UniFFI marshaling. Passing here is real evidence.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:1111:+| [M10.5](plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md) | FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof | Hard gate | |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:1116:+| [M14](plan/m14-uniffi.md) | UniFFI migration | Arc 3 — Release | |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2027:+| **NDK-style subscription aggregation** | [M2](m2-subscription-compilation.md) | Per `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7, the planner becomes a subscription compiler. Logical interests → per-relay plans → wire REQs, semantics-preserving merge/split. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2030:+| **FFI hardening + empirical iOS proof** | [M10.5](m10.5-ffi-hardening.md) | Dedicated stress harness, real-device measurement, simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI suite; hard gate before M11. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2083:+| Cross-FFI | UniFFI binding round-trip tests | Bindings stability, rev ordering, callback delivery | `apps/<name>/nmp-app-<name>/tests/` (post-M14) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2098: `AppState` is a `uniffi::Record`. It is the entire UI's source of truth. It is cloned across FFI on every `FullState` update.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2235:docs/aim.md:171:A **scaffolding CLI** (`<framework> init`) generates a complete starter project: the Rust core crate, the UniFFI binding generator, an iOS SwiftUI app, an Android Compose app, an iced desktop app, a web wasm shell, the `justfile` build orchestrator, an optional Nix flake. The starter app implements login, a timeline, compose, a profile screen, and DMs. It builds and runs on all four platforms immediately. This is modeled directly on RMP's `rmp init`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2240:docs/plan.md:5:> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2249:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:7:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2284:docs/design/view-catalog/conversation-and-cross-cutting.md:7:A paginated message list for a single NIP-17 DM peer (1:1 or a single group). Messages are decrypted server-side in the actor; plaintext crosses FFI only as fields of `ConversationMessage`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2286:docs/design/framework-magic.md:5:> **Source directives:** `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` "Framework magic contract" section; `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12; `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (cardinal doctrines D0–D5) + §3.3 (bug-class extinction); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1–§7.8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2316:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2355:docs/design/ffi-hardening/ci.md:198:            fails.append("FFI grep yielded TODO/FIXME tokens; see §7.1")
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2363:docs/design/ffi-hardening/scenarios-detail.md:62:recoverable error is deferred to M14 (UniFFI migration); tracked as
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md: WARNING: stopped searching binary file after match (found "\0" byte around offset 256048)
docs/design/view-catalog/conversation-and-cross-cutting.md:7:A paginated message list for a single NIP-17 DM peer (1:1 or a single group). Messages are decrypted server-side in the actor; plaintext crosses FFI only as fields of `ConversationMessage`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5b86de4.md:9:1. Address-pointer InterestShape decision was deferred ("v1.x fallback") — picked now.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5b86de4.md:15:- **`docs/research/ndk/meta-subscribe.md:150`** — D1 placeholder wording is off. It says shortened npub parsed from an `a`-tag plus `"loading"-style content`, but D1's placeholder contract says content body placeholder is empty string with no loading gate. Also an `e`-tag target has no expected author. Split this into address-placeholder vs event-id-placeholder expectations and avoid "loading-style" language.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5b86de4.md:20:FFI/ADR: no blocker. When implemented, `NaddrCoord` should be a named generated record if it crosses `ViewSpec`, not a tuple-shaped FFI type.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/59f8d1b.md:98: Reads happen at action boot only. Per RMP bible commandment #2, keys never cross FFI as part of dispatch — they live on the platform side; the bridge resolves and injects them when executing the capability.

docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:1:# Design: FFI hardening stress harness (M10.5)
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:8:This is the design spec for the M10.5 dedicated FFI-surface hardening pass. The
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:10:C FFI between `crates/nmp-core` and `ios/NmpStress` is rock-solid before a
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:17:1. **Empirical proof of FFI invariants.** Every claim made in the RMP bible
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:19:   (`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D0–D5), and every ownership
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:20:   rule in the current FFI surface is verified by a concrete harness scenario
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:24:   FFI bugs a non-social-domain consumer would otherwise find the hard way.
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:36:- **Changing the FFI shape.** The current surface (14 raw `extern "C"`
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:38:  what we harden. Migrating to UniFFI + per-app generated enums per
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:43:  web FFI hardening is a separate milestone.
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:46:  harness here exercises the *FFI surface*, not the *protocol surface*.
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:49:## 2. FFI surface inventory
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:51:The current FFI surface is **14 exported C symbols** in
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:66:| 8 | `nmp_app_open_author(*mut, *const c_char)` | `void nmp_app_open_author(void *app, const char *pubkey)` | UTF-8 C string, expected 64-char lowercase hex pubkey. Hex-validated via `is_hex_pubkey`. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace. Empty / non-hex inputs are **silently dropped** (see §7 finding D3-gap). | Silent no-op on null app or null pubkey. |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:74:### 2.1 Callback contract (`UpdateCallback`)
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:114:| S1 | Mount/unmount churn | actor recv + refcount | D5 (snapshot bounded), bible #5 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:116:| S3 | Snapshot pressure | listener serialization | bible #9 (≤60 Hz), D5 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:117:| S4 | Reconciler back-pressure | listener channel growth | bible #9, D1 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:118:| S5 | Reentrancy | callback re-enters FFI | bible #3, deadlock-free |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:120:| S7 | Error-shape exhaustion | every invalid input path | D3 (no errors cross FFI) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:121:| S8 | Subscription planner DOS | OpenView/CloseView storm | D2 (≤60 Hz/view), D5 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:122:| S9 | Relay flap | reconnect + watermark | bible #7, D2 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:130:1. **`crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/`** — Rust binary. Drives the FFI
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:176:├── doctrine-review.md       # D0–D5 sign-off (M10.5 exit-gate artifact)
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:196:| D3-doc | `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/status.rs::relay_status_for` | Doc that `last_error`/`last_notice` are advisory data fields (D3-compliant: errors as state, not as FFI returns) | 3 min |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:214:### 7.2 D3 structural gap (named, not hidden)
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:216:The harness will exercise every typed FFI input path that fails
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:217:validation (§3 scenario S7). The current FFI is **silently no-op** on
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:220:caller and without setting any state field. This is **D3-compliant in
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:221:the strict sense** (no error crosses FFI as a value) **but
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:222:D3-incomplete in the user-visible sense** (no toast surfaces in
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:225:The debt-inventory's D3 audit (lines 317–334) concludes the same:
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:226:errors-as-data crosses FFI via `RelayStatus.last_error` and
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:234:a breaking FFI change.
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:237:The M14 UniFFI migration moves the surface to typed `Result`-shaped
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:242:Full D0–D5 line-item-to-scenario mapping in
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:247:| **D0** kernel never grows app nouns | debt-inventory §3 D0 audit + S6 (the kernel does not grow capability variants under churn) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:248:| **D1** best-effort rendering with placeholders | S3 (snapshot pressure) + S4 (reconciler back-pressure) + code proof (ProfileCard.placeholder) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:249:| **D2** ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound | S2, S3, S8 — emit-rate cap, planner dedup |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:250:| **D3** errors never cross FFI | S7 (exhaustion) + §7.2 (toast bridge) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:251:| **D4** one writer per fact | S1, S5 — refcount only mutated on actor thread; reentrancy under same single-writer rule |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:252:| **D5** capabilities report, never decide | CapabilityModule code proof + S6 (relay capability lifecycle storms) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:269:   typed `Result` via UniFFI in M14. Recommend: keep it scalar for
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:293:   M14 UniFFI migration replaces raw pointers with typed handles, eliminating
