docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:131:## 6.3 Public surface (compiler-facing, not FFI-facing)
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:221:- **No FFI types.** `MailboxesPayload` is exposed at FFI via the per-app generated enum (per ADR-0010 codegen); the crate itself ships pure Rust.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:227:- `nmp-core::substrate::{ViewModule, ViewContext, InterestContext, LogicalInterest,
docs/design/nip29-crate.md:23:Treating NIP-29 as "just another kind range" would force NMP's kernel actor or M2 compiler to grow group-aware special cases, violating ADR-0009 doctrine D0 ("the kernel never grows app nouns"). The crate is the boundary where the special cases live; the kernel sees a generic `RelayPinnedInterest` and `RelayPinnedPublish` it already knows how to handle (see `routing.md`).
docs/design/nip29-crate.md:29:├── nmp-core/                  # kernel substrate — D0: knows nothing of groups
docs/design/nip29-crate.md:63:| `GroupDiscussion` | 11 (when `h` present and `["t","discussion"]` marker) | `(host_relay_url, group_id, event_id)` | Threaded discussion *roots only*. Replies today are public NIP-22 kind:1111 *without* `h`, owned by `nmp-nip22` (verified per `kinds.md` §2.1 against Highlighter's `comments.rs::publish_comment`). `nmp-nip29::GroupComment` exists for *future* h-tagged in-room comments (Highlighter post-M11.5 may attach `h` to in-room composers; until then `GroupComment` is forward-looking and not consumed by the M11.5 Highlighter rebuild). |
docs/design/nip29-crate.md:74:These are the projections the UI consumes. Each declares `LogicalInterest`s the M2 compiler turns into wire-level REQs, with routing pinned to the host relay per `routing.md`.
docs/design/nip29-crate.md:86:### 3.3 `ActionModule` impls (15)
docs/design/nip29-crate.md:103:| `PostArtifact` | 11 with `h` and *no* `t=discussion` marker, carrying catalog reference tags per `kinds.md` §2.1 | same as above | Host relay only. |
docs/design/nip29-crate.md:152:| `DiscussionsWithReplyCounts` | `nmp-nip29::GroupDiscussions` + `nmp-nip22::Comment { e: <discussion_id> }` per discussion root | Discussion replies in Highlighter today are *non-h-tagged* NIP-22 comments (verified per `kinds.md` §2.1 notes), so they live in `nmp-nip22` and route per the replier's NIP-65 write relays. The count + latest-reply join happens in `highlighter-core`'s `project()`. (If/when Highlighter changes its composer to attach `h`, the dependency shifts to `nmp-nip29::GroupComment` with no other code change — that's the point of the substrate's generic composite-key joins.) |
docs/design/nip29-crate.md:167:- All 15 `ActionModule` impls per §3.3 (with host-relay-pin routing fully wired through the M2 publish planner)

docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:238:- UniFFI records/enums for exposed specs, payloads, deltas, actions, and capability reports,
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:334:1. Should app extension modules compile into one generated app enum, or should the runtime use a type-erased registry at the FFI boundary?
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:336:3. Can UniFFI comfortably expose generated app-level enums across multiple extension crates, or do we need a thin app-specific FFI crate per app?

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:218:./docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:241:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:88:- Zero open TODO/FIXME/unimplemented in FFI/actor/relay/kernel/iOS bridge
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:244:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:603:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:359:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1488:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5869:+docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:378:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:508:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5893:+docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:396:The FFI and iOS bridge code is **clean of blocking debt markers** (TODO/FIXME/unimplemented/panic). docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:511:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5914:+docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:666:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8173:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:745:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1607:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:905:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1552:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

crates/nmp-testing/bin/firehose-bench/report.rs:80:        "The highest-risk unmeasured surfaces are durable storage latency, real relay burstiness, UniFFI/platform-shadow memory, and actual NIP-77 support.".to_string(),
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:123:    /// set is a policy choice that lives inside lane 4 (User-configured). D3: the operator
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:216:Per `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("the developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does") and ADR-0007's domain-of-diagnostics separation, end-user product UIs do not show "relay X is serving 12 authors." That is operator/debug surface. Normal apps consume the `LogicalInterestStatus` summaries; `RelayCoveragePayload` is for the diagnostics screen (proof iOS app screenshot in `docs/perf/m2/outbox-routing.md` per [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)).
docs/design/framework-magic.md:3:> **Status:** Draft. Research citations folded in from `docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md` and `docs/research/ndk/kind3-auto-tracking.md`. Doctrine wording aligned with `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (D0–D8 canonical set).
docs/design/framework-magic.md:5:> **Source directives:** `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` "Framework magic contract" section; `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (cardinal doctrines D0–D8) + §3.3 (bug-class extinction); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1–§7.8.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:30:| C1 | Replaceable-event supersession (kind 0 / 3 / 10000–19999) on insert | replaceable.md | `c1_replaceable_supersedes_on_insert` | **[PARTIAL]** kind:0 done; kind:3 and kind:10002 pending M3 | spec §7.1 row "Replaceable kinds"; §3.3 bug #1 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:31:| C2 | Parameterized replaceable supersession (30000–39999) by `(pubkey, kind, d-tag)` | replaceable.md | `c2_parameterized_replaceable_supersedes_by_dtag` | **[PENDING M3]** | spec §7.1 row "Parameterized replaceable"; §3.3 bug #1 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:34:| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:35:| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:37:| C8 | Subscription planner deduplicates overlapping interests into one wire REQ per relay, auto-closes on EOSE / last-consumer-drop, and buffers ingress to ≤60Hz per view | subs.md | `c8_subscriptions_coalesce_autoclose_and_buffer` | **[PENDING M2]** | spec §7.2; §3.3 bug #2, bug #8 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:38:| C9 | Provenance preserved: same event id arriving from N relays merges into one stored event with N-entry provenance set; original `id` and signature untouched | sync.md | `c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries` | **[PENDING M3]** | aim §6 doctrine 10; spec §7.1 row "Provenance"; §3.3 bug #10 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:41:| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:42:| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[PARTIAL]** author_display placeholder done; author_picture_url still Option<String> violating D1; in-place refinement **[PENDING M2/M3]** | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:82:- `subs.md` §3 — Applesauce's logical-vs-wire split: `EventModels.model()` at `event-models.ts:50-86` is the logical layer (one shared pipeline per `(constructor, args)` hash); the underlying `EventStore.insert$` / `remove$` streams are the wire layer. NMP's `LogicalInterest` (`subscription-compilation/intro.md` §2.1) covers the same split.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:89:- This document does **not** introduce new types or traits — `PublishPlanner`, `ViewModule`, `LogicalInterest`, `SubscriptionCompiler`, `MailboxCache`, `KeyringCapability` are already defined in the cited design docs and product spec. The contract uses those names.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e8b1726.md:27:All four codex issues are resolved. No new TODO/FIXME/unimplemented! introduced. All changed files remain under 300 LOC soft limit (gates.md: 303 LOC, marginally over soft limit — acceptable). No FFI surface changed in code; no new ADR required. Doctrine compliance intact.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:40:   for an unrelated pubkey does NOT churn plan-ids (D8: recompile cost must be bounded by
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:46:6. **Auth-paused relays in compiled plans.** If a relay is in `RelayAuthState::ChallengeReceived`, the compiler still produces a plan that assigns interests to it (so reconnect-after-auth resumes correctly), but emission must pause. Is the pause modeled inside the compiler (per-relay gate) or inside the wire-emitter (consumes plans, applies pause)? Bias: wire-emitter, but the compiler must surface the pause as a fact for `LogicalInterestStatus`. Resolve before M5.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:48:8. **Relay-count optimization (greedy set-cover).** Stage 3 currently assigns interests to every declared write relay. Applesauce's `selectOptimalRelays` (`docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md` §3, `relay-selection.ts:14-93`) shows a greedy set-cover heuristic that caps to `maxConnections` relays while maximizing author coverage. NMP should add an equivalent cap policy in a future ADR, after M2 proves the basic per-relay fan-out is correct. Referenced by `compiler.md` §3.3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:15:You are reviewing merge 8d633e8 (research(applesauce+ndk): deep-dive notes for M2 outbox + framework-magic) on master in nostr-multi-platform. This adds research notes from studying the Applesauce and NDK codebases to inform M2 outbox routing and the framework-magic contract. Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:17:- D1: best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place; no spinners gating renderable content
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:18:- D2: negentropy first, REQ second; every filter/relay pair is a tracked sync target
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:19:- D3: outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:20:- D4: single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:21:- D5: snapshots bounded by what is open

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2193:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:81:The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2202:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:113:| 3. UniFFI for app-level enums across crates | Resolved in `kernel-substrate.md` §5 — per-app `nmp-app-<name>` codegen crate owns the FFI exposure |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2239:docs/design/framework-magic.md:5:> **Source directives:** `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` "Framework magic contract" section; `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12; `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (cardinal doctrines D0–D5) + §3.3 (bug-class extinction); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1–§7.8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2243:docs/design/framework-magic.md:30:| C1 | Replaceable-event supersession (kind 0 / 3 / 10000–19999) on insert | replaceable.md | `c1_replaceable_supersedes_on_insert` | **[DONE]** kernel | spec §7.1 row "Replaceable kinds"; §3.3 bug #1 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2244:docs/design/framework-magic.md:34:| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2245:docs/design/framework-magic.md:35:| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2246:docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2247:docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2249:docs/design/framework-magic.md:41:| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2250:docs/design/framework-magic.md:42:| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2261:docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:39:- Profile placeholders → in-place refinement (D1; already done)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2278:docs/plan.md:5:> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2280:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2292:docs/aim.md:13:The framing concern is one sentence: **make it nearly impossible to build a broken Nostr application.** Today, building a Nostr client involves dozens of subtle correctness pitfalls — stale replaceable events, lost subscriptions, wrong relays for wrong events, race conditions between local state and relay state, leaked signing operations, multi-account state desync. The framework's job is to make each of those classes of bug structurally impossible through the safe app-kernel and FFI API: not merely documented as a footgun or caught by a linter, but ruled out by the type system, actor ownership, and public API surface. Lower-level Rust escape hatches, when they exist for internal subsystems or tests, must be explicitly named, instrumented, and covered by regression tests.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2298:docs/aim.md:99:The architectural delta is the core idea of this project: use the Rust Nostr SDK family for protocol primitives, then build a new Rust application kernel above it. We are not forking the Rust SDK and we are not porting Applesauce or NDK APIs. We are creating the missing multiplatform app layer: actor-owned state, bounded FFI projections, canonical store semantics, subscription and action lifecycle, storage/metrics/test harnesses, and extension seams for later product modules.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2299:docs/aim.md:111:In our Rust framing, the actor owns the event store as internal substrate. `AppState` is not the store; it is the bounded UI projection of currently open views plus small app metadata. The full event store never crosses FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2307:docs/aim.md:171:A **scaffolding CLI** (`<framework> init`) generates a complete starter project: the Rust core crate, the UniFFI binding generator, an iOS SwiftUI app, an Android Compose app, an iced desktop app, a web wasm shell, the `justfile` build orchestrator, an optional Nix flake. The starter app implements login, a timeline, compose, a profile screen, and DMs. It builds and runs on all four platforms immediately. This is modeled directly on RMP's `rmp init`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2309:docs/aim.md:184:│   │                              # outbox routing. Pure Rust, no FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2314:docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6:`nmp-nip65` is the first NMP protocol module (per the v1 reference-modules list in `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §11) whose job is *not* to expose product views. It exists primarily as **the canonical source of the mailbox cache** the compiler consults. It contributes one `ViewModule` (for app-side rendering of "this user's relay list") and a small public API the compiler imports directly without going through the FFI surface.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2326:docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:194:- `nmp-core::substrate::{ViewModule, ViewContext, InterestContext, LogicalInterest, ...}` — kernel trait surface.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2336:docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:188:Per `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("the developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does") and ADR-0007's domain-of-diagnostics separation, end-user product UIs do not show "relay X is serving 12 authors." That is operator/debug surface. Normal apps consume the `LogicalInterestStatus` summaries; `RelayCoveragePayload` is for the diagnostics screen (proof iOS app screenshot in `docs/perf/m2/outbox-routing.md` per [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2347:docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:62:The test's assertion that no plaintext nsec crosses FFI is the structural witness for `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 (bounded native state) and for the implicit "secrets stay in Rust" rule — the platform layer never sees the unencrypted key material because every read/write of the key goes through the in-Rust `IdentityModule::sign` function.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2356:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2358:docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52:Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2361:docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:88:1. **Mailbox probe.** For every author with `mailbox_cache.get(author) == None`, the compiler emits a `IndexerProbe { author }` side effect on the plan. The probe registers as its own short-lived `LogicalInterest { shape: { kinds: [10002], authors: [author], limit: 1 }, lifecycle: OneShot, scope: Global }`. Recompilation triggers (§4 trigger A1) re-route the original interest once the kind:10002 lands.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2366:docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:158:The `plan_id` is stored on `CompiledPlan` and rendered into `LogicalInterestStatus` (extending the record at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:147-154` with `plan_id: String, plan_generation: u64`). Tests in §9 assert plan-id stability across no-op recompilations.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2368:docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:167:| `open_author` (requests.rs:118-140) | Registers three `LogicalInterest`s scoped `ActiveAccount` (kind:10002, kind:0, kinds:1+6 for author); calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::ViewOpen)`. Refcount stays — but it lives on `InterestId` now, not on `ViewInterest { key, refcount }`. The `can_send` gate disappears: the compiler always produces a plan; the wire-emitter is the only thing that may queue deferrals. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2369:docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:169:| `open_firehose_tag` (requests.rs:170-200) | Registers one `LogicalInterest { shape: { kinds: [1], tags: { #t: [tag] } }, scope: ActiveAccount, lifecycle: Tailing }`. Routes to active-account read relays per §3.1 table. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2383:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2393:docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2402:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2405:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:19:The summary diagnosis: **the planner is a string formatter, not a compiler.** Every REQ is a per-call-site decision; routing is one of two literals; recompilation is impossible because nothing is compiled. The diagnostics in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:117-154` already type `RelayStatus` / `WireSubscriptionStatus` / `LogicalInterestStatus` per ADR-0007 — but the planner currently emits at most one `LogicalInterestStatus` per view kind because there is no logical-interest object to scope it against.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2411:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:87:`InterestShape` mirrors the Nostr filter shape closely on purpose: most logical interests correspond directly to a single filter, and the kernel ships canonical normalisation (sort, dedup, fold ranges) so equality and hashing are deterministic. The compiler is then free to merge two shapes (or refuse to) on the basis of structural compatibility (§3 step 3).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2415:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:115:The seed-bootstrap path (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:50-106`) becomes one `LogicalInterest` per concern, registered at actor `Start` rather than emitted as raw REQs. The compiler produces the wire artifacts.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2447:docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:215:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2451:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:21:The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2452:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:25:| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2453:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:26:| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2454:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:27:| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2455:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2456:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:29:| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2457:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:30:| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2460:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:56:1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2461:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2466:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2467:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:29:The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2470:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:55:C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2471:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:57:C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2475:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2481:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:42:**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #3 ("Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays") and bug #4 ("DM published to public relays"). Plus the doctrine-10 footgun: a "send everywhere" fallback that publishes a gift wrap to the global content relay because the recipient's inbox lookup returned empty.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2488:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2489:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2490:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2495:docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:186:The M2 `PlannerHarness` (`subscription-compilation/tests.md` §9.3) is deliberately scoped to the planner subsystem: it tests the compile function in isolation, with no actor, no FFI seam, no storage backend. Several framework-magic bullets (C7 publish flow, C11 onboarding, C12 account switch, C13 in-place refinement) require the full actor and the full FFI emission path. The `ContractHarness` is therefore a strict superset.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2524:     1	# ADR 0010: Generated per-app concrete enums at the FFI boundary
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2535:    12	The framework's existing FFI surface (UniFFI on iOS/Android, wasm-bindgen on web) expects **concrete, closed types**. Open enums don't exist in UniFFI. Two paths are possible:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2537:    14	1. **Generated app enum.** Each app's build runs a codegen step that produces concrete per-app `AppAction = KernelAction | Nip01Action | Nip17Action | TwitterAction | ...` enums by composing variants from the kernel, the chosen protocol modules, and the app's own core crate. The FFI exposes the per-app concrete types. Compile-time type safety end-to-end.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2538:    15	2. **Type-erased registry.** Modules register handlers keyed by string namespace; payloads cross FFI as `Vec<u8>` (or `serde_json::Value`). The kernel doesn't know module types statically; it dispatches via the registry. Plug-and-play module loading; loses type safety at the FFI boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2544:    21	**Generated app enum.** Each app's build produces concrete per-app `AppAction`, `AppUpdate`, `ViewSpec`, `Capability*` enums via the `nmp gen modules` codegen step. UniFFI exposes the per-app concrete types via a thin per-app FFI crate (`nmp-app-<name>`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2647:   124	Each module registers itself with the kernel at startup, providing the `dispatch` closure that knows how to deserialize its own action type. The action bytes are produced by the generated FFI layer (which has typed access) and consumed by the module (which has typed access). The kernel in the middle is module-agnostic.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2649:   126	### What UniFFI sees
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2651:   128	UniFFI sees only the generated per-app `nmp-app-twitter` crate. From its perspective, `AppAction` is a normal closed enum. Bindings regenerate deterministically.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2669:   146	- **Cleaner FFI surface.** No `Vec<u8>` blobs across FFI. No JSON parsing on the platform side. No string-keyed switches.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2675:   152	- **Per-app FFI crate adds a build step.** Each app has its own `nmp-app-<name>` crate. Slight build-graph complexity. Mitigation: scaffolded by `nmp init` and `nmp add module`; developers rarely touch it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2682:   159	- **Loses safety where it matters most.** The FFI boundary is exactly where strong typing has the highest payoff. Swift / Kotlin / TypeScript consumers benefit from idiomatic typed enums. Vec<u8> + opaque dispatch reproduces JavaScript-shaped fragility in three statically-typed languages.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2693:   170	- A per-app FFI crate becomes the canonical FFI surface; raw `nmp-ffi` is for the kernel only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2701:   178	| Trait object dispatch over `Box<dyn Action>` | UniFFI doesn't support trait objects across FFI cleanly. Same root issue. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2703:   180	| Procedural macros generating per-module FFI | Tried in head. Inverts the dependency direction — modules would need to know about FFI exposure. The codegen tool generating an aggregating crate is cleaner. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2827:    10	- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2847:    30	- UniFFI bindings (current iOS bridge is raw C FFI).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2860:    43	- **No dedicated FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof gate before the kernel-boundary proof.** The prior M11 implicitly assumed the FFI surface was ready; this rewrite makes it a separate milestone (M10.5).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2863:    46	The plan below is a single ladder of eighteen milestones (M0–M17, with M10.5 inserted as the FFI gate), each producing a runnable artifact, ordered so that each milestone strictly adds capabilities to the prior demoable product.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2904:    39	- Profile placeholders → in-place refinement (D1; already done)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2943:    78	M10.5 FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof                             — design done
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2948:    83	M14   UniFFI migration                                                — pending
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2979:    19	- DM round-trip in `MockRelay` (alice ↔ bob): content matches; no plaintext crosses FFI other than as `ConversationMessage.body`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3402:docs/design/framework-magic.md:35:| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3403:docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3404:docs/design/framework-magic.md:37:| C8 | Subscription planner deduplicates overlapping interests into one wire REQ per relay, auto-closes on EOSE / last-consumer-drop, and buffers ingress to ≤60Hz per view | subs.md | `c8_subscriptions_coalesce_autoclose_and_buffer` | **[PENDING M2]** | spec §7.2; §3.3 bug #2, bug #8 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3405:docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3406:docs/design/framework-magic.md:42:| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3412:docs/design/framework-magic.md:91:- This document does **not** introduce new types or traits — `PublishPlanner`, `ViewModule`, `LogicalInterest`, `SubscriptionCompiler`, `MailboxCache`, `KeyringCapability` are already defined in the cited design docs and product spec. The contract uses those names.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3413:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:13:- `Trigger::ActiveAccountChanged { from, to }` (`subscription-compilation/recompilation.md` §4.2) fires as a consequence of the state change. The planner re-runs `interests()` on every `ViewModule` whose registered interest carries `InterestScope::ActiveAccount` (`subscription-compilation/intro.md` §2.1 line 60 + §2.3); `InterestScope::Account(specific)` and `InterestScope::Global` interests are untouched.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3414:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3426:docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:215:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3430:docs/design/podcast/wiring.md:88:- Acceptance: subscribe to a real RSS feed (Tim Ferriss) → library shows it → tap → see ≥ 5 episodes parsed; UI updates as `FetchFeed` action completes (no spinner gate; placeholder rows during fetch per doctrine D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3446:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:187:  immediately from `debt-inventory.md` §3 D1 audit; no spinner, no
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3448:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:198:### D2. Reactivity contract — composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3453:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:279:| D1 | PASS | S3 + S4 metrics.json + S3/screenshots (placeholder-then-refine path) | <name> | <date> |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3503:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3504:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:26:Every `@Model` final class becomes a `DomainRecord` inside a `DomainModule` in `podcast-core`. The SwiftData `@Relationship(deleteRule:)` semantics are reimplemented as Rust-side cascade in CRUD APIs (see [`podcast-core.md`](podcast-core.md) §B). The Swift `@Model` files are deleted from the new app — Rust types cross FFI via generated ViewModule payloads.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3625:   224	`TimelineItem` is a flat summary. The full event content, raw tags, signature, and provenance live in the event store inside Rust and do not cross FFI. This matches the precedent set by the bible's reference implementation (Pika): chat list is summaries; current chat loads full content on demand.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3641:docs/product-spec/appendices.md:21:**The planner batches at ≤60Hz.** 500 reactions arriving in 100ms become ≤6 batched deltas, not 500 callbacks. Bible commandment 9 (no high-frequency FFI loops) is honored by construction.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3659:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:13:- `Trigger::ActiveAccountChanged { from, to }` (`subscription-compilation/recompilation.md` §4.2) fires as a consequence of the state change. The planner re-runs `interests()` on every `ViewModule` whose registered interest carries `InterestScope::ActiveAccount` (`subscription-compilation/intro.md` §2.1 line 60 + §2.3); `InterestScope::Account(specific)` and `InterestScope::Global` interests are untouched.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3666:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:30:| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3827:    39	- Closed FFI enums that prevent modules from contributing typed views, actions, updates, capabilities, or identity scopes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3829:    41	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3861:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:23:The framework treats common Nostr-correctness failures (stale replaceable events, lost subscriptions, mis-routed publishes, double-publication, multi-account desync, leaked secrets across FFI, naive cache invalidation, withheld cached data, blocking-on-fetch UI patterns) as **product defects in the framework** rather than as developer mistakes. The public API is designed so that the wrong thing is hard to type.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3867:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:182:| `nmp-ffi` | UniFFI building blocks used by generated app crates | UniFFI |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3876:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:55:C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3994:    41	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:4079:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:17:A Cargo workspace shipping a Nostr-native **app kernel** (`nmp-core`), reusable **Nostr protocol modules** (`nmp-nip01`, `nmp-nip17`, `nmp-nip65`, etc.), app-owned extension modules, a codegen tool (`nmp gen modules`) that produces per-app concrete FFI enums/wrappers, FFI bindings for Swift/Kotlin/TypeScript, a wasm target, a scaffolding CLI, and reference platform shells.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:4087:docs/plan.md:5:> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:4093:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:4111:- **P1:** [event-store-query-builders.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada/docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md:98) says “render loading,” and [ndk/gotchas.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada/docs/research/ndk/gotchas.md:120) says the feed UI should wait for relay lists. Both contradict D1: cached/renderable content must render immediately and refine in place. Reword as planner coverage/freshness/backfill behavior, not UI gating/loading.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:4127:- **P1:** [event-store-query-builders.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada/docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md:98) says “render loading,” and [ndk/gotchas.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada/docs/research/ndk/gotchas.md:120) says the feed UI should wait for relay lists. Both contradict D1: cached/renderable content must render immediately and refine in place. Reword as planner coverage/freshness/backfill behavior, not UI gating/loading.
docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:242:17 `ViewModule`s. Each in `podcast-core/src/views/<name>.rs`. All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).
docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:417:`nmp gen modules` produces `apps/podcast/nmp-app-podcast/` with the generated `AppAction`, `AppUpdate`, `ViewSpec`, capability trait composites, and UniFFI Swift bindings. Per ADR-0010 the Swift app sees:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:103:Emitted by the view registry when a `ViewModule::open` returns a non-empty `Vec<LogicalInterest>` (per [intro.md](intro.md) §2.2). May fire in batches when a screen opens many rows at once.
docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:122:All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/a4ef834.md:46:to M14 per T22". Doctrine review sections updated from D0-D5 to D0-D8.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/a4ef834.md:67:- `docs/plan.md`: D2/D3/D5 wording updated to canonical D0-D8 names.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/a4ef834.md:87:- D0 violation fully resolved; production `nmp-core` crate exposes no actor internals.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/a4ef834.md:90:- Doctrine docs consistent with D0-D8 canonical naming across all touchpoints.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/6782b97.md:11:- **PASS.** Both wording issues are resolved at `docs/research/ndk/meta-subscribe.md:137` (NaddrCoord shape + correct address-merge cap) and `docs/research/ndk/meta-subscribe.md:150` (D1 placeholder split into e-tag and a-tag cases, no loading-style language).
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:15:**Modeled budget contract** — the harness's deterministic replay mode runs synthetic workloads through a *model* of the runtime: simulated relay sockets, simulated storage, simulated UniFFI marshaling, simulated NSE. Passing the gates here proves the budgets are internally consistent and the harness scaffolding works. It does **not** prove the real runtime hits those budgets.
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:17:**Runtime evidence** — live mode (or replay mode where modeled segments have been swapped for real adapters) runs against actual LMDB, actual WebSockets, actual UniFFI, actual NSE. Passing here is real evidence the architecture meets its budgets.
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:25:`reactivity-bench` proved the algorithmic core scales: reverse-index lookup, view recompute, delta emission. It is synthetic — single Rust process, no relays, no disk, no real-world event distribution, no FFI.
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:33:- **FFI marshaling under steady-state pressure:** are we actually staying ≤ 60Hz with real event arrival patterns?

docs/design/firehose-bench.md:126:- ViewBatch FFI emission rate.
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:141:### 3.3 Profile thrashing
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:151:- Net FFI dispatch rate.
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:188:- FFI dispatch rate.
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:274:| FFI marshaling under realistic patterns | — | ✓ |
docs/design/ffi-hardening/ci-tiers.md:1:# FFI hardening — CI tiers and M10.5 close protocol (§9)
docs/design/ffi-hardening/ci-tiers.md:23:name: FFI stress (pre-merge)
docs/design/ffi-hardening/ci-tiers.md:66:name: FFI stress (nightly)
docs/design/ffi-hardening/ci-tiers.md:112:The Rust harness supports `--capture-trace` (records all FFI
docs/design/ffi-hardening/ci-tiers.md:134:  doctrine sign-off evidence for M10.5 (see gates.md §D1 note).
docs/design/ffi-hardening/ci-tiers.md:159:4. Doctrine review (D0–D5) signed off in `doctrine-review.md`.
docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:164:`podcast-core` owns the action **shape**; the LLM call is delegated to `podcast-llm` via a private `ctx.dispatch_internal(SummarizePrompt { episode_id, style })` (one actor message tick — no FFI boundary crossed). This avoids `podcast-core` depending on `podcast-llm` while keeping the action namespaced under `podcast.*` from the Swift app's POV.
docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:271:Reads happen at action boot only. Per RMP bible commandment #2, keys never cross FFI as part of dispatch — they live on the platform side; the bridge resolves and injects them when executing the capability.
docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:280:- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
docs/design/podcast/risks.md:51:- **Why**: `AVAudioSession` interactions with the kernel's actor crossing FFI on every `Tick` event can introduce jitter. The reference Swift app handles this in-process; we cross FFI ~4×/s plus on every state change. On a battery-throttled iPhone 12, this is non-zero.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c53ed1e.md:3:**Commit:** `c53ed1e docs(framework-magic): adopt D0-D8 canonical doctrine set (PD-001 resolution)`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c53ed1e.md:11:T19 reconciliation pass: framework-magic docs aligned with canonical D0-D8 doctrine set. Reviewed:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c53ed1e.md:17:- D6/D7/D8 doctrine numbers adopted throughout
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c53ed1e.md:23:1. **README.md:23, :95, :108** — stale D0-D5 wording; T19 still described as "in flight." Update to D0-D8 and mark reconciliation complete. *(Fixed in follow-up commit 76769d9)*
docs/perf/codex-reviews/c53ed1e.md:25:2. **docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:62** — wrong D-number mapping. "No plaintext nsec crosses FFI" is not D6 (D6 is errors-never-FFI-as-exceptions). Keep as key-security invariant; tie signer failures to D6, not the nsec boundary. *(Fixed in follow-up commit 175632b)*

docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:4:> Read first: [intro.md](intro.md) for the `LogicalInterest` shape this stage consumes.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:12:   Vec<LogicalInterest>                       │
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:29:   │ Stage 3: Per-relay shape merge                    │  (§3.3)
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52:Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:81:| Both populated | Outbox primarily; Inbox interests split (see §3.3) | both |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:92:1. **Mailbox probe.** For every author with `mailbox_cache.get(author) == None`, the compiler emits a `IndexerProbe { author }` side effect on the plan. The probe registers as its own short-lived `LogicalInterest { shape: { kinds: [10002], authors: [author], limit: 1 }, lifecycle: OneShot, scope: Global }`. Recompilation triggers (§4 trigger A1) re-route the original interest once the kind:10002 lands.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:97:## 3.3 Stage 3 — Per-relay shape merge
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:103:Two `InterestShape`s `A` and `B` are **mergeable on relay R** iff:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:112:8. **Rule 8 (address-pointer union).** `A.addresses` and `B.addresses` merge by `A.addresses ∪ B.addresses`, provided their other constraints (`authors`, `kinds`, `tags`, time, lifecycle) merge per Rules 1–7. Overlapping coordinates with differing time or lifecycle constraints do **not** merge — the compiler emits per-address sub-shapes so each `NaddrCoord`'s routing is independent. The union cap is the relay's per-filter `#a` value limit (default 1000). Address routing uses `NaddrCoord::pubkey` as the `AuthorRouting` input (Outbox direction, Stage 1), so the relays chosen are the addressed author's write relays — the same path as an `authors`-bearing filter for that pubkey.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:137:    pub shape: InterestShape,                  // canonical, post-merge
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:176:  property is critical for D8 (reactivity contract — §1.5): recompilation work must be bounded
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:182:The `plan_id` is stored on `CompiledPlan` and rendered into `LogicalInterestStatus` (extending the record at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:147-154` with `plan_id: String, plan_generation: u64`). Tests in §9 assert plan-id stability across no-op recompilations.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:190:| `startup_requests` (requests.rs:50-106) | **Relocated outside `nmp-core` per D0.** The seed timeline / kind:0 / kind:10002 / kind:3 bootstrap is social graph knowledge — not kernel substrate. It moves to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / the demo app's startup sequence. `nmp-core` only executes interests registered by modules; it does not hard-code which interests those are. The TEST_PUBKEY-specific bootstrap (line 71-82) moves to the demo app (or a test fixture in `nmp-testing`). The kernel's `ActorStart` handler calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::Startup)`, which is a no-op until a module registers interests. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:191:| `open_author` (requests.rs:118-140) | Registers three `LogicalInterest`s scoped `ActiveAccount` (kind:10002, kind:0, kinds:1+6 for author); calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::ViewOpen)`. Refcount stays — but it lives on `InterestId` now, not on `ViewInterest { key, refcount }`. The `can_send` gate disappears: the compiler always produces a plan; the wire-emitter is the only thing that may queue deferrals. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:193:| `open_firehose_tag` (requests.rs:170-200) | Registers one `LogicalInterest { shape: { kinds: [1], tags: { #t: [tag] } }, scope: ActiveAccount, lifecycle: Tailing }`. Routes to active-account read relays per §3.1 table. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:194:| `claim_profile` / `release_profile` (requests.rs:202-263) | Registers/unregisters one `LogicalInterest { shape: { authors: [pk], kinds: [0], limit: 1 }, lifecycle: OneShot }` per claim. Refcount of distinct consumers becomes the `InterestId` claim set inside the registry. **Dedup of (pk, kinds=[0]) across N timeline rows yields one merged SubShape and one wire REQ** — this is what bug-extinction "1000 avatars do not produce 1000 REQs" verifies. The refcount must be an integer counter, not a boolean presence flag — Applesauce gotcha `75ef7d5f` (`docs/research/applesauce/gotchas.md` §G2) shows the boolean model causes leaks when two consumers share a claim and one unsubscribes. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:208:| `maybe_open_timeline` (ingest.rs:329-365) | The "seed-contacts arrive → open union timeline" logic moves to `nmp-nip02` (follows/contacts module), not to an `nmp-core` helper. D0: `nmp-core` must not know the social graph. The contacts module watches the kind:3 projection and registers a `Timeline { authors: union }` interest on behalf of whichever view asked for it. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:217:  into `nmp-core` — those are module concerns (D0). The impl PR for M2 must register those
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:15:| Trait | Purpose | Owns | Generated FFI artifact |
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:74:**Generated FFI:** none directly; domain records cross FFI only via `ViewModule` payloads. Apps don't `dispatch(SaveDraft(...))` — they dispatch an action that writes a domain record as a side effect.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:166:- `ViewBatch` emission across FFI.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:173:- Pre-formatted display fields per doctrine D1.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:176:**Cross-FFI:** the module's Spec, Payload, and Delta types are surfaced in the per-app generated enums (ADR-0010). UniFFI bindings expose them as native types on each platform.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:303:The generated FFI exposes one Swift protocol / Kotlin interface per `CapabilityModule`, with methods matching the request shape and a reverse-callback mechanism for results.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:382:Cross-crate enum composition is done by the codegen tool, not by macros, to keep the build graph linear and avoid macro recursion issues with UniFFI.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:400:- `ViewModule` shows up in `LogicalInterestStatus::Module { namespace, key }`.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:427:`TestHarness` mocks the actor, the storage backend, and the FFI emission. Modules can be tested without a real relay, without LMDB on disk, without UniFFI bindings.
docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:111:### 9.2 Relay Metadata Is Durable Domain State
docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:139:This should be actor-derived diagnostic state, not raw socket callbacks crossing FFI.
