docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:17:NMP doctrine canonical D0-D8 (docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md §1.5):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:18:- D0-D5 = policy doctrines; D6-D8 = substrate invariants.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:19:- D2 Negentropy first, D3 Outbox automatic, D6 Errors never cross FFI, D8 Reactivity contract.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:58:+Per doctrine D6: "errors never cross FFI as exceptions." The corollary is that the *store itself* must never silently produce an incorrect answer because of partial writes. Any scenario where two separate `lmdb::Environment` handles could each commit independently would violate this — we would have no way to roll back one side when the other fails.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:60:+**Why this matters (D6):** if the store returns wrong data because of a partial write, that wrong data eventually reaches FFI as an `AppUpdate`. There is no way to signal "this snapshot is partially corrupt" through a `Result<T, StoreError>` once the data is already in the `AppUpdate` struct. The only safe design is to prevent partial writes at the store layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:206:+    // Ceilings (enforced on every claim() call — D8 / ADR-0001..0004).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:283:+- `max_pinned_total`: 20 000 events globally. Prevents many moderate-sized views from collectively overwhelming the working set (D8 / ADR-0003 gate).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:478:+### 2.12a Migration atomicity / crash-recovery (watermarks.md §4.2)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:515: ### 2.13 Domain isolation (`kernel-substrate.md` §8)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:750:+    /// Rationale: D8 (reactivity contract) requires that the kernel's working-set
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:783:-## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:784:+## 4. Error semantics (doctrine D6)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:790:-- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:795:+- `OverPinned` → the caller (actor) surfaces this as `Effect::ViewOverPinned { claimer }` and then calls `release(claimer)` to drop the offending claim, keeping the working set bounded per D8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:797:-No `StoreError` ever crosses FFI. The `AppUpdate` carries only successful state + optional `toast: Option<String>`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:798:+No `StoreError` ever crosses FFI (D6). The `AppUpdate` carries only successful state + optional `toast: Option<String>`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:985:+    /// Maps to D8 (reactivity contract): a working-set overflow must be surfaced
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:992:+No `StoreError` ever crosses FFI (D6). The actor maps every variant to a typed `Effect` or a graceful degraded state. See [`../trait.md`](../trait.md) §5 for the full mapping.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1018:1. Are the 12 original findings actually addressed? Spot-check vs the original codex flag list: atomicity ADR (#1), D2-via-D8 trait register_view_cover (#2), GC pinned ceiling (#3), kind:5 a-tag tombstones (#4), tombstone optional kind5_id (#5), secondary key encoding consistency (#6), Coverage + scan_by_kind_dtag in trait (#7), empty-kinds semantics (#8), list_watermarks_for_relay secondary index (#9), migration atomicity (#10), single-insert-path test as M3 gate (#11), trait.md size split (#12).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1020:3. Doctrine references: does the M3 design correctly cite D0-D8 (not D0-D5)?
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1046:f765f60 (HEAD -> master, origin/master) docs(m3): fix codex feedback (atomicity ADR, D2 trait, GC ceilings, tombstone semantics, key consistency)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1076:/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "D0|D1|D2|D3|D4|D5|D6|D7|D8|doctrine|Doctrine|policy doctrines|substrate invariants" docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md docs/design/lmdb-schema.md docs/design/lmdb/gc.md docs/design/lmdb/keys.md docs/design/lmdb/tests.md docs/design/lmdb/trait.md docs/design/lmdb/trait/types.md docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md' in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1078:docs/design/lmdb/trait/types.md:175:    /// Maps to D8 (reactivity contract): a working-set overflow must be surfaced
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1079:docs/design/lmdb/trait/types.md:182:No `StoreError` ever crosses FFI (D6). The actor maps every variant to a typed `Effect` or a graceful degraded state. See [`../trait.md`](../trait.md) §5 for the full mapping.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1080:docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:17:Per doctrine D6: "errors never cross FFI as exceptions." The corollary is that the *store itself* must never silently produce an incorrect answer because of partial writes. Any scenario where two separate `lmdb::Environment` handles could each commit independently would violate this — we would have no way to roll back one side when the other fails.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1081:docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:19:**Why this matters (D6):** if the store returns wrong data because of a partial write, that wrong data eventually reaches FFI as an `AppUpdate`. There is no way to signal "this snapshot is partially corrupt" through a `Result<T, StoreError>` once the data is already in the `AppUpdate` struct. The only safe design is to prevent partial writes at the store layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1082:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:138:    /// Rationale: D8 (reactivity contract) requires that the kernel's working-set
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1083:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:201:## 4. Error semantics (doctrine D6)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1084:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:211:- `OverPinned` → the caller (actor) surfaces this as `Effect::ViewOverPinned { claimer }` and then calls `release(claimer)` to drop the offending claim, keeping the working set bounded per D8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1085:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:213:No `StoreError` ever crosses FFI (D6). The `AppUpdate` carries only successful state + optional `toast: Option<String>`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1086:docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:40:    // Ceilings (enforced on every claim() call — D8 / ADR-0001..0004).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1087:docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:132:- `max_pinned_total`: 20 000 events globally. Prevents many moderate-sized views from collectively overwhelming the working set (D8 / ADR-0003 gate).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1088:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1090:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1091:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:33:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1092:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:43:### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1094:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:56:### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1095:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:62:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1096:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:75:### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1098:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:79:### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1099:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:83:### D6. Errors never cross FFI as exceptions
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1100:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:95:### D7. Capabilities report; never decide policy
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1101:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:107:### D8. Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1102:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:109:The substrate that delivers D1, D2, D4, D5 in practice. Every inserted event participates in a composite reverse index that names the views interested in it; view recompute is bounded to ≤60 deltas per second per view; the working set is hot-resident with claim-pinned overlays. Allocations after warmup are linear in active-view count, never in cached-event count. False-wakeup rate ≤ 0.10 candidates per delta.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1125:docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md:188:- `LogicalInterestStatus.cache_coverage` resolves from `coverage()` (§1.1 above) for the relevant `(filter_hash, relay_url)` pair.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1145:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:211:- `OverPinned` → the caller (actor) surfaces this as `Effect::ViewOverPinned { claimer }` and then calls `release(claimer)` to drop the offending claim, keeping the working set bounded per D8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1255:    74	### 2.1 Mutation hot path
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1263:    82	The M2 subscription planner consults `provenance_for(id)` to learn which relays have already delivered an event when scoring per-relay coverage in `LogicalInterestStatus::relay_urls`. This avoids re-fetching the same event from relays we already know carry it. The provenance sidecar is also part of the ADR-0007 diagnostics bridge — the diagnostics screen shows per-event source counts in the firehose tap view.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1369:   188	- `LogicalInterestStatus.cache_coverage` resolves from `coverage()` (§1.1 above) for the relevant `(filter_hash, relay_url)` pair.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1688:    75	**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:1850:   237	The M3 implementation deletes none of the existing files outright — the kernel's `events: HashMap` and `profiles: HashMap` are replaced by calls to `Box<dyn EventStore>` held inside the `Kernel` struct, and the per-field tests (`kernel/tests.rs`) shift to the new trait via `MemEventStore`. No public FFI surface changes.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:2821:    17	Per doctrine D6: "errors never cross FFI as exceptions." The corollary is that the *store itself* must never silently produce an incorrect answer because of partial writes. Any scenario where two separate `lmdb::Environment` handles could each commit independently would violate this — we would have no way to roll back one side when the other fails.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:2823:    19	**Why this matters (D6):** if the store returns wrong data because of a partial write, that wrong data eventually reaches FFI as an `AppUpdate`. There is no way to signal "this snapshot is partially corrupt" through a `Result<T, StoreError>` once the data is already in the `AppUpdate` struct. The only safe design is to prevent partial writes at the store layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:2903:docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md:188:- `LogicalInterestStatus.cache_coverage` resolves from `coverage()` (§1.1 above) for the relevant `(filter_hash, relay_url)` pair.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:2919:docs/design/lmdb/trait/types.md:182:No `StoreError` ever crosses FFI (D6). The actor maps every variant to a typed `Effect` or a graceful degraded state. See [`../trait.md`](../trait.md) §5 for the full mapping.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:16938:/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"Coverage|coverage\\(|list_watermarks_for_relay|idx_watermark_relay|register_view_cover|claim\\(|OverPinned|scan_by_kind_dtag|scan_by_author_kind|scan_by_kind_time|kind5_event_id|tombstones_addr|created_at_desc_be|write_meta_schema_version|run_migration_step|WriteCounting|only_insert_writes_primary|D3|D6|D8|open question 1|DomainSchemaTooNew|MigrationFailed\" docs/design/lmdb-schema.md docs/design/lmdb/gc.md docs/design/lmdb/keys.md docs/design/lmdb/tests.md docs/design/lmdb/trait.md docs/design/lmdb/trait/types.md docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:16945:docs/design/lmdb/trait/types.md:175:    /// Maps to D8 (reactivity contract): a working-set overflow must be surfaced
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:16947:docs/design/lmdb/trait/types.md:182:No `StoreError` ever crosses FFI (D6). The actor maps every variant to a typed `Effect` or a graceful degraded state. See [`../trait.md`](../trait.md) §5 for the full mapping.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:16956:docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md:188:- `LogicalInterestStatus.cache_coverage` resolves from `coverage()` (§1.1 above) for the relevant `(filter_hash, relay_url)` pair.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:16959:docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:17:Per doctrine D6: "errors never cross FFI as exceptions." The corollary is that the *store itself* must never silently produce an incorrect answer because of partial writes. Any scenario where two separate `lmdb::Environment` handles could each commit independently would violate this — we would have no way to roll back one side when the other fails.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:16960:docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:19:**Why this matters (D6):** if the store returns wrong data because of a partial write, that wrong data eventually reaches FFI as an `AppUpdate`. There is no way to signal "this snapshot is partially corrupt" through a `Result<T, StoreError>` once the data is already in the `AppUpdate` struct. The only safe design is to prevent partial writes at the store layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:16971:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:138:    /// Rationale: D8 (reactivity contract) requires that the kernel's working-set
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:16977:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:201:## 4. Error semantics (doctrine D6)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:16980:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:211:- `OverPinned` → the caller (actor) surfaces this as `Effect::ViewOverPinned { claimer }` and then calls `release(claimer)` to drop the offending claim, keeping the working set bounded per D8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:16981:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:213:No `StoreError` ever crosses FFI (D6). The `AppUpdate` carries only successful state + optional `toast: Option<String>`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:16983:docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:40:    // Ceilings (enforced on every claim() call — D8 / ADR-0001..0004).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:16994:docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:132:- `max_pinned_total`: 20 000 events globally. Prevents many moderate-sized views from collectively overwhelming the working set (D8 / ADR-0003 gate).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:17028:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:17046:f765f60 docs(m3): fix codex feedback (atomicity ADR, D2 trait, GC ceilings, tombstone semantics, key consistency)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:17058:15:You are reviewing merge 9fead0e (M3 LMDB schema + EventStore trait + GC policy design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5. File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:17095:285:+The M3 implementation deletes none of the existing files outright — the kernel's `events: HashMap` and `profiles: HashMap` are replaced by calls to `Box<dyn EventStore>` held inside the `Kernel` struct, and the per-field tests (`kernel/tests.rs`) shift to the new trait via `MemEventStore`. No public FFI surface changes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:17112:839:+### 2.16 Restart preserves replaceable semantics (`plan.md` §M3 exit gate)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:17146:1419:docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:396:The FFI and iOS bridge code is **clean of blocking debt markers** (TODO/FIXME/unimplemented/panic). All 20 code inspection findings are either justified by design or actionable as documentation cleanup. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:17147:1440:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:17148:1451:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1567:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:19:The summary diagnosis: **the planner is a string formatter, not a compiler.** Every REQ is a per-call-site decision; routing is one of two literals; recompilation is impossible because nothing is compiled. The diagnostics in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:117-154` already type `RelayStatus` / `WireSubscriptionStatus` / `LogicalInterestStatus` per ADR-0007 — but the planner currently emits at most one `LogicalInterestStatus` per view kind because there is no logical-interest object to scope it against.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:89:pub struct InterestShape {

docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:117:`InterestShape` mirrors the Nostr filter shape closely on purpose: most logical interests correspond directly to a single filter, and the kernel ships canonical normalisation (sort, dedup, fold ranges) so equality and hashing are deterministic. The compiler is then free to merge two shapes (or refuse to) on the basis of structural compatibility (§3 step 3).

docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:163:The seed-bootstrap path (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:50-106`) becomes one `LogicalInterest` per concern, registered at actor `Start` rather than emitted as raw REQs. The compiler produces the wire artifacts.

docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:17:- The signer catalog at `subsystems.md` §7.4 lines 127–135 names both kinds as supported in `nmp-core` (no FFI signer extensibility — apps don't implement signers).
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:34:   e. Assert no plaintext bunker secret crossed FFI (the test's reconciler audit log shows no `Account` snapshot field carrying the raw URL); only the typed `Account` + `signer_kind` enum.
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:60:This bullet is a load-bearing demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D7** ("capabilities report; never decide policy" — `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D7). The KeyringCapability **reports** (here is the stored bytes; persistence succeeded/failed). It does **not decide** (whether to retry, whether to fall back to a different storage backend, whether to surface a UI prompt). The framework decides; the capability executes.
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:62:The test's assertion that no plaintext nsec crosses FFI is a key-security invariant: the platform layer never sees unencrypted key material because every signing operation goes through the in-Rust `IdentityModule::sign` function. Signer failures surface as `toast: Option<String>` state (per **D6**, "errors never cross FFI as exceptions"; `overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D6) rather than as exceptions or `Result<T, E>` across the FFI boundary.
docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:21:| Pure-Rust HNSW (`hnsw_rs`, `instant-distance`) | No C extension; pure Rust; no FFI surface to worry about. | We give up on-disk persistence (memory-only) or roll our own (significant complexity). Recall-vs-build-time trade tuning becomes ours. | Considered as the fallback if sqlite-vec iOS spike fails. |
docs/design/podcast/copy.md:145:### 3.3 After Step 7 (wired)
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:12:Applesauce action can pass any relay list. NMP's structural guarantee is stronger — D3
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:78:    /// Does NOT fall back to indexers (D3) — publishes must reach only declared write relays.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:107:    // intentionally no `indexer_set` field: publish NEVER falls back to indexers (D3).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:109:    // indexer the author has not declared as a write relay violates D3 (outbox routing
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:184:- **Step 2's `resolve_author_outbox_no_indexer`** is the structural enforcement of D3 and
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:283:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:287:When an event references a `NaddrCoord` (via an `#a` tag, e.g. a NIP-22 kind:1111 comment on a NIP-23 article), the publish routing follows `PublicWithNotifications`:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:289:- The **author's own write relays** receive the event (standard D3 outbox rule).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:292:The publish planner does **not** require a new routing variant for address pointers — the `#p` inbox lane already covers the notification path. What M2 adds is the *subscription-side* routing: `InterestShape::addresses` causes Stage 1 to resolve each `NaddrCoord::pubkey` as an Outbox direction (the addressed author's write relays), routing the REQ to where the article lives. The publish fan-out is unchanged.

docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md:75:### 2.1 Mutation hot path
docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md:83:The M2 subscription planner consults `provenance_for(id)` to learn which relays have already delivered an event when scoring per-relay coverage in `LogicalInterestStatus::relay_urls`. This avoids re-fetching the same event from relays we already know carry it. The provenance sidecar is also part of the ADR-0007 diagnostics bridge — the diagnostics screen shows per-event source counts in the firehose tap view.
docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md:189:- `LogicalInterestStatus.cache_coverage` resolves from `coverage()` (§1.1 above) for the relevant `(filter_hash, relay_url)` pair.
docs/design/ffi-hardening/harness.md:1:# FFI hardening — harness architecture (§4)
docs/design/ffi-hardening/harness.md:4:FFI symbols directly (fastest iteration). The XCUITest target exercises
docs/design/ffi-hardening/harness.md:15:caller thread ──────► │ nmp_app_*() FFI entry    │
docs/design/ffi-hardening/harness.md:96:  --capture-trace      record FFI call log + emit log for replay
docs/design/ffi-hardening/harness.md:216:### 2.1 Layout
docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:15:**Framework does:** the subscription-compilation pipeline (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md`) for dedup and coalesce; the wire-emitter's diff (compiler §3 final stage) for auto-close on plan changes; the view registry's refcount drop for auto-close on consumer loss; `docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md` for the per-tick batching; the FullState backpressure fallback at `subsystems.md` §7.2 line 69. The ≤60Hz cap is D8 (reactivity contract per `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D8; budget validated by ADR-0002 in `docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md` §7.2).
docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:19:**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of subscription-management bugs in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 numbers 2 ("Subscription leaked after its UI is destroyed") and 8 ("Two concurrent UI subscriptions for the same filter producing two relay REQs"). Plus the hand-rolled grouping-window + dedup-LRU pattern that `ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7 calls out as the work clients typically do manually.
docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. D5 (snapshots bounded by what's open) covers the view-scoped auto-close property; D8 (reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded) covers the ≤60Hz emit cap, per `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D8. Different milestone responsibility from C6/C7.
docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:38:- `docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md` §9.2 assertion 2 already asserts the per-relay author partition + sub-shape merge (the coalesce property at the planner layer). The framework-magic version of sub-path 2 reuses that mailbox cache setup but reads the wire output through the platform shadow's audit log instead of through the planner harness.
docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:48:**Applesauce cross-validation** (`docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md`): the logical-vs-wire split maps as follows. Applesauce's logical layer is `EventModels.model(Constructor, ...args)` at `event-models.ts:50-86`, which returns one shared `Observable` per `(constructor, key)` hash regardless of how many callers subscribe — equivalent to NMP's `LogicalInterest`. The wire layer is the `EventStore.insert$` / `remove$` subjects at `event-store.ts:93-99`, which all pipelines read from. NMP's `LogicalInterest` (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md` §2.1) covers the same surface: multiple view consumers sharing one compiled wire REQ, each filtered locally. No observable property is lost; the primary architectural difference is Applesauce uses RxJS `share()` while NMP uses an actor-owned compiler that emits wire frames.
docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:40:    // Ceilings (enforced on every claim() call — D8 / ADR-0001..0004).
docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:139:- `max_pinned_total`: 20 000 events globally. Prevents many moderate-sized views from collectively overwhelming the working set (D8 / ADR-0003 gate).
docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:165:    // 3.3 — Purge old tombstones whose target event is absent.
docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:181:`gc_step()` is **never** invoked from an FFI call path — it runs on the actor's own schedule so any latency it introduces is invisible to the platform.
docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md:81:                       │ ViewBatch / FullState via FFI
docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md:104:- **Reads flow up; updates flow down.** Component reads happen entirely in Tier 3 — no FFI on the read path. Updates from relays land in Tier 1, propagate to Tier 2 working set + projections, then to Tier 3 via `ViewBatch`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:108:    51	- **Why**: `AVAudioSession` interactions with the kernel's actor crossing FFI on every `Tick` event can introduce jitter. The reference Swift app handles this in-process; we cross FFI ~4×/s plus on every state change. On a battery-throttled iPhone 12, this is non-zero.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:216:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:414:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:603:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:648:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:218:./docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:789:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:359:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1488:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5869:+docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:808:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:378:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:1152:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:722:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1388:Review: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 + D2 — composite reverse index + working-set bound must remain enforced through the trait); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance; (4) consistency of EventStore trait surface across the 6 sub-y-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3968:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3973:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:282:## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3974:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:288:- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3989:3f5a2b3 docs(readme): align D0-D5 with canonical product-spec; log doctrine collision (PD-001)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4003:     3	> A Rust multiplatform framework for building Nostr apps. One Rust core + thin platform shells (iOS / Android / desktop / web). Goal: make it nearly impossible to ship a broken Nostr app — invariants enforced by the type system, actor ownership, and FFI surface rather than docs.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4017:    17	- **M10.5 (FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof):** design landed; **hard gate before M11**.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4020:    20	- **M13 (Web-of-Trust), M14 (UniFFI migration), M15 (Android+Desktop+Web), M16 (CLI+starter+recipes), M17 (v1 release):** pending.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4025:    25	- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Canonical wording from [`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5](docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected. Conflicts between doctrines resolve in the order listed.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4026:    26	  - **D0** Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core` (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4027:    27	  - **D1** Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place. Placeholders are part of the type contract; the API has no "spinner gating cached content" pattern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4028:    28	  - **D2** Negentropy first, REQ second. NIP-77 reconciliation with durable watermarks is the default backfill; live REQ is the tailing path. (See M4.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4029:    29	  - **D3** Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out. Reads route per author write relays; publishes route to author write relays + tagged recipients' inbox relays. (See M2.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4030:    30	  - **D4** Single writer per fact; caches derive. Five cache layers exist; one writer per fact; cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4031:    31	  - **D5** Snapshots bounded by what's open. `AppState` carries the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4033:    33	  Three additional load-bearing rules are not D-numbered but are equally binding (RMP-bible invariants from [`docs/aim.md` §2](docs/aim.md)): **errors never cross FFI** (operational failures become `toast: Option<String>` state fields), **capabilities report; never decide policy** (native bridges execute and report; Rust decides retry/recovery), and the **reactivity contract** (composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded; ADR-0001..0004).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4035:    35	- **App-extension kernel boundary** (ADR-0009). NMP is a kernel + five extension trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`). Per-app concrete enums generated at the FFI boundary via `nmp gen modules` (ADR-0010). Apps assemble themselves from `nmp-core` + protocol modules + their own `<app>-core` crate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4040:    40	- **Empirical iOS proof before podcast rebuild (M10.5 gate).** No "we'll wire it up later" — the FFI surface ships rock-solid (stress harness + simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI fleet + Instruments leaks audit + iPhone-12 baseline) before any line of M11 code is written.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4052:    52	   │  Generated per-app FFI crate (nmp-app-<name>)         │  UniFFI / wasm-bindgen
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4069:    69	   │ 10│      │ 42  │    │…       │    │  podcast,│   FFI crate, never into
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4074:    74	**Single source of truth, four delivery paths.** The kernel is compiled as `cdylib + staticlib + rlib`. Desktop and CLI consumers link the rlib directly (no FFI). iOS links the staticlib via xcframework. Android links the cdylib via cargo-ndk. Web compiles to wasm32-unknown-unknown via the wasm crate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4090:    90	| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D5 lives in §1.5. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4092:    92	| `docs/design/` | Per-subsystem design docs — subscription compilation, LMDB schema, FFI hardening, podcast rebuild, framework-magic, NIP-29 crate. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4095:    95	| `crates/` | `nmp-core` (substrate), `nmp-codegen` (per-app FFI crate generator), `nmp-testing` (mock relay, harnesses, scenarios), `fixture-todo-core` (non-Nostr extension-module proof). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4097:    97	| `ios/NmpStress` | iOS SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI (will migrate to UniFFI in M14). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4103:   103	2. `docs/product-spec.md` §1.5 — the doctrine D0–D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4106:   106	5. `docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md` — how FFI types are generated.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4256:     5	> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4260:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4295:    44	| [M10.5](plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md) | FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof | Hard gate | |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4300:    49	| [M14](plan/m14-uniffi.md) | UniFFI migration | Arc 3 — Release | |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4333:    17	A Cargo workspace shipping a Nostr-native **app kernel** (`nmp-core`), reusable **Nostr protocol modules** (`nmp-nip01`, `nmp-nip17`, `nmp-nip65`, etc.), app-owned extension modules, a codegen tool (`nmp gen modules`) that produces per-app concrete FFI enums/wrappers, FFI bindings for Swift/Kotlin/TypeScript, a wasm target, a scaffolding CLI, and reference platform shells.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4339:    23	The framework treats common Nostr-correctness failures (stale replaceable events, lost subscriptions, mis-routed publishes, double-publication, multi-account desync, leaked secrets across FFI, naive cache invalidation, withheld cached data, blocking-on-fetch UI patterns) as **product defects in the framework** rather than as developer mistakes. The public API is designed so that the wrong thing is hard to type.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4347:    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4355:    39	- Closed FFI enums that prevent modules from contributing typed views, actions, updates, capabilities, or identity scopes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4357:    41	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4370:    54	### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4376:    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4389:    73	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4393:    77	### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4395:    79	What crosses FFI is the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store. `AppState` carries small screen-shaped data plus a map of `ViewId → ViewPayload` for views currently in use. Closing a view evicts its payload from the snapshot. The event store itself never crosses FFI. See §6.2 and the FFI architecture appendix (§A1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4474:The main discrepancy is now clear: 810d0f8’s README used the older “RMP invariant” labels as D2/D3/D5, while the product spec’s canonical §1.5 uses negentropy/outbox/snapshots. I’m doing one more pass over status and path claims so the final review stays focused.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4489:    10	- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4509:    30	- UniFFI bindings (current iOS bridge is raw C FFI).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4522:    43	- **No dedicated FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof gate before the kernel-boundary proof.** The prior M11 implicitly assumed the FFI surface was ready; this rewrite makes it a separate milestone (M10.5).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4525:    46	The plan below is a single ladder of eighteen milestones (M0–M17, with M10.5 inserted as the FFI gate), each producing a runnable artifact, ordered so that each milestone strictly adds capabilities to the prior demoable product.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4566:    39	- Profile placeholders → in-place refinement (D1; already done)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4605:    78	M10.5 FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof                             — design done
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4610:    83	M14   UniFFI migration                                                — pending

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4782:docs/plan/principles.md:9:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the [M11](m11-podcast.md) podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4790:docs/plan/status.md:43:- **No dedicated FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof gate before the kernel-boundary proof.** The prior M11 implicitly assumed the FFI surface was ready; this rewrite makes it a separate milestone (M10.5).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4792:docs/plan/status.md:46:The plan below is a single ladder of eighteen milestones (M0–M17, with M10.5 inserted as the FFI gate), each producing a runnable artifact, ordered so that each milestone strictly adds capabilities to the prior demoable product.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4804:docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:154:- Android / Desktop / Web targets — post-M14 (UniFFI migration) and M15 (cross-platform).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4806:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:1:# Design: FFI hardening stress harness (M10.5)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4809:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:8:This is the design spec for the M10.5 dedicated FFI-surface hardening pass. The
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4817:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:176:├── doctrine-review.md       # D0–D5 sign-off (M10.5 exit-gate artifact)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4822:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:237:The M14 UniFFI migration moves the surface to typed `Result`-shaped
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4824:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:269:   typed `Result` via UniFFI in M14. Recommend: keep it scalar for
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4831:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:293:   M14 UniFFI migration replaces raw pointers with typed handles, eliminating
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4835:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:1:# M10.5 — FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof *(hard gate before M11 starts)*
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4837:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:5:**Demo product:** The iOS Twitter slice from [M1](m1-twitter-slice.md)–[M10](m10-blossom.md) subjected to a published, exhaustive stress harness on the iOS simulator and a real iPhone 12. The kernel↔FFI↔SwiftUI path is proven, in measured numbers, to be **rock-solid and demonstrably performant** before a single line of the podcast app is written.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4838:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:7:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4841:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:23:- **All M1–M10 perf reports re-run** on the final FFI surface to confirm no regressions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4849:docs/plan/test-pyramid.md:9:| Cross-FFI | UniFFI binding round-trip tests | Bindings stability, rev ordering, callback delivery | `apps/<name>/nmp-app-<name>/tests/` (post-M14) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4875:docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:78:M10.5 FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof                             — design done
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4880:docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:83:M14   UniFFI migration                                                — pending
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4891:docs/plan/status.md:43:- **No dedicated FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof gate before the kernel-boundary proof.** The prior M11 implicitly assumed the FFI surface was ready; this rewrite makes it a separate milestone (M10.5).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4893:docs/plan/status.md:46:The plan below is a single ladder of eighteen milestones (M0–M17, with M10.5 inserted as the FFI gate), each producing a runnable artifact, ordered so that each milestone strictly adds capabilities to the prior demoable product.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4897:docs/plan/parallelization.md:10:- **[M10.5](m10.5-ffi-hardening.md) (FFI hardening)** is itself parallelizable: the stress harness, the iPhone-12 perf rerun, the UI-script Sonnet-agent fleet, and the FFI surface audit are four independent workstreams.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4899:docs/plan/parallelization.md:12:- **[M15](m15-cross-platform.md) (Android + Desktop + Web)** is three parallel tracks once [M14](m14-uniffi.md) (UniFFI) lands.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4901:docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md:1:# M14 — UniFFI migration
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4918:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:234:The M3 implementation deletes none of the existing files outright — the kernel's `events: HashMap` and `profiles: HashMap` are replaced by calls to `Box<dyn EventStore>` held inside the `Kernel` struct, and the per-field tests (`kernel/tests.rs`) shift to the new trait via `MemEventStore`. No public FFI surface changes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4919:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:7:| 2026-05-18 01:24 | 0 | Session start. Pulled to e9cbafa. Plan revised (55dd5f2) inserting M10.5 (FFI hardening) and concretizing M11 (`../podcast` rebuild). 15-min cron heartbeat armed. First wave of 6 background agents dispatched: build-verifier (T7), debt-auditor (T6), m2-designer (T2), m3-designer (T3), m105-designer (T4), m11-designer (T5). T1 blocked on T7. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4921:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4922:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:10:| 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4930:2026-05-17T23:17:33.340610Z ERROR codex_models_manager::manager: failed to renew cache TTL: EOF while parsing a value at line 1 column 0
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5008:docs/aim.md:215:The core crate compiles as `cdylib + staticlib + rlib`. Desktop and CLI consumers link the rlib directly (no FFI). iOS links the staticlib via xcframework. Android links the cdylib via cargo-ndk. Web compiles to wasm32-unknown-unknown via the wasm crate. **One source of truth, four delivery paths.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5009:docs/plan.md:49:| [M14](plan/m14-uniffi.md) | UniFFI migration | Arc 3 — Release | |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5010:docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:25:│  Desktop iced shell (no FFI; direct rlib link)               │
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5011:docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:69:- **FFI.** Desktop slice uses direct rlib linking (per spec §4.3.1, this is bible-canonical for desktop). UniFFI is wired in when porting the slice to iOS/Android.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5012:docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:119:- **Slice through FFI to iOS first.** Rejected — desktop's direct rlib linking lets us validate the architecture without UniFFI noise. iOS wrap comes after the actor's API is stable.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5014:docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:12:The framework's existing FFI surface (UniFFI on iOS/Android, wasm-bindgen on web) expects **concrete, closed types**. Open enums don't exist in UniFFI. Two paths are possible:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5025:docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:168:- **Action.** A `uniffi::Enum` variant + an async fn in `nmp-actions` that implements it. Fire-and-forget at the FFI boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5026:docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:170:- **AppState.** A `uniffi::Record` carrying everything the UI needs to render. Cloned across FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5028:docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:41:| Reference platform | Desktop iced (rlib-linked, no FFI) | Maintained alongside iOS as a UniFFI-free diagnostic target — if something breaks on iOS but works on desktop, it's an FFI/Xcode issue, not architecture. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5031:docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:156:The desktop iced binary built in 1a.1 stays alive throughout 1a.2 through 1a.6. It links the rlib directly (no UniFFI). It is not a shipping product, but it acts as a non-FFI reference:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5032:docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:169:- **iOS toolchain risk is front-loaded.** UniFFI surprises, xcframework build, Xcode versioning — all happen in 1a.2. If they don't resolve cleanly, we know early and the desktop reference target is the fallback validation path.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5215:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4901:docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md:1:# M14 — UniFFI migration
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5273:    67	- Desktop: direct path dependency; no FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5288:   215	The core crate compiles as `cdylib + staticlib + rlib`. Desktop and CLI consumers link the rlib directly (no FFI). iOS links the staticlib via xcframework. Android links the cdylib via cargo-ndk. Web compiles to wasm32-unknown-unknown via the wasm crate. **One source of truth, four delivery paths.**

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5387:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:15:You are reviewing merge 810d0f8 (docs(readme): top-level README with TL;DR, decisions, architecture map) on master in nostr-multi-platform. This adds a top-level README.md with TL;DR summary, decision log, and architecture map. Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5402:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:187:/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"TODO|FIXME|D0|D1|D2|D3|D4|D5|M11\\.5|M12|M9|Wallet|DMs|Highlighter|podcast|framework-magic|outbox|negentropy|single writer|snapshots\" README.md docs crates apps ios AGENTS.md" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5408:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:194:README.md:25:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Canonical wording from [`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5](docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected. Conflicts between doctrines resolve in the order listed.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5409:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:195:README.md:26:  - **D0** Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core` (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5410:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:196:README.md:27:  - **D1** Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place. Placeholders are part of the type contract; the API has no "spinner gating cached content" pattern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5411:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:197:README.md:28:  - **D2** Negentropy first, REQ second. NIP-77 reconciliation with durable watermarks is the default backfill; live REQ is the tailing path. (See M4.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5412:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:198:README.md:29:  - **D3** Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out. Reads route per author write relays; publishes route to author write relays + tagged recipients' inbox relays. (See M2.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5413:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:199:README.md:30:  - **D4** Single writer per fact; caches derive. Five cache layers exist; one writer per fact; cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5414:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:200:README.md:31:  - **D5** Snapshots bounded by what's open. `AppState` carries the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5417:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:203:README.md:40:- **Empirical iOS proof before podcast rebuild (M10.5 gate).** No "we'll wire it up later" — the FFI surface ships rock-solid (stress harness + simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI fleet + Instruments leaks audit + iPhone-12 baseline) before any line of M11 code is written.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5418:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:204:README.md:69:   │ 10│      │ 42  │    │…       │    │  podcast,│   FFI crate, never into
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5420:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:206:README.md:90:| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D5 lives in §1.5. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5421:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:207:README.md:92:| `docs/design/` | Per-subsystem design docs — subscription compilation, LMDB schema, FFI hardening, podcast rebuild, framework-magic, NIP-29 crate. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5440:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2744:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2174:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3641:README.md:26:  - **D0** Kernel never grows app nouns (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5441:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2745:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2175:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3642:README.md:27:  - **D1** Best-effort rendering: placeholders → in-place refinement, never withhold known data
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5442:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2746:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2176:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3643:README.md:28:  - **D2** Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5443:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2747:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2177:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3644:README.md:29:  - **D3** Errors never cross FFI — become `toast: Option<String>` state fields
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5444:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2748:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2178:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3645:README.md:30:  - **D4** One writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5445:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2749:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2179:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3646:README.md:31:  - **D5** Capabilities report, never decide policy

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5651:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7235:README.md:25:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Canonical wording from [`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5](docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected. Conflicts between doctrines resolve in the order listed.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5652:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7236:README.md:26:  - **D0** Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core` (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5653:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7237:README.md:27:  - **D1** Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place. Placeholders are part of the type contract; the API has no "spinner gating cached content" pattern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5654:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7238:README.md:28:  - **D2** Negentropy first, REQ second. NIP-77 reconciliation with durable watermarks is the default backfill; live REQ is the tailing path. (See M4.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5655:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7239:README.md:29:  - **D3** Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out. Reads route per author write relays; publishes route to author write relays + tagged recipients' inbox relays. (See M2.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5656:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7240:README.md:30:  - **D4** Single writer per fact; caches derive. Five cache layers exist; one writer per fact; cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5657:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7241:README.md:31:  - **D5** Snapshots bounded by what's open. `AppState` carries the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5658:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7242:README.md:33:  Three additional load-bearing rules are not D-numbered but are equally binding (RMP-bible invariants from [`docs/aim.md` §2](docs/aim.md)): **errors never cross FFI** (operational failures become `toast: Option<String>` state fields), **capabilities report; never decide policy** (native bridges execute and report; Rust decides retry/recovery), and the **reactivity contract** (composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded; ADR-0001..0004).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:15:You are reviewing merge b49f8f3 (docs(plan): fold scope-adjustments into per-milestone files (codex follow-up)) on master in nostr-multi-platform. This folds scope adjustment notes into the per-milestone plan files (codex follow-up after plan split). Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:17:- D1: best-effort rendering
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:18:- D2: negentropy first
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:19:- D3: outbox routing automatic
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:20:- D4: single writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:21:- D5: snapshots bounded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:149:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:7:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:156:docs/plan.md:5:> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:318:     5	> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:322:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:357:    44	| [M10.5](plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md) | FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof | Hard gate | |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:362:    49	| [M14](plan/m14-uniffi.md) | UniFFI migration | Arc 3 — Release | |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:388:    10	| **NDK-style subscription aggregation** | [M2](m2-subscription-compilation.md) | Per `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7, the planner becomes a subscription compiler. Logical interests → per-relay plans → wire REQs, semantics-preserving merge/split. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:391:    13	| **FFI hardening + empirical iOS proof** | [M10.5](m10.5-ffi-hardening.md) | Dedicated stress harness, real-device measurement, simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI suite; hard gate before M11. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:437:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:7:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:452:    10	- **[M10.5](m10.5-ffi-hardening.md) (FFI hardening)** is itself parallelizable: the stress harness, the iPhone-12 perf rerun, the UI-script Sonnet-agent fleet, and the FFI surface audit are four independent workstreams.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:454:    12	- **[M15](m15-cross-platform.md) (Android + Desktop + Web)** is three parallel tracks once [M14](m14-uniffi.md) (UniFFI) lands.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:489:     1	# M14 — UniFFI migration
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:493:     5	**Demo product:** iOS app, podcast app, and (incoming) Android/Desktop/Web shells all bind to the kernel via UniFFI-generated bindings produced by `nmp gen modules`, not raw C FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:495:     7	**Scope.** Replace the current raw C FFI surface in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs` with the per-app generated `nmp-app-<name>` crate per ADR-0010. The iOS app stops importing `NmpCore.h` and instead imports the generated Swift module.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:499:    11	- `nmp-codegen` extended to produce UniFFI scaffolding in the generated per-app crate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:507:    19	- iOS app builds and runs against UniFFI-generated bindings; no raw C FFI in the app target.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:508:    20	- Cross-platform consistency test (next milestone) is unblocked because the FFI shape is now identical across platforms.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:511:    23	**Runnable artifact.** iOS Twitter + iOS Podcast apps both using UniFFI. Report in `docs/perf/m14/uniffi-migration.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:522:    11	- Kotlin bindings via UniFFI; cargo-ndk + Gradle pipeline.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:583:docs/plan.md:5:> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:618:    17	- Appendices: [FFI details and NIP glossary](product-spec/appendices.md)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:621:/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "D0|D1|D2|D3|D4|D5|doctrine|best-effort|negentropy first|outbox routing|single writer|snapshots bounded|capabilities report|errors never cross" docs/product-spec.md docs/plan.md docs/design docs/plan' in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:623:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:626:docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:56:- **Computed-from-native fields.** All formatting, all derivations live in Rust per doctrine D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:627:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:18:  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:628:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:42:- Doctrine review (D0–D5) signed off on the FFI surface in writing in `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:629:docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md:14:- ✅ Best-effort rendering (D1): placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:633:docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:39:- Profile placeholders → in-place refinement (D1; already done)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:639:docs/plan/status.md:10:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:641:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:103:- **Bare pubkey path.** If no kind:0 exists in the store yet, `open()` returns a placeholder-filled payload and the reverse-index dependency triggers `on_event_inserted` when one arrives. Doctrine D1 prohibits returning `None` here.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:642:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:234:- **Author kind:0 not yet loaded.** Per doctrine D1, render the item with placeholders. The `by_author` reindex on `on_projection_changed` updates the item in place. **The post is rendered immediately.**

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:682:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

Hex-validated via `is_hex_pubkey`. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace. Empty / non-hex inputs are **silently dropped** (see §7 finding D3-gap). | Silent no-op on null app or null pubkey. |
