docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:753:docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:215:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:754:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:242:17 `ViewModule`s. Each in `podcast-core/src/views/<name>.rs`. All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:759:docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:122:All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:760:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:4:> Substrate reference: [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §5; doctrine: D5 (capabilities report, never decide).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:761:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:85:### Bounded-state proof (D5)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:762:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:763:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:106:- The doctrine review at `docs/perf/m11/doctrine-review.md` signs off D0–D5 against the M11 surface (template: `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:765:docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:62:The test's assertion that no plaintext nsec crosses FFI is the structural witness for `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 (bounded native state) and for the implicit "secrets stay in Rust" rule — the platform layer never sees the unencrypted key material because every read/write of the key goes through the in-Rust `IdentityModule::sign` function.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:766:docs/design/podcast/wiring.md:88:- Acceptance: subscribe to a real RSS feed (Tim Ferriss) → library shows it → tap → see ≥ 5 episodes parsed; UI updates as `FetchFeed` action completes (no spinner gate; placeholder rows during fetch per doctrine D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:767:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:280:- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:768:docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:106:- "No business logic in native." (Doctrine D0 + AGENTS.md guardrails.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:769:docs/design/ffi-hardening/scenarios-detail.md:152:doctrine sign-off evidence for M10.5 (see gates.md §D1 note).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:771:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:282:## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:772:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:288:- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:773:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:774:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:42:**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #3 ("Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays") and bug #4 ("DM published to public relays"). Plus the doctrine-10 footgun: a "send everywhere" fallback that publishes a gift wrap to the global content relay because the recipient's inbox lookup returned empty.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:775:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:776:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:777:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:779:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:781:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:21:The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:783:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:25:| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:784:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:26:| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:785:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:27:| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:786:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:787:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:29:| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:788:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:30:| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:790:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:56:1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:793:docs/design/framework-magic/replaceable.md:6:This chapter holds four bullets, all of which discharge `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 **bug-extinction #1** ("Stale replaceable event retained in state after a newer one arrives") and `docs/aim.md` §6 **doctrine 4** ("replaceable-event invariants enforced on insert"). The four are split because they cover four distinct kind-class shapes and have four distinct test surfaces.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:794:docs/design/framework-magic/replaceable.md:16:**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #1. Plus the doctrine-4 footgun: an app caches kind:3 in its own state, fails to re-fetch on UI nav, renders a stale follow list, double-subscribes on the next session.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:797:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:799:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:800:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:801:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:29:The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:802:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:55:C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:803:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:57:C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:804:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:805:docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:905:There’s a larger consistency issue around the doctrine names: the plan header still names the old D2/D3/D5 meanings, while the newer framework-magic docs and your review brief use negentropy/outbox/bounded-snapshots. I’m checking whether that is isolated to the plan header or repeated in the touched docs.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:925:    17	A Cargo workspace shipping a Nostr-native **app kernel** (`nmp-core`), reusable **Nostr protocol modules** (`nmp-nip01`, `nmp-nip17`, `nmp-nip65`, etc.), app-owned extension modules, a codegen tool (`nmp gen modules`) that produces per-app concrete FFI enums/wrappers, FFI bindings for Swift/Kotlin/TypeScript, a wasm target, a scaffolding CLI, and reference platform shells.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:931:    23	The framework treats common Nostr-correctness failures (stale replaceable events, lost subscriptions, mis-routed publishes, double-publication, multi-account desync, leaked secrets across FFI, naive cache invalidation, withheld cached data, blocking-on-fetch UI patterns) as **product defects in the framework** rather than as developer mistakes. The public API is designed so that the wrong thing is hard to type.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:939:    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:947:    39	- Closed FFI enums that prevent modules from contributing typed views, actions, updates, capabilities, or identity scopes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:949:    41	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:962:    54	### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:968:    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:981:    73	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:985:    77	### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:987:    79	What crosses FFI is the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store. `AppState` carries small screen-shaped data plus a map of `ViewId → ViewPayload` for views currently in use. Closing a view evicts its payload from the snapshot. The event store itself never crosses FFI. See §6.2 and the FFI architecture appendix (§A1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1034:    21	The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1054:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1055:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:500:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:4:> Substrate reference: [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §5; doctrine: D5 (capabilities report, never decide).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1056:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:4:> Substrate reference: [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §5; doctrine: D5 (capabilities report, never decide).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1057:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:15:You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity contract, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1058:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:547:+- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1077:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:577:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2280:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1078:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:640:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2402:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1125:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2428:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1857:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6581:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1188:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3400:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2107:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1229:docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:682:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1328:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1763:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:591:++- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1368:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:2330:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6581:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1481:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3362:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1553:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4505:docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:48:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1566:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4869:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1624:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:299-351`: D2, D3, and D5 audit the wrong doctrines. They cover “reactivity contract,” “errors never cross FFI,” and “capabilities report,” but the stated doctrines are negentropy-first, automatic outbox routing, and snapshots bounded by open views. Fix those sections and remove the “all doctrines compliant” claims at `:410`, `:422`, and `:430` until the real doctrines are audited.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1567:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4871:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1635:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:299-351`: D2, D3, and D5 audit the wrong doctrines. They cover “reactivity contract,” “errors never cross FFI,” and “capabilities report,” but the stated doctrines are negentropy-first, automatic outbox routing, and snapshots bounded by open views. Fix those sections and remove the “all doctrines compliant” claims at `:410`, `:422`, and `:430` until the real doctrines are audited.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1568:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4961:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:4:> Substrate reference: [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §5; doctrine: D5 (capabilities report, never decide).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1569:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4977:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:15:You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity contract, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1570:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4981:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:547:+- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1610:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5556:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2107:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1641:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1624:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:299-351`: D2, D3, and D5 audit the wrong doctrines. They cover “reactivity contract,” “errors never cross FFI,” and “capabilities report,” but the stated doctrines are negentropy-first, automatic outbox routing, and snapshots bounded by open views. Fix those sections and remove the “all doctrines compliant” claims at `:410`, `:422`, and `:430` until the real doctrines are audited.

They cover “reactivity contract,” “errors never cross FFI,” and “capabilities report,” but the stated doctrines are negentropy-first, automatic outbox routing, and snapshots bounded by open views. Fix those sections and remove the “all doctrines compliant” claims at `:410`, `:422`, and `:430` until the real doctrines are audited.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1643:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:15:You are reviewing merge fb139ab on master in the nostr-multi-platform repo. Doctrine D0–D5 (kernel never grows app nouns; best-effort rendering; reactivity contract ≤60 Hz/view; errors never cross FFI; one writer per fact; capabilities report don't decide). File-size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1644:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:59:+You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity contract, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1645:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:591:++- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1703:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1448:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:15:You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity contract, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1704:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1449:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:547:+- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1707:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1491:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3465:    15	- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity contract, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1799:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:624:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1449:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:547:+- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1830:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:949:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2051:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:58:✅ D3 (errors never cross FFI): errors as advisory JSON data, not FFI codes
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1831:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:951:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2053:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:60:✅ D5 (capabilities report): iOS bridge is pure relay, no policy decisions
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1832:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:962:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2070:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:576:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1833:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:972:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2107:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1834:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:993:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2142:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:270:| **D2** ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound | S2, S3, S8 — emit-rate cap, planner dedup |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1835:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:994:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2143:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:271:| **D3** errors never cross FFI | S7 (exhaustion) + §7.2 (toast bridge) |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1837:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1052:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2679:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1841:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1178:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1897:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1857:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6581:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1985:    10	- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:2005:    30	- UniFFI bindings (current iOS bridge is raw C FFI).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:2045:- [docs/plan.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada/docs/plan.md:9) still lists the old doctrine map for D2/D3/D5, but the current spec uses D2 negentropy first, D3 automatic outbox routing, D5 bounded snapshots. The plan’s review rubric is now internally stale.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:15:You are reviewing merge 0dfb97581315aa04bd66341a98a32215de5e14d6 (the M11 podcast-app rebuild design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:664:+### 3.3 After Step 7 (wired)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:895:+Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:916:+- The doctrine review at `docs/perf/m11/doctrine-review.md` signs off D0–D5 against the M11 surface (template: `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:937:+- **Side**: `swift` = stays in `ios/NmpPodcast/Views/` verbatim (copy step only). `rust` = body deleted; replaced by Rust modules and FFI dispatch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:954:+Every `@Model` final class becomes a `DomainRecord` inside a `DomainModule` in `podcast-core`. The SwiftData `@Relationship(deleteRule:)` semantics are reimplemented as Rust-side cascade in CRUD APIs (see [`podcast-core.md`](podcast-core.md) §B). The Swift `@Model` files are deleted from the new app — Rust types cross FFI via generated ViewModule payloads.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:993:+| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1132:+### 2.1 Rust owns playback policy; native owns OS handles only
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1164:+### 3.3 Their 14-milestone roadmap
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1182:+- "No business logic in native." (Doctrine D0 + AGENTS.md guardrails.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1441:+17 `ViewModule`s. Each in `podcast-core/src/views/<name>.rs`. All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1616:+`nmp gen modules` produces `apps/podcast/nmp-app-podcast/` with the generated `AppAction`, `AppUpdate`, `ViewSpec`, capability trait composites, and UniFFI Swift bindings. Per ADR-0010 the Swift app sees:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1767:+All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1979:+`podcast-core` owns the action **shape**; the LLM call is delegated to `podcast-llm` via a private `ctx.dispatch_internal(SummarizePrompt { episode_id, style })` (one actor message tick — no FFI boundary crossed). This avoids `podcast-core` depending on `podcast-llm` while keeping the action namespaced under `podcast.*` from the Swift app's POV.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2078:+Reads happen at action boot only. Per RMP bible commandment #2, keys never cross FFI as part of dispatch — they live on the platform side; the bridge resolves and injects them when executing the capability.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2087:+- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2114:+| Pure-Rust HNSW (`hnsw_rs`, `instant-distance`) | No C extension; pure Rust; no FFI surface to worry about. | We give up on-disk persistence (memory-only) or roll our own (significant complexity). Recall-vs-build-time trade tuning becomes ours. | Considered as the fallback if sqlite-vec iOS spike fails. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2402:+- **Why**: `AVAudioSession` interactions with the kernel's actor crossing FFI on every `Tick` event can introduce jitter. The reference Swift app handles this in-process; we cross FFI ~4×/s plus on every state change. On a battery-throttled iPhone 12, this is non-zero.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2830:+- Acceptance: subscribe to a real RSS feed (Tim Ferriss) → library shows it → tap → see ≥ 5 episodes parsed; UI updates as `FetchFeed` action completes (no spinner gate; placeholder rows during fetch per doctrine D1).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2974:2. D5 — do the capability traits report only, or do any of them embed policy?

docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3035:docs/plan.md:5:> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3037:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3039:docs/plan.md:53:- **No dedicated FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof gate before the kernel-boundary proof.** The prior M11 implicitly assumed the FFI surface was ready; this rewrite makes it a separate milestone (M10.5).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3041:docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3053:docs/plan.md:341:### M10.5 — FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof *(hard gate before M11 starts)*
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3054:docs/plan.md:343:**Demo product:** The iOS Twitter slice from M1–M10 subjected to a published, exhaustive stress harness on the iOS simulator and a real iPhone 12. The kernel↔FFI↔SwiftUI path is proven, in measured numbers, to be **rock-solid and demonstrably performant** before a single line of the podcast app is written.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3055:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3103:docs/plan.md:537:**Demo product:** iOS app, podcast app, and (incoming) Android/Desktop/Web shells all bind to the kernel via UniFFI-generated bindings produced by `nmp gen modules`, not raw C FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3108:docs/plan.md:651:| **FFI hardening + empirical iOS proof** | M10.5 | Dedicated stress harness, real-device measurement, simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI suite; hard gate before M11. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3111:docs/plan.md:700:- **M10.5 (FFI hardening)** is itself parallelizable: the stress harness, the iPhone-12 perf rerun, the UI-script Sonnet-agent fleet, and the FFI surface audit are four independent workstreams.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3129:docs/aim.md:111:In our Rust framing, the actor owns the event store as internal substrate. `AppState` is not the store; it is the bounded UI projection of currently open views plus small app metadata. The full event store never crosses FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3132:docs/aim.md:133:The actor maintains a **global subscription planner**. Concurrent UI subscriptions with overlapping filters are coalesced into a single REQ on the wire — the kind of work clients typically do manually with hand-rolled grouping windows and dedup LRUs. Subscriptions auto-close when the last consumer drops them and when EOSE arrives if marked as one-shot. The planner buffers high-throughput events into batched UI updates (configurable; default ≤60Hz) to satisfy the RMP bible's constraint against high-frequency FFI loops.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3136:docs/aim.md:171:A **scaffolding CLI** (`<framework> init`) generates a complete starter project: the Rust core crate, the UniFFI binding generator, an iOS SwiftUI app, an Android Compose app, an iced desktop app, a web wasm shell, the `justfile` build orchestrator, an optional Nix flake. The starter app implements login, a timeline, compose, a profile screen, and DMs. It builds and runs on all four platforms immediately. This is modeled directly on RMP's `rmp init`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3137:docs/aim.md:241:2. **Where do views live?** (a) Materialized in `AppState`, (b) lazy with `ViewHandle` opaque references the UI subscribes to, (c) computed in platform code. Bible rules out (c). Pick between (a) and (b) — leaning (b) for efficiency, but it complicates the FFI surface.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3148:docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:15:Both surfaces are easy to get wrong. If relay status becomes raw socket callbacks, platform code starts owning protocol policy. If every diagnostic transition crosses FFI immediately, the status UI becomes its own firehose. If non-Nostr data is forced into fake Nostr events, the event store becomes polluted with records that do not obey Nostr semantics.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3154:docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:222:- Debug UIs can inspect relay and subscription state without raw socket callbacks crossing FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3158:docs/decisions/0005-domain-keyed-platform-shadow.md:64:- **Per-component FFI subscriptions** (one subscription per UI component). Rejected in the prior turn — multiplies FFI surface and pushes subscription lifecycle into native component code, which has known race conditions across fast remount/unmount in all three platforms.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3174:docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:159:- When a behavior is right on desktop but wrong on iOS → UniFFI / SwiftUI / build issue.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3185:docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:21:| Pure-Rust HNSW (`hnsw_rs`, `instant-distance`) | No C extension; pure Rust; no FFI surface to worry about. | We give up on-disk persistence (memory-only) or roll our own (significant complexity). Recall-vs-build-time trade tuning becomes ours. | Considered as the fallback if sqlite-vec iOS spike fails. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3203:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:164:`podcast-core` owns the action **shape**; the LLM call is delegated to `podcast-llm` via a private `ctx.dispatch_internal(SummarizePrompt { episode_id, style })` (one actor message tick — no FFI boundary crossed). This avoids `podcast-core` depending on `podcast-llm` while keeping the action namespaced under `podcast.*` from the Swift app's POV.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3493:A few issues are now concrete rather than stylistic: the design has at least one hard LOC conflict with the copy-verbatim requirement, and the screenshot gate currently depends on an agent-only UI introspection path. I’m pulling the exact lines for those plus the doctrine/D4 ownership concerns.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3720:    85	### Bounded-state proof (D5)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3776:    56	### 2.1 Rust owns playback policy; native owns OS handles only
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3978:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3980:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3997:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3999:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
