 succeeded in 0ms:
    55	**In scope.**
    56	
    57	- General-purpose social clients (timeline, threads, profiles, follows, reactions, reposts, quotes).
    58	- DM-first messengers (NIP-17 over NIP-44 + NIP-59).
    59	- Long-form publishing tools (NIP-23).
    60	- Wallets and zap-centric apps (NIP-47 / NIP-57 / NIP-60 / NIP-61).
    61	- Media-heavy clients (Blossom BUD-01/02).
    62	- List managers and curation tools.
    63	
    64	**Out of scope for v1.**
    65	
    66	- Relay implementations (we depend on `relay-builder` for tests; we do not ship a production relay).
    67	- New NIP authorship.
    68	- Game engines, AR, low-latency audio/video pipelines (the bible's Pika has these because it has voice/video calls; we do not adopt that scope).
    69	- Non-Nostr protocol support (Bluesky, ActivityPub, etc.).
    70	
    71	---
    72	
    73	## 3. Success criteria
    74	
    75	Acceptance is **demonstrable, not aspirational**. A claim that the framework works is provable by running these:
    76	
    77	### 3.1 Zero-to-running starter
    78	
    79	```bash
    80	nmp init my-app
    81	cd my-app && just run-ios   # works
    82	just run-android            # works
    83	just run-desktop            # works
    84	just run-web                # works
    85	```
    86	
    87	Result on each platform: a starter app with login (private key + NIP-46 bunker), a "following" timeline, compose, profile view, profile edit, and a DM inbox + thread. End-to-end build + first launch ≤ 5 minutes on a developer laptop with the framework's `nix develop` shell active, ≤ 15 minutes from cold without Nix.
    88	
    89	### 3.2 The "few hundred lines" test
    90	
    91	Across the four platform shells of the starter app, total non-generated platform code must fit within these budgets (excluding asset declarations and boilerplate `main`):
    92	
    93	| Platform | Budget (LOC, hand-written) |
    94	|----------|----------------------------|
    95	| iOS (SwiftUI) | ≤ 400 |
    96	| Android (Compose) | ≤ 400 |
    97	| Desktop (iced) | ≤ 600 (iced is more verbose; this is the bible's pattern) |
    98	| Web (wasm + TS/JSX shell) | ≤ 400 |
    99	
   100	Exceeding any budget is a framework-design failure: it means rendering logic is being forced to compensate for missing surface in the core.
   101	
   102	### 3.3 Bug class extinction
   103	
   104	Each of these classes must be structurally impossible to introduce through the safe framework public API. Lower-level Rust escape hatches used for tests or internal policy modules must be named, instrumented, and regression-tested. Each bug class below is paired with a regression test in `crates/nmp-testing`.
   105	
   106	1. Stale replaceable event (kind 0/3/10000-19999/30000-39999) retained in state after a newer one arrives.
   107	2. Subscription leaked after its UI is destroyed.
   108	3. Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays, without explicit override.
   109	4. DM published to public relays.
   110	5. Two account contexts having overlapping mutable state.
   111	6. Cache miss returning empty without triggering a fallback fetch.
   112	7. Profile-edit action that updates the local cache but fails to publish (or vice versa).
   113	8. Two concurrent UI subscriptions for the same filter producing two relay REQs.
   114	9. NIP-46 signing session lost on app suspend/resume without prompt.
   115	10. Re-published event missing its original `id` due to re-signing.
   116	
   117	Each test asserts the framework refuses the broken usage (compile-time, type-system, or runtime panic in debug) or routes around it transparently.
   118	
   119	### 3.4 LLM-friendliness
   120	

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Subscription Compilation §1–§2 — Problem and Logical Interest Model
     2	
     3	> Parent: `docs/design/subscription-compilation.md`.
     4	> Read first: `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7 (subscription compilation lessons), §2 (NDK outbox lessons), §9 (NMP principles).
     5	
     6	## 1. Problem — what is wrong with the current planner
     7	
     8	The kernel today encodes "where a REQ goes" as a two-valued enum and resolves it at the call site of every request. Concretely:
     9	
    10	- **Two hardcoded relays.** `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:1-2` declares `CONTENT_RELAY_URL = "wss://relay.primal.net"` and `INDEXER_RELAY_URL = "wss://purplepag.es"` as module-level constants. There is no per-author routing.
    11	- **Relay choice is a 2-variant enum, not a URL set.** `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:15-39` defines `RelayRole::{Content, Indexer}` with a `.url() -> &'static str` that returns one of the two literals. This shape cannot express "this REQ should go to the union of these N authors' write relays."
    12	- **The seam that emits REQs is parameterized by `RelayRole`.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:530-556` (`req()`) inserts a `WireSub { role, .. }` keyed by a string sub-id and emits `OutboundMessage { role, text }`. The role *is* the routing decision; there is no relay-URL field on `WireSub` or `OutboundMessage`. Any compiler that fans an interest out across N URLs has to replace this helper.
    13	- **Startup REQs ignore mailboxes by construction.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:50-106` (`startup_requests`) issues six fixed REQs, each pinned to `Content` or `Indexer`. The seed-bootstrap timeline (line 65–70) fans seven hundred-author future timelines through one relay. The exit-gate test for M2 ([`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)) requires that this fan exactly equal the union of those authors' write relays.
    14	- **View-open REQs ignore mailboxes too.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:404-439` (`author_requests`) hardcodes a three-REQ shape — `author-relays-N` on Indexer, `author-profile-N` on Indexer, `author-notes-N` on Content. The author's notes are fetched from the global content relay even though by the time the view opens we may already have that author's kind:10002 in cache (see next bullet).
    15	- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
    16	- **Profile claim path is single-relay.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:390-402` (`profile_claim_request`) sends a kind:0 fetch to `RelayRole::Indexer` unconditionally. It cannot consult mailboxes for the claimed author.
    17	- **No publish path exists yet.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:30` (no occurrences of `EVENT` outbound) and `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:42-45` (`OutboundMessage` carries only role + text). The first publish action (M6 `SendNote`) will hit this same `req()`-style seam. M2 must establish the planner shape before M6 builds the first user of it; the doctrine "no developer-supplied relays for a publish" (`docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 row "Publish leaked to wrong relays") needs a structural enforcement point.
    18	
    19	The summary diagnosis: **the planner is a string formatter, not a compiler.** Every REQ is a per-call-site decision; routing is one of two literals; recompilation is impossible because nothing is compiled. The diagnostics in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:117-154` already type `RelayStatus` / `WireSubscriptionStatus` / `LogicalInterestStatus` per ADR-0007 — but the planner currently emits at most one `LogicalInterestStatus` per view kind because there is no logical-interest object to scope it against.
    20	
    21	## 2. The logical interest model
    22	
    23	A **logical interest** is the actor-internal, semantics-preserving description of what a view, action, or monitor wants the kernel to keep alive on the wire. It is the input to compilation. It is *not* a Nostr filter (a filter is one possible wire artifact a plan can produce — `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7 lines 89–90).
    24	
    25	### 2.1 Formal shape
    26	
    27	```rust
    28	// crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/planner/interest.rs (proposed)
    29	
    30	/// A logical interest is what a kernel-side consumer (view, action, monitor,
    31	/// sync job, pointer loader) wants alive on the wire. The compiler turns N
    32	/// logical interests into M ≤ N per-relay plans.
    33	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    34	pub struct LogicalInterest {
    35	    /// Stable identity assigned by the registry on first insertion. Survives
    36	    /// recompilation. Two interests with identical content collide on hash but
    37	    /// keep distinct ids if they were registered by distinct claims.
    38	    pub id: InterestId,
    39	
    40	    /// Scope decides how mailbox resolution and indexer fallback behave.
    41	    /// Account-scoped interests resolve via the active account's mailbox view
    42	    /// for ambiguous filters (e.g. interests with no `authors` and no `#p`).
    43	    pub scope: InterestScope,
    44	
    45	    /// What the consumer wants. This is a normalised filter set, not a Nostr
    46	    /// wire filter. Tags use sorted vec representation so equality is stable.
    47	    pub shape: InterestShape,
    48	
    49	    /// Routing hints the consumer wants honoured. The compiler may ignore
    50	    /// hints if they conflict with policy (e.g. private-publish privacy).
    51	    pub hints: Vec<RelayHint>,
    52	
    53	    /// Lifecycle: tailing means "stay open after EOSE"; one_shot closes on
    54	    /// EOSE. Window is the planner's intent, not necessarily the relay
    55	    /// `since`/`until` it ends up emitting.
    56	    pub lifecycle: InterestLifecycle,
    57	}
    58	
    59	pub enum InterestScope {
    60	    /// Bound to the active account in SessionState. Re-routes on account switch.
    61	    ActiveAccount,
    62	    /// Bound to a specific account (multi-account UIs, M8). Re-routes on that
    63	    /// account's mailbox refresh; ignored on account switch.
    64	    Account(AccountId),
    65	    /// No account context. Used for global pointer loaders, NIP-19-driven
    66	    /// fetches, and indexer-direct probes.
    67	    Global,
    68	}
    69	
    70	/// A parameterized-replaceable event coordinate: the triple that uniquely
    71	/// identifies an addressable event (kinds 10000–19999, 30000–39999) across
    72	/// all relays. Equivalent to the `naddr` bech32 encoding without the relay hint.
    73	///
    74	/// Helper constructors (proposed in `nmp-nip19`):
    75	///   `NaddrCoord::from_naddr_bech32(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Nip19Error>`
    76	///   `fn to_naddr_bech32(&self, relay_hint: Option<&RelayUrl>) -> String`
    77	///
    78	/// Used in D8 substrate invariant: the composite reverse index extends to
    79	/// address pointers so view modules for NIP-22 thread comments and
    80	/// MetaTimelineViewModule highlights share one REQ per relay.
    81	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Ord, PartialOrd,
    82	         Serialize, Deserialize)]
    83	pub struct NaddrCoord {
    84	    pub pubkey: Pubkey,   // author of the addressed event
    85	    pub kind:   u32,      // addressable kind (matches BTreeSet<u32> shape)
    86	    pub d_tag:  String,   // the `d` tag value; empty string for kind:0
    87	}
    88	
    89	pub struct InterestShape {
    90	    pub authors:    BTreeSet<Pubkey>,        // empty = wildcard
    91	    pub kinds:      BTreeSet<u32>,           // empty = wildcard (rare)
    92	    pub tags:       BTreeMap<TagKey, BTreeSet<String>>,  // sorted for hash stability
    93	    pub since:      Option<UnixSeconds>,
    94	    pub until:      Option<UnixSeconds>,
    95	    pub limit:      Option<u32>,
    96	    pub event_ids:  BTreeSet<EventId>,       // for pointer/thread hydration
    97	    /// Parameterized-replaceable event coordinates for address-pointer hydration.
    98	    /// Non-empty when a view needs to resolve a specific `naddr` (e.g. a NIP-23
    99	    /// article in ThreadViewModule or MetaTimelineViewModule). The compiler routes
   100	    /// each coordinate to the addressed author's write relays (Stage 1 Outbox
   101	    /// direction keyed on `NaddrCoord::pubkey`). See §3.3 Rule 8 and §7.
   102	    /// Rationale: T21 research (NDK `$metaSubscribe` / svelte subscription
   103	    /// grouping — `docs/research/ndk/subscription-compilation.md` §Grouping)
   104	    /// shows filter-key-set identity drives merge eligibility; adding `addresses`
   105	    /// as a first-class field gives the merge lattice a stable key to union on
   106	    /// rather than encoding coords into opaque `#a` tag strings.
   107	    pub addresses:  BTreeSet<NaddrCoord>,    // empty = no address-pointer hydration
   108	}
   109	
   110	pub enum InterestLifecycle {
   111	    Tailing,                                   // stays open after EOSE
   112	    OneShot,                                   // CLOSE on EOSE
   113	    BoundedTime { until_ms: u64 },             // CLOSE on EOSE or deadline
   114	}
   115	```
   116	
   117	`InterestShape` mirrors the Nostr filter shape closely on purpose: most logical interests correspond directly to a single filter, and the kernel ships canonical normalisation (sort, dedup, fold ranges) so equality and hashing are deterministic. The compiler is then free to merge two shapes (or refuse to) on the basis of structural compatibility (§3 step 3).
   118	
   119	### 2.2 How view modules express interests
   120	
   121	Every `ViewModule` (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3) declares its dependencies via the existing `ViewDependencies` mechanism. M2 adds one new method:
   122	
   123	```rust
   124	pub trait ViewModule {
   125	    // ... existing methods ...
   126	
   127	    /// Translate a view spec into the logical interests required to keep it
   128	    /// live. Called by the planner when the view opens; re-called on
   129	    /// recompilation triggers that the view module opts into (account-switch,
   130	    /// mailbox refresh).
   131	    fn interests(spec: &Self::Spec, ctx: &InterestContext)
   132	        -> Vec<LogicalInterest>;
   133	}
   134	```
   135	
   136	`InterestContext` exposes read access to the mailbox cache and to the active account but **not** to the relay set; the view module never names relays. That keeps `nmp-aim.md` doctrine 5 ("outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") structurally enforced — there is no place for a view module to write a URL.
   137	
   138	Concrete examples for the existing seed-timeline path:
   139	
   140	- `TimelineView { authors: [pablof7z, fiatjaf, jb55, ...follows] }` returns one `LogicalInterest { shape: { authors, kinds: {1, 6}, limit: 200 }, lifecycle: Tailing }`.
   141	- `AuthorView { pubkey }` returns three interests: kind:10002 (Indexer fallback policy, see §3), kind:0 (one-shot), and `{ authors: [pubkey], kinds: {1, 6}, limit: 100 }` (Tailing).
   142	- `ProfileClaim { pubkey }` (the refcounted UI path from `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:202-237`) returns one interest: `{ authors: [pubkey], kinds: {0}, limit: 1, lifecycle: OneShot }`.
   143	- `ThreadView { event_id }` returns up to two interests: `{ ids: [...] }` for context, `{ kinds: {1, 6}, tags: { #e: [...] } }` for replies.
   144	- `ThreadViewModule` for a NIP-22 comment thread on a NIP-23 article returns an additional hydration interest:
   145	  `{ addresses: {NaddrCoord { pubkey: article_pk, kind: 30023, d_tag: "slug" }}, kinds: {30023}, lifecycle: OneShot }`.
   146	- `MetaTimelineViewModule` highlights-of-article registers the same coordinate:
   147	  `{ addresses: {NaddrCoord { pubkey: article_pk, kind: 30023, d_tag: "slug" }}, lifecycle: OneShot }`.
   148	
   149	**Worked example — address-pointer dedup across ThreadView and MetaTimeline:**
   150	
   151	```
   152	ThreadViewModule for kind:1111 comment on kind:30023 article →
   153	  hydrate interest { addresses: {(article_pk, 30023, "slug")} }
   154	MetaTimelineViewModule highlights-of-article →
   155	  hydrate interest { addresses: {(article_pk, 30023, "slug")} }
   156	Compiler Stage 1: both coords resolve to article_pk's write relays.
   157	Compiler Stage 3: Rule 7 (§3.3) unions the address sets (identical here).
   158	Result: ONE REQ per relay carrying { #a: ["30023:<article_pk>:slug"] }.
   159	```
   160	
   161	This is the D8 substrate invariant applied to address pointers: the composite reverse index, when extended to `NaddrCoord`, deduplicates across views without any view-module coordination.
   162	
   163	The seed-bootstrap path (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:50-106`) becomes one `LogicalInterest` per concern, registered at actor `Start` rather than emitted as raw REQs. The compiler produces the wire artifacts.
   164	
   165	### 2.3 Account scope binding
   166	
   167	The kernel `SessionState` (`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.4; M8 graduates this to multi-account) carries an active account id. `InterestScope::ActiveAccount` resolves at compile time, not at registration time. On account switch (§4 trigger A4), the compiler re-evaluates every `ActiveAccount`-scoped interest against the new active account's mailbox view. This is the structural enforcement of bug-extinction #5 (account-context overlap) referenced in [`docs/plan/m8-multi-account.md`](../../plan/m8-multi-account.md) — the kernel cannot "forget" to re-route because every plan re-derives from the active scope.
   168	
   169	Account-scoped interests with empty `authors` and empty `#p` (e.g. a free-form hashtag firehose) resolve against the active account's *read relays* (NIP-65 read side) — the user's own subscription preferences, not a globally hardcoded relay. Today's `firehose_requests()` at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:357-372` hardcodes `RelayRole::Content`; under the compiler this becomes "active-account read relays, falling back to indexer set if the active account has no kind:10002."
   170	
   171	### 2.4 What is *not* a logical interest
   172	
   173	To keep the surface small, the following are explicitly **not** logical interests:
   174	
   175	- A **wire REQ**. Wire REQs are produced by the compiler; they live in `WireSubscriptionStatus` per ADR-0007.
   176	- A **publish**. Publishes are durable actions on the action ledger (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4); they consult the `PublishPlanner` (§7) but they are not interests because they do not stay alive.
   177	- A **diagnostic record**. ADR-0007 lanes are facts derived from the planner's state, not inputs.
   178	- An **HTTP fetch** (Blossom upload, indexer JSON probe). Those are `CapabilityModule` requests.
   179	
   180	The boundary is intentional: an interest is anything that asks the planner to *keep a REQ open*. Everything else routes through a different seam.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Subscription Compilation §3 — The Compilation Pipeline
     2	
     3	> Parent: `docs/design/subscription-compilation.md`.
     4	> Read first: [intro.md](intro.md) for the `LogicalInterest` shape this stage consumes.
     5	
     6	The compiler is a pure function plus a small amount of state (the mailbox cache and the active plan registry). It runs whenever a recompilation trigger fires (§4) and produces a `CompiledPlan` that the wire-emitter applies as a diff against the relay sockets.
     7	
     8	## 3.0 Pipeline overview
     9	
    10	```
    11	   logical_interests:                 mailbox_cache + relay_config
    12	   Vec<LogicalInterest>                       │
    13	            │                                 │
    14	            ▼                                 ▼
    15	   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    16	   │ Stage 1: Resolve authors → mailboxes              │  (§3.1)
    17	   │   each author → { write, read, both, missing }    │
    18	   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    19	            │
    20	            ▼
    21	   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    22	   │ Stage 2: Indexer fallback for missing mailboxes   │  (§3.2)
    23	   │   missing → enqueue kind:10002 probe              │
    24	   │   missing-author reads → indexer set (read only)  │
    25	   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    26	            │
    27	            ▼
    28	   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    29	   │ Stage 3: Per-relay shape merge                    │  (§3.3)
    30	   │   group interests by target relay URL             │
    31	   │   merge compatible shapes inside each relay       │
    32	   │   refuse merges that would change semantics       │
    33	   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    34	            │
    35	            ▼
    36	   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    37	   │ Stage 4: Plan-id binding                          │  (§3.4)
    38	   │   compute plan_id = hash(interest_set,            │
    39	   │                          mailbox_snapshot,        │
    40	   │                          merge_lattice_version)   │
    41	   │   stable across no-op recompilations              │
    42	   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    43	            │
    44	            ▼
    45	   CompiledPlan { plan_id, per_relay: Vec<RelayPlan> }
    46	```
    47	
    48	The wire-emitter (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/wire.rs`, to be added) diffs the new plan against the current wire-sub registry: opens new REQs, closes orphaned ones, leaves stable assignments untouched.
    49	
    50	## 3.1 Stage 1 — Resolve authors to mailboxes
    51	
    52	Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
    53	
    54	Output: an `AuthorRouting` per author per direction:
    55	
    56	```rust
    57	pub struct AuthorRouting {
    58	    pub author: Pubkey,
    59	    pub direction: RoutingDirection,        // Outbox or Inbox
    60	    pub relays: BTreeSet<RelayUrl>,         // resolved write/read/both
    61	    pub source: RoutingSource,              // Nip65 | UserConfigured | Hint
    62	    // Note: there is no RoutingSource::Indexer variant — indexer fallback is
    63	    // modeled as UserConfigured { category: Indexer } (see diagnostics.md §5.0).
    64	    // This keeps the four-lane discipline strict: indexers are a sub-category of
    65	    // user-configured, not a fifth lane.
    66	    pub freshness_ms: Option<u64>,          // age of the kind:10002 record
    67	}
    68	
    69	pub enum RoutingDirection {
    70	    Outbox,    // for `authors:` filters — the author's *write* relays
    71	    Inbox,     // for `#p:` filters    — the tagged author's *read* relays
    72	}
    73	```
    74	
    75	Direction is decided by the interest's filter shape per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3:
    76	
    77	| Interest shape | Direction | Source per author |
    78	|---|---|---|
    79	| Non-empty `authors`, no `#p` | Outbox | author's `write_relays ∪ both_relays` |
    80	| Empty `authors`, non-empty `#p` | Inbox | tagged author's `read_relays ∪ both_relays` |
    81	| Both populated | Outbox primarily; Inbox interests split (see §3.3) | both |
    82	| Neither populated | (handled by stage 3 as "use active-account read relays") | — |
    83	
    84	`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 specifies one explicit override: DMs (NIP-17 gift-wraps, M9) fail closed if recipient inbox relays are missing. The compiler enforces this by refusing to produce a plan for an interest tagged `privacy = FailClosed` if any tagged-pubkey inbox lookup has empty relays or was sourced from `UserConfigured { category: Indexer }` (meaning no NIP-65-declared inbox exists). §7 details the publish-side enforcement.
    85	
    86	## 3.2 Stage 2 — Indexer fallback for unknown mailboxes
    87	
    88	The indexer set is a kernel-configured `Vec<RelayUrl>` (default: a small curated list; user-configurable in `AppConfig`). Today's `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:2` is the placeholder for one indexer relay (`purplepag.es`); the v1 indexer set lives in `AppConfig.indexer_relays`.
    89	
    90	Two distinct behaviours:
    91	
    92	1. **Mailbox probe.** For every author with `mailbox_cache.get(author) == None`, the compiler emits a `IndexerProbe { author }` side effect on the plan. The probe registers as its own short-lived `LogicalInterest { shape: { kinds: [10002], authors: [author], limit: 1 }, lifecycle: OneShot, scope: Global }`. Recompilation triggers (§4 trigger A1) re-route the original interest once the kind:10002 lands.
    93	2. **Read fallback.** For a `RoutingDirection::Outbox` interest whose author has no known mailboxes, the compiler routes the interest to the indexer set **as read-only fallback**. Per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." The resulting `AuthorRouting` carries `source: RoutingSource::UserConfigured` with a `UserConfiguredRelayFact { category: UserConfiguredCategory::Indexer }` record flowing to the diagnostic surface. The four-lane view (§5) renders "author X is being served by indexer Y because we have no mailbox for them" by examining the `category` subcategory — the Indexer is a sub-category of lane 4 (User-configured), not a fifth lane.
    94	
    95	Bounded: a single author's indexer probe is enqueued at most once per `compiler_probe_window_secs` (default 60s) to prevent thundering-herd probes if a screen of N rows all claim the same unknown pubkey.
    96	
    97	## 3.3 Stage 3 — Per-relay shape merge
    98	
    99	After Stage 1, every interest has one or more `(relay_url, sub_shape)` assignments. Stage 3 groups by relay URL and merges shapes where merging preserves semantics.
   100	
   101	### Merge rules (the lattice)
   102	
   103	Two `InterestShape`s `A` and `B` are **mergeable on relay R** iff:
   104	
   105	1. `A.kinds == B.kinds` **or** one is empty (wildcard absorbs).
   106	2. `A.tags.keys() == B.tags.keys()` (same tag dimensions) **and** the union of values per dimension stays under the relay's per-filter limit (default 1000).
   107	3. `A.since` and `B.since`: merged `since = min(A.since, B.since)` *only if* both are present or both absent. Mixing a bounded interest with an unbounded one is **not** merged (would broaden the bounded one's window).
   108	4. `A.until` and `B.until`: same rule, mirror of (3) with `max`.
   109	5. `A.limit` and `B.limit`: mergeable iff both are absent. If either has a `limit`, **do not merge** — broadening would mask the limit's intent.
   110	6. `A.lifecycle == B.lifecycle`. Tailing and one-shot do not merge (one-shot would never close).
   111	7. `A.event_ids` and `B.event_ids`: merge by union, capped at the relay's per-filter `ids` limit.
   112	8. **Rule 8 (address-pointer union).** `A.addresses` and `B.addresses` merge by `A.addresses ∪ B.addresses`, provided their other constraints (`authors`, `kinds`, `tags`, time, lifecycle) merge per Rules 1–7. Overlapping coordinates with differing time or lifecycle constraints do **not** merge — the compiler emits per-address sub-shapes so each `NaddrCoord`'s routing is independent. The union cap is the relay's per-filter `#a` value limit (default 1000). Address routing uses `NaddrCoord::pubkey` as the `AuthorRouting` input (Outbox direction, Stage 1), so the relays chosen are the addressed author's write relays — the same path as an `authors`-bearing filter for that pubkey.
   113	
   114	When mergeable, the merged shape is `{ authors: A.authors ∪ B.authors, addresses: A.addresses ∪ B.addresses, ... }`. The merged interest tracks both originating `InterestId`s so per-event dispatch back to consumers stays correct.
   115	
   116	When not mergeable, the two interests get distinct sub-shapes on the same relay, producing two distinct REQs. That is fine and expected.
   117	
   118	Open question 2 in the parent index (`subscription-compilation.md`) covers the `limit`-only corner case formally.
   119	
   120	The NMP merge lattice is simpler than Applesauce's `selectOptimalRelays` greedy set-cover
   121	(`docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md` §3, `relay-selection.ts:14-93`): Applesauce optimizes
   122	the number of relay connections by picking a minimum covering set across all contacts. NMP's
   123	Stage 3 merges shapes per relay but does not eliminate relays — the set-cover optimization
   124	(capping to `maxConnections`) is a future extension. For M2, every declared write relay gets
   125	a REQ; relay-count optimization is open question 8 (future ADR).
   126	
   127	### Per-relay output
   128	
   129	```rust
   130	pub struct RelayPlan {
   131	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
   132	    pub role_tags: BTreeSet<RoutingSource>,   // why this relay is in the plan
   133	    pub sub_shapes: Vec<SubShape>,            // each emits one REQ
   134	}
   135	
   136	pub struct SubShape {
   137	    pub shape: InterestShape,                  // canonical, post-merge
   138	    pub originating_interests: Vec<InterestId>,
   139	    pub canonical_filter_hash: String,         // for ADR-0007 WireSubscriptionStatus
   140	}
   141	```
   142	
   143	The wire-emitter renders each `SubShape` as exactly one `REQ` on `relay_url` with a sub-id of `c{plan_id}-r{relay_idx}-s{shape_idx}`. The sub-id is meaningful only to the kernel; diagnostics use `canonical_filter_hash` for stable identity across re-emission.
   144	
   145	## 3.4 Stage 4 — Plan-id binding
   146	
   147	`plan_id` is the **stable identity** the platform observes for diagnostic continuity. It answers: "did this recompilation actually change anything observable?"
   148	
   149	Definition: **hash only mailboxes referenced by the current interest set**, not the whole
   150	cache. This resolves open question 1 in the parent index — choosing this scope means that
   151	an unrelated author's kind:10002 arriving does not churn plan-ids for unrelated interests.
   152	
   153	```
   154	// referenced_pubkeys = union of all shape.authors and shape.tags[#p] across interest_set
   155	referenced_pubkeys = interest_set.iter()
   156	    .flat_map(|i| i.shape.authors.iter().chain(i.shape.tags.get("#p").unwrap_or(&[])))
   157	    .collect::<BTreeSet<Pubkey>>();
   158	
   159	plan_id = blake3(
   160	    sorted(interest_set.iter().map(|i| (i.id, i.shape, i.scope, i.lifecycle))),
   161	    sorted(referenced_pubkeys.iter().filter_map(|pk| mailbox_cache.get(pk))
   162	              .map(|ml| (ml.pubkey, ml.created_at, sorted(ml.write), sorted(ml.read)))),
   163	    INDEXER_SET_VERSION,
   164	    USER_CONFIGURED_RELAYS_VERSION,
   165	    MERGE_LATTICE_VERSION,
   166	)
   167	```
   168	
   169	Properties:
   170	
   171	- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
   172	- **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
   173	- **A new kind:10002 for a referenced author changes plan-id.** The hash only covers mailboxes for authors the current interest set touches, so a kind:10002 for an unrelated pubkey (not in any active interest's `authors` or `#p`) does not churn plan-ids.
   174	- **A new kind:10002 for an unreferenced author does NOT change plan-id.** This is the key
   175	  difference from hashing the whole snapshot. The wire-emitter's diff is unaffected. This
   176	  property is critical for D8 (reactivity contract — §1.5): recompilation work must be bounded
   177	  by what's open, not by the size of the total mailbox cache. Hashing only referenced mailboxes
   178	  ensures recompile cost scales with `|interest_set| × |referenced_authors|`, not with the
   179	  entire cache.
   180	- **Indexer set change changes plan-id.** Operator config edits surface immediately.
   181	
   182	The `plan_id` is stored on `CompiledPlan` and rendered into `LogicalInterestStatus` (extending the record at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:147-154` with `plan_id: String, plan_generation: u64`). Tests in §9 assert plan-id stability across no-op recompilations.
   183	
   184	## 3.5 Migration of existing functions
   185	
   186	This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
   187	
   188	| Current function (file:line) | M2 replacement |
   189	|---|---|
   190	| `startup_requests` (requests.rs:50-106) | **Relocated outside `nmp-core` per D0.** The seed timeline / kind:0 / kind:10002 / kind:3 bootstrap is social graph knowledge — not kernel substrate. It moves to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / the demo app's startup sequence. `nmp-core` only executes interests registered by modules; it does not hard-code which interests those are. The TEST_PUBKEY-specific bootstrap (line 71-82) moves to the demo app (or a test fixture in `nmp-testing`). The kernel's `ActorStart` handler calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::Startup)`, which is a no-op until a module registers interests. |
   191	| `open_author` (requests.rs:118-140) | Registers three `LogicalInterest`s scoped `ActiveAccount` (kind:10002, kind:0, kinds:1+6 for author); calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::ViewOpen)`. Refcount stays — but it lives on `InterestId` now, not on `ViewInterest { key, refcount }`. The `can_send` gate disappears: the compiler always produces a plan; the wire-emitter is the only thing that may queue deferrals. |
   192	| `open_thread` (requests.rs:142-168) | Registers a `Thread { event_id }` view-module spec; the view module returns interests with `event_ids` and `#e`-tag shapes. Hydration cascade in `prepare_thread_requests` (requests.rs:441-466) becomes part of the view module's `reduce` returning new interests when new event ids surface. |
   193	| `open_firehose_tag` (requests.rs:170-200) | Registers one `LogicalInterest { shape: { kinds: [1], tags: { #t: [tag] } }, scope: ActiveAccount, lifecycle: Tailing }`. Routes to active-account read relays per §3.1 table. |
   194	| `claim_profile` / `release_profile` (requests.rs:202-263) | Registers/unregisters one `LogicalInterest { shape: { authors: [pk], kinds: [0], limit: 1 }, lifecycle: OneShot }` per claim. Refcount of distinct consumers becomes the `InterestId` claim set inside the registry. **Dedup of (pk, kinds=[0]) across N timeline rows yields one merged SubShape and one wire REQ** — this is what bug-extinction "1000 avatars do not produce 1000 REQs" verifies. The refcount must be an integer counter, not a boolean presence flag — Applesauce gotcha `75ef7d5f` (`docs/research/applesauce/gotchas.md` §G2) shows the boolean model causes leaks when two consumers share a claim and one unsubscribes. |
   195	| `close_author` / `close_thread` (requests.rs:265-311) | Drop interests by `InterestId`; recompile with `Trigger::ViewClose`. Wire-emitter closes orphaned REQs. The "warm-close" grace from the view-warmth doctrine (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 "lifecycle") is the compiler's, not the view's — interests stay registered for the warmth window after their last claim. |
   196	| `close_subscriptions_with_prefixes` (requests.rs:313-331) | **Deleted.** The wire-emitter closes by `WireSubId`, which is the compiler's diff output. String-prefix matching of sub-ids is a 2026-05-period scaffold that the compiler removes. |
   197	| `pending_view_requests` (requests.rs:333-355) | Becomes `compiler.flush_deferred_for_relay(role, url)`: called when a relay reconnects (§4 trigger A3). The compiler resubmits its current plan against that relay's slot. |
   198	| `firehose_requests` (requests.rs:357-372) | Replaced as described above for `open_firehose_tag`. The `diag-firehose-N` sub-id scheme goes away — `canonical_filter_hash` plus `plan_id` give stable identity. |
   199	| `pending_profile_claim_requests` (requests.rs:374-388) | Disappears. Claims are interests; the compiler is the only thing that decides "this interest needs a REQ." |
   200	| `profile_claim_request` (requests.rs:390-402) | Disappears. The compiler routes claimed-profile interests through Stage 1; indexer fallback (Stage 2) handles the no-mailbox case. |
   201	| `author_requests` (requests.rs:404-439) | Disappears (replaced by `open_author`'s interest registration). |
   202	| `prepare_thread_requests` / `enqueue_thread_*` / `maybe_open_thread_hydration` (requests.rs:441-528) | Move to a `ThreadViewModule` in `nmp-nip10`. The hydration cascade is `view_module.reduce(...)` returning additional interests as new event ids surface in store. |
   203	| `req` (requests.rs:530-556) | **Deleted.** Replaced by the wire-emitter's `emit_req(relay_url, sub_id, filter)`. No call site outside the wire-emitter is permitted to construct a REQ. |
   204	| `defer_outbound` (requests.rs:558-568) | Moves to the wire-emitter; deferral is per-relay, keyed by URL, not by role. |
   205	| `ingest_relay_list` (ingest.rs:209-233) | Stays, but emits a `Trigger::Nip65Arrived { pubkey }` event (§4 trigger A1) on a material update. Becomes the producer side of the recompilation cycle. |
   206	| `ingest_profile` / `ingest_contacts` / `ingest_timeline_event` (ingest.rs:166-279) | Unchanged in storage shape. Their relevance to compilation is that they feed the view modules' projections (per `docs/design/reactivity/view-deltas-and-projections.md`). |
   207	| `should_store_event` (ingest.rs:268-279) | Unchanged. Per-sub-id string filtering goes away when sub-ids become `c{plan}-r{relay}-s{shape}`, but the predicate switches to "is this event id covered by an active interest?" — a `compiler.is_covered(event)` call. |
   208	| `maybe_open_timeline` (ingest.rs:329-365) | The "seed-contacts arrive → open union timeline" logic moves to `nmp-nip02` (follows/contacts module), not to an `nmp-core` helper. D0: `nmp-core` must not know the social graph. The contacts module watches the kind:3 projection and registers a `Timeline { authors: union }` interest on behalf of whichever view asked for it. |
   209	
   210	What this migration does **not** do (deferred per parent index open questions 3, 6, 7):
   211	
   212	- It does not move the action ledger into M2 — `SendNote` lands in M6.
   213	- It does not implement LMDB persistence for the mailbox cache — M3.
   214	- It does not implement NIP-77 watermarks — M4.
   215	- It does not add a per-author indexer-fallback ledger row — open question 3.
   216	- It does not move social bootstrap (follow-list timeline, account kind:0/3/10002 fetches)
   217	  into `nmp-core` — those are module concerns (D0). The impl PR for M2 must register those
   218	  interests from `nmp-nip01`/`nmp-nip02` or the demo app, not from the kernel actor directly.
   219	
   220	The compiler is **in-memory v1** by design. The mailbox cache is the existing `HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:313`); it just gets a new consumer.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Subscription Compilation §9 — Wire-Frame Audit Gate
     2	
     3	> Parent: `docs/design/subscription-compilation.md`.
     4	> Read first: [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md) (M2 exit gates); `docs/design/firehose-bench.md` (the modeled bench harness this test does *not* duplicate).
     5	
     6	The M2 exit gate is a single integration test that asserts on the *shape and identity* of the compiler's wire output, not on perf budgets. It is the structural-correctness counterpart to firehose-bench's perf-correctness suite.
     7	
     8	## 9.1 Test file location
     9	
    10	```
    11	crates/nmp-testing/tests/m2_subscription_compilation_audit.rs
    12	```
    13	
    14	The `crates/nmp-testing/tests/` directory does not exist yet; M2 creates it. This is intentional — it establishes the convention that planner-correctness gates live as Cargo integration tests under `nmp-testing/tests/`, distinct from the modeled benches under `nmp-testing/bin/`.
    15	
    16	`Cargo.toml` for `nmp-testing` needs the standard `[[test]]` block:
    17	
    18	```toml
    19	[[test]]
    20	name = "m2_subscription_compilation_audit"
    21	path = "tests/m2_subscription_compilation_audit.rs"
    22	```
    23	
    24	`cargo test -p nmp-testing --test m2_subscription_compilation_audit` is the M2 exit-gate invocation. CI adds this to the `cargo test --workspace` pre-merge gate per [`docs/plan/ci-hygiene.md`](../../plan/ci-hygiene.md).
    25	
    26	## 9.2 What the test asserts
    27	
    28	Four assertions corresponding to the four M2 exit-gate bullets in [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md), plus one substrate-invariant assertion (Assertion 5) covering D8 address-pointer dedup added by T24:
    29	
    30	### Assertion 1 — Bug-extinction #3 surface check
    31	
    32	> "No public API path lets the developer specify relays for a publish; explicit override action exists and produces a debug warning."
    33	
    34	> **Codegen dependency (M2 impl-PR gate).** The test below introspects an
    35	> `AppActionMeta` reflection helper that lives in the per-app generated crate
    36	> (ADR-0010). `AppActionMeta` does not exist yet — it is a hard M2 implementation-PR
    37	> deliverable. ADR-0010's codegen must emit it before this assertion can compile.
    38	> The M2 impl PR must either (a) add `AppActionMeta` emission to the codegen tool
    39	> (`nmp-gen modules`), or (b) provide a `proc-macro`-driven enum walker as a
    40	> fallback. The test is **not** `#[ignore]` in the final M2 gate (per §9.5 — the
    41	> audit test must never be skipped). The impl PR is blocked from merging until
    42	> `AppActionMeta` compiles. If the codegen work is not yet started, the M2 impl PR
    43	> is split: Part A (compiler + planner) can merge first; Part B (codegen + audit
    44	> test) merges when `AppActionMeta` is ready.
    45	
    46	```rust
    47	#[test]
    48	fn no_public_publish_action_carries_relay_list() {
    49	    // Compile-time-ish check: introspect the AppAction enum's generated variants
    50	    // (per ADR-0010, the per-app generated enum is a closed enum we can match
    51	    // exhaustively in tests). For every variant whose namespace starts with
    52	    // "kernel.publish" or "nip01.send" or "nip17.send", assert that no field
    53	    // is of type Vec<RelayUrl> EXCEPT the one variant `PublishWithOverride`.
    54	    let variants = AppActionMeta::all_variants();
    55	    for v in variants {
    56	        if v.is_publish_action() {
    57	            let has_relay_field = v.fields().any(|f| f.ty == "Vec<RelayUrl>");
    58	            if v.namespace == "kernel.publish_override" {
    59	                assert!(has_relay_field, "override must carry override_relays");
    60	            } else {
    61	                assert!(!has_relay_field,
    62	                    "{} must not expose a relays parameter", v.namespace);
    63	            }
    64	        }
    65	    }
    66	}
    67	```
    68	
    69	This is a "shape of the API" assertion, not a behaviour assertion. If a future PR adds a relay field to `SendNote`, the test fails.
    70	
    71	### Assertion 2 — Per-author wire fan-out for a 1000-author timeline
    72	
    73	> "For a timeline of 1000 authors, the compiled plan opens REQs only against the union of those authors' write relays (de-duplicated). Test asserts on the wire frame count."
    74	
    75	```rust
    76	#[test]
    77	fn timeline_compiles_to_per_relay_union() {
    78	    let mut harness = PlannerHarness::new();
    79	
    80	    // Seed mailbox cache with 1000 authors, deliberately overlapping relay sets:
    81	    //   - 600 authors use { wss://relay.damus.io, wss://nos.lol }
    82	    //   - 300 authors use { wss://nostr.wine, wss://nos.lol }
    83	    //   - 100 authors use { wss://operator-niche.example }
    84	    let authors = make_authors_with_overlapping_mailboxes(1000);
    85	    for (pk, mb) in &authors { harness.mailbox_cache_mut().put(pk.clone(), mb.clone()); }
    86	
    87	    // Register one Timeline interest containing all 1000 authors.
    88	    let interest_id = harness.register_interest(LogicalInterest {
    89	        scope: InterestScope::ActiveAccount,
    90	        shape: InterestShape::timeline_for(authors.iter().map(|(pk, _)| pk.clone()).collect()),
    91	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::Tailing,
    92	        ..LogicalInterest::default()
    93	    });
    94	
    95	    let plan = harness.compile().expect("compile");
    96	
    97	    // Assert: exactly one REQ per relay in the union of write relays.
    98	    let expected_relays: BTreeSet<RelayUrl> = authors.iter()
    99	        .flat_map(|(_, mb)| mb.write.iter().cloned())
   100	        .collect();
   101	    let actual_relays: BTreeSet<RelayUrl> = plan.per_relay.iter()
   102	        .map(|rp| rp.relay_url.clone()).collect();
   103	    assert_eq!(actual_relays, expected_relays);
   104	
   105	    // Assert: each relay carries exactly one SubShape (merge happened).
   106	    for rp in &plan.per_relay {
   107	        assert_eq!(rp.sub_shapes.len(), 1,
   108	            "relay {} should have one merged sub-shape, has {}",
   109	            rp.relay_url, rp.sub_shapes.len());
   110	    }
   111	
   112	    // Assert: each relay's authors are exactly the subset that declared it.
   113	    for rp in &plan.per_relay {
   114	        let expected_authors: BTreeSet<Pubkey> = authors.iter()
   115	            .filter(|(_, mb)| mb.write.contains(&rp.relay_url))
   116	            .map(|(pk, _)| pk.clone())
   117	            .collect();
   118	        let actual_authors = &rp.sub_shapes[0].shape.authors;
   119	        assert_eq!(actual_authors, &expected_authors,
   120	            "relay {} should serve only its declared authors", rp.relay_url);
   121	    }
   122	
   123	    // Assert: plan-id is deterministic — running compile twice yields the same id.
   124	    let plan2 = harness.compile().expect("compile #2");
   125	    assert_eq!(plan.plan_id, plan2.plan_id, "recompile with no input changes ≠ same plan_id");
   126	}
   127	```
   128	
   129	This is the single most load-bearing test in M2. It assert on:
   130	
   131	- **Relay count** = size of union of declared write relays (no extras, no misses).
   132	- **Per-relay author partition** = exact subset semantics.
   133	- **Sub-shape merge** = one REQ per relay (merge lattice worked).
   134	- **Plan-id stability** = re-compile is idempotent.
   135	
   136	### Assertion 3 — Late-arriving kind:10002 triggers recompilation
   137	
   138	> "An author whose mailbox was unknown gets re-routed once their kind:10002 lands, without the platform observing protocol churn."
   139	
   140	```rust
   141	#[test]
   142	fn late_nip65_arrival_reroutes_without_churn() {
   143	    let mut harness = PlannerHarness::new();
   144	    let target = pubkey("alice");
   145	
   146	    // Seed: no mailbox for alice. Register an interest that needs her.
   147	    harness.register_interest(LogicalInterest::timeline_for(vec![target.clone()]));
   148	    let plan_v1 = harness.compile().unwrap();
   149	
   150	    // The author should be routed via indexer fallback.
   151	    // Indexer fallback is UserConfigured { category: Indexer } — not a RoutingSource::Indexer
   152	    // variant (which does not exist; see compiler.md §3.1 and diagnostics.md §5.0).
   153	    let alice_relay_v1 = plan_v1.per_relay.iter()
   154	        .find(|rp| rp.sub_shapes[0].shape.authors.contains(&target))
   155	        .expect("alice routed somewhere");
   156	    assert!(alice_relay_v1.role_tags.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, RoutingSource::UserConfigured)),
   157	        "before NIP-65 lands, alice must route via UserConfigured (indexer fallback)");
   158	
   159	    // Now alice's kind:10002 arrives.
   160	    harness.ingest_nip65(&target, ["wss://alice-relay.example"]);
   161	
   162	    // The ingest emits Trigger::Nip65Arrived → recompile happens internally.
   163	    harness.flush_pending_triggers();
   164	    let plan_v2 = harness.last_compiled_plan();
   165	
   166	    // Assert: plan-id changed.
   167	    assert_ne!(plan_v1.plan_id, plan_v2.plan_id);
   168	
   169	    // Assert: alice now routes to her declared relay, not the indexer.
   170	    let alice_relay_v2 = plan_v2.per_relay.iter()
   171	        .find(|rp| rp.sub_shapes[0].shape.authors.contains(&target))
   172	        .expect("alice still routed");
   173	    assert_eq!(alice_relay_v2.relay_url, "wss://alice-relay.example".into());
   174	    assert!(alice_relay_v2.role_tags.contains(&RoutingSource::Nip65));
   175	
   176	    // Assert: the audit stream contains exactly ONE planner re-emission for alice;
   177	    // the platform sees one transition, not a thrash of N intermediate states.
   178	    let audit = harness.compile_audit_log();
   179	    let alice_transitions = audit.iter()
   180	        .filter(|a| a.affected_authors.contains(&target))
   181	        .count();
   182	    assert_eq!(alice_transitions, 1, "expected exactly one recompile for late NIP-65");
   183	}
   184	```
   185	
   186	This assertion is what `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §2 line 19 calls out as NDK's important operational truth: "metadata can arrive late… the system should be able to refresh or expand active work without the app tearing down and recreating views."
   187	
   188	### Assertion 4 — Four-lane diagnostic distinctness
   189	
   190	> "The diagnostics screen shows the four relay-fact lanes (NIP-65 / hint / provenance / user-configured) separately."
   191	
   192	```rust
   193	#[test]
   194	fn four_lanes_stay_distinct_in_diagnostic_payload() {
   195	    let mut harness = PlannerHarness::new();
   196	    let author = pubkey("alice");
   197	
   198	    // Set up evidence in all four lanes for the same relay url.
   199	    let url: RelayUrl = "wss://r.example".into();
   200	    harness.ingest_nip65(&author, [url.clone()]);              // Nip65 fact
   201	    harness.observe_hint(&author, url.clone(),                 // Hint fact (with subject: Pubkey)
   202	        HintSource::EventTag { event_id: eid("e1"), tag: TagKey::E, position: 2 });
   203	    harness.observe_provenance(&author, url.clone(), eid("e2")); // Provenance fact
   204	    harness.user_configured_relay(url.clone(),                 // UserConfigured fact
   205	        UserConfiguredCategory::Indexer);
   206	
   207	    let coverage = harness.open_view::<RelayCoverageView>(
   208	        RelayCoverageSpec { relay_url: url.clone() });
   209	
   210	    // Assert all four lane counts are non-zero — this is the structural guarantee.
   211	    // If any count is zero, the reducer is not consuming that lane's fact stream.
   212	    assert!(coverage.by_lane.nip65 >= 1,
   213	        "NIP-65 lane must be non-zero: reducer is not consuming Nip65RelayFact stream");
   214	    assert!(coverage.by_lane.hint >= 1,
   215	        "Hint lane must be non-zero: reducer is not consuming HintRelayFact stream \
   216	         (check that HintRelayFact.subject is set and the reducer reads it)");
   217	    assert!(coverage.by_lane.user_configured >= 1,
   218	        "UserConfigured lane must be non-zero: reducer is not consuming UserConfiguredRelayFact stream");
   219	    // Provenance count is the rolling 60s counter; alice's event landed once.
   220	    assert!(coverage.provenance_count_last_minute >= 1,

 succeeded in 0ms:
   220	    assert!(coverage.provenance_count_last_minute >= 1,
   221	        "Provenance lane must be non-zero: reducer is not consuming ProvenanceRelayFact stream");
   222	
   223	    // Exact counts (lanes must be distinct, not collapsed):
   224	    assert_eq!(coverage.by_lane.nip65, 1, "NIP-65 count wrong");
   225	    assert_eq!(coverage.by_lane.hint,  1, "Hint count wrong");
   226	    assert_eq!(coverage.by_lane.user_configured, 1, "UserConfigured count wrong");
   227	    assert_eq!(coverage.provenance_count_last_minute, 1, "Provenance count wrong");
   228	
   229	    // Structural: no compiler output collapses lanes.
   230	    let plan = harness.compile().unwrap();
   231	    let alice_assignment = plan.per_relay.iter()
   232	        .find(|rp| rp.relay_url == url).unwrap();
   233	    // role_tags is a SET, not a single value — lanes are preserved.
   234	    assert!(alice_assignment.role_tags.len() >= 1,
   235	        "role_tags should record at least one routing reason for this relay");
   236	    assert!(matches!(alice_assignment.role_tags.iter().next().unwrap(),
   237	        RoutingSource::Nip65 | RoutingSource::UserConfigured),
   238	        "routing source must be a known lane discriminant, not a collapsed 'relays' field");
   239	}
   240	```
   241	
   242	This assertion encodes the doctrine: a single relay may be in the plan for multiple reasons; the plan must say which reasons, not collapse them. The explicit non-zero checks (before the exact-count checks) catch the failure mode where a reducer is wired but receives no facts — a silent count-stays-at-zero bug that the original `assert_eq!(count, 1)` alone would catch, but which is clearer with an explicit "lane not consumed" message.
   243	
   244	### Assertion 5 — Address-pointer dedup across ThreadView and MetaTimeline
   245	
   246	> "Two views registering the same `NaddrCoord` emit ONE REQ per relay (Rule 8 address-pointer union, D8 invariant)."
   247	
   248	```rust
   249	#[test]
   250	fn address_pointer_dedup_across_thread_and_meta_subscribe() {
   251	    let mut h = PlannerHarness::new();
   252	    let pk = pubkey("article_author");
   253	    h.ingest_nip65(&pk, ["wss://article-relay.example"]);
   254	    let coord = NaddrCoord { pubkey: pk, kind: 30023, d_tag: "my-post".into() };
   255	    let mk = || LogicalInterest {
   256	        scope: InterestScope::Global,
   257	        shape: InterestShape { addresses: [coord.clone()].into(),
   258	                               kinds: [30023].into(), ..Default::default() },
   259	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::OneShot, ..Default::default()
   260	    };
   261	    h.register_interest(mk()); // ThreadViewModule hydration
   262	    h.register_interest(mk()); // MetaTimelineViewModule hydration
   263	    let plan = h.compile().expect("compile");
   264	    assert_eq!(plan.per_relay.len(), 1);
   265	    assert_eq!(plan.per_relay[0].sub_shapes.len(), 1,
   266	        "Rule 8 must merge identical address sets into one SubShape");
   267	}
   268	```
   269	
   270	### Assertion 5 — Address-pointer dedup across ThreadView and MetaTimeline
   271	
   272	> "Two views registering the same `NaddrCoord` emit ONE REQ per relay (Rule 8 address-pointer union, D8 invariant)."
   273	
   274	```rust
   275	#[test]
   276	fn address_pointer_dedup_across_thread_and_meta_subscribe() {
   277	    let mut h = PlannerHarness::new();
   278	    let pk = pubkey("article_author");
   279	    h.ingest_nip65(&pk, ["wss://article-relay.example"]);
   280	    let coord = NaddrCoord { pubkey: pk, kind: 30023, d_tag: "my-post".into() };
   281	    let mk = || LogicalInterest {
   282	        scope: InterestScope::Global,
   283	        shape: InterestShape { addresses: [coord.clone()].into(),
   284	                               kinds: [30023].into(), ..Default::default() },
   285	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::OneShot, ..Default::default()
   286	    };
   287	    h.register_interest(mk()); // ThreadViewModule hydration
   288	    h.register_interest(mk()); // MetaTimelineViewModule hydration
   289	    let plan = h.compile().expect("compile");
   290	    assert_eq!(plan.per_relay.len(), 1);
   291	    assert_eq!(plan.per_relay[0].sub_shapes.len(), 1,
   292	        "Rule 8 must merge identical address sets into one SubShape");
   293	}
   294	```
   295	
   296	## 9.3 The `PlannerHarness`
   297	
   298	The test harness is itself part of `nmp-testing`:
   299	
   300	```rust
   301	// crates/nmp-testing/src/planner_harness.rs (proposed)
   302	
   303	pub struct PlannerHarness {
   304	    cache: InMemoryMailboxCache,
   305	    user_config: UserConfiguredRelays,
   306	    indexer_set: Vec<RelayUrl>,
   307	    interests: InterestRegistry,
   308	    compiler: SubscriptionCompiler,
   309	    audit_log: Vec<CompileAuditEntry>,
   310	}
   311	
   312	impl PlannerHarness {
   313	    pub fn new() -> Self;
   314	    pub fn mailbox_cache_mut(&mut self) -> &mut dyn MailboxCache;
   315	    pub fn register_interest(&mut self, i: LogicalInterest) -> InterestId;
   316	    pub fn drop_interest(&mut self, id: InterestId);
   317	    pub fn ingest_nip65(&mut self, author: &Pubkey, relays: impl IntoIterator<Item = RelayUrl>);
   318	    pub fn observe_hint(&mut self, author: &Pubkey, url: RelayUrl, source: HintSource);
   319	    pub fn observe_provenance(&mut self, author: &Pubkey, url: RelayUrl, event: EventId);
   320	    pub fn user_configured_relay(&mut self, url: RelayUrl, cat: UserConfiguredCategory);
   321	    pub fn force_recompile(&mut self, reason: InvalidateReason);
   322	    pub fn flush_pending_triggers(&mut self);
   323	    pub fn compile(&mut self) -> Result<CompiledPlan, CompileError>;
   324	    pub fn last_compiled_plan(&self) -> &CompiledPlan;
   325	    pub fn compile_audit_log(&self) -> &[CompileAuditEntry];
   326	    pub fn open_view<V: ViewModule>(&mut self, spec: V::Spec) -> V::Payload;
   327	}
   328	```
   329	
   330	The harness is the *minimum* surface required for the four assertions above. It is deliberately small so it does not become its own moving target.
   331	
   332	## 9.4 What this test does *not* cover
   333	
   334	By design (these belong to other M2 gates or later milestones):
   335	
   336	- **Real wire frames against a relay.** This is `firehose-bench live` per [`docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md`](../../plan/m1-twitter-slice.md) (M1 exit gate "Firehose-bench live cold_start"); the audit test is offline and synthetic.
   337	- **Wire-emitter diff correctness across two plans.** That is a separate unit test inside `nmp-core::kernel::wire`, not the milestone-exit gate.
   338	- **NIP-77 watermarks.** M4.
   339	- **Per-account auth state.** M5.
   340	- **The publish path running end-to-end.** M6.
   341	
   342	The audit gate's job is exactly the four assertions: API shape, fan-out structure, recompilation on late NIP-65, and four-lane diagnostic distinctness. Those are the four exit-gate bullets the milestone document lists; this test is the verification surface for all four.
   343	
   344	## 9.5 CI integration
   345	
   346	The test runs in the default `cargo test --workspace` job and takes < 1 second on standard hardware (no networking, no LMDB, in-memory cache only). It is the canonical regression test for "did someone re-introduce the hardcoded two-role planner?" and as such must never be skipped or `#[ignore]`d.
   347	
   348	If the M3 (LMDB) milestone graduates the mailbox cache to a real backend, this test continues to exercise the trait surface via the `InMemoryMailboxCache` impl — no changes required. That is the seam `nmp-nip65::cache::MailboxCache` exists for.

 succeeded in 0ms:
README.md:23:- **Framework-magic contract** (kind:3 auto-tracking, `bunker://` onboarding, new-nsec creation, outbox-by-default-on-publish, etc.): designed at [`docs/design/framework-magic.md`](docs/design/framework-magic.md) with 13 behaviors and 14 named tests in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`. Aligned with canonical D0–D8 (T19 complete).
README.md:29:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D8).** Canonical wording from [`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5](docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected. Conflicts resolve in the order listed. **Two kinds:** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime / FFI / hot-path constraints). Both equally binding.
README.md:30:  - **D0** Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`.
README.md:31:  - **D1** Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place. Placeholders are part of the type contract.
README.md:32:  - **D2** Negentropy first, REQ second. NIP-77 reconciliation with durable watermarks is the default backfill. (M4.)
README.md:33:  - **D3** Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out. (M2.)
README.md:34:  - **D4** Single writer per fact; caches derive. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API.
README.md:35:  - **D5** Snapshots bounded by what's open. `AppState` carries the projection through currently-open views.
README.md:36:  - **D6** Errors never cross FFI as exceptions. Surface as `toast: Option<String>` state fields. (RMP bible invariant #2.)
README.md:37:  - **D7** Capabilities report; never decide policy. Native bridges execute platform APIs; Rust decides retry/recovery/routing. (RMP bible rule #6.)
README.md:38:  - **D8** Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded · zero per-event allocations after warmup. Validated by `reactivity-bench`. (ADR-0001..0004.)
README.md:44:- **Post-merge codex review.** After every push to master, `codex exec` reviews the diff against the doctrine + file-size rules + spec coherence. Codex output saved to `docs/perf/codex-reviews/<sha>.md`. Any real concerns become fix-it TaskList entries.
README.md:84:- **Codex review on every merge.** `codex exec` is the post-merge doctrine reviewer. Saved transcripts in `docs/perf/codex-reviews/`.
README.md:95:| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D8 lives in §1.5. |
README.md:108:2. `docs/product-spec.md` §1.5 — the doctrine D0–D8.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:27:## 1.5 Cardinal doctrines D0–D8
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:29:See [`docs/product-spec/doctrine.md`](./doctrine.md) for the full text of all nine doctrines.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:Summary: D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime implementation constraints). Both are equally binding. Every API decision answers to at least one; conflicts resolve in the order D0 > D1 > … > D8.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:35:| D0 | No app nouns in `nmp-core`; test surface gated behind `test-support` feature | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:36:| D1 | Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:37:| D2 | Negentropy first, REQ second | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:38:| D3 | Outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:39:| D4 | Single writer per fact; caches derive | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:40:| D5 | Snapshots bounded by what's open | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:| D6 | Errors never cross FFI as exceptions | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:42:| D7 | Capabilities report; never decide policy | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:43:| D8 | Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded; idle-tick emit gated on `changed_since_emit()` | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:1:# Cardinal Doctrines D0–D8
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:7:**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:9:## D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:21:## D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:25:The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:34:## D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:40:## D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:53:## D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:55:The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:57:## D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:61:## D6. Errors never cross FFI as exceptions
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:73:## D7. Capabilities report; never decide policy
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:85:## D8. Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:87:The substrate that delivers D1, D2, D4, D5 in practice. Every inserted event participates in a composite reverse index that names the views interested in it; view recompute is bounded to ≤60 deltas per second per view; the working set is hot-resident with claim-pinned overlays. Allocations after warmup are linear in active-view count, never in cached-event count. False-wakeup rate ≤ 0.10 candidates per delta.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:89:The idle-tick emit path is gated on `kernel.changed_since_emit()` — idle ticks that produce no state change must not emit snapshots (D8 regression guard).

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! The subscription compiler: 4-stage pipeline from `Vec<LogicalInterest>`
     2	//! to `CompiledPlan`.
     3	//!
     4	//! ## Pipeline stages
     5	//!
     6	//! 1. **Resolve authors → mailboxes** — consult `MailboxCache` (stubbed in
     7	//!    phase 1 via `EmptyMailboxCache`; real impl lives in `nmp-nip65`).
     8	//! 2. **Indexer fallback** — authors with no known mailbox route to the
     9	//!    configured indexer set.
    10	//! 3. **Per-relay shape merge** — group by relay URL; merge compatible shapes
    11	//!    with `lattice::merge()` (Rules 1–8). Author sets are partitioned per
    12	//!    relay — only authors that declared a relay appear in its sub-shape.
    13	//! 4. **Plan-id binding** — hash(sorted interests, sorted mailbox snapshot,
    14	//!    lattice version) → stable `plan_id`.
    15	//!
    16	//! Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3
    17	//! Doctrine: D3 (outbox routing automatic), D6 (errors never cross FFI),
    18	//!           D8 (zero per-event allocs after warmup).
    19	//!
    20	//! Phase 1 scope: mailbox stage stubs to `EmptyMailboxCache`. Real mailbox
    21	//! resolution lives in `nmp-nip65` (separate later slice). The call-point
    22	//! for `EventStore::coverage()` is marked with a placeholder comment per the
    23	//! task spec (not wired in phase 1).
    24	
    25	use std::collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet, HashMap};
    26	
    27	use super::{
    28	    interest::{
    29	        InterestId, InterestLifecycle, InterestShape, LogicalInterest, NaddrCoord, Pubkey,
    30	        RelayUrl,
    31	    },
    32	    lattice::{merge, MergeOutcome},
    33	    plan::{CompiledPlan, PlannerError, RelayPlan, RoutingSource, SubShape},
    34	};
    35	
    36	// ─── MailboxCache seam ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37	
    38	/// Minimal mailbox snapshot used by the compiler.
    39	///
    40	/// Phase 1: only `write_relays` and `both_relays` are consumed (Outbox
    41	/// direction). Inbox direction (read_relays) is used for `#p` interests, which
    42	/// are not yet wired in phase 1.
    43	///
    44	/// Full trait lives in `nmp-nip65::cache::MailboxCache` (later slice).
    45	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)]
    46	pub struct MailboxSnapshot {
    47	    pub write_relays: Vec<RelayUrl>,
    48	    pub read_relays: Vec<RelayUrl>,
    49	    pub both_relays: Vec<RelayUrl>,
    50	}
    51	
    52	impl MailboxSnapshot {
    53	    /// All relays relevant for Outbox direction (write + both).
    54	    pub fn outbox_relays(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &RelayUrl> {
    55	        self.write_relays.iter().chain(self.both_relays.iter())
    56	    }
    57	}
    58	
    59	/// Minimum surface the compiler needs for mailbox lookups.
    60	/// Phase 1 implementation: `EmptyMailboxCache` always returns `None`.
    61	/// Phase 2 implementation: `nmp-nip65::InMemoryMailboxCache`.
    62	pub trait MailboxCache: Send + Sync {
    63	    fn get(&self, pubkey: &Pubkey) -> Option<MailboxSnapshot>;
    64	    /// Snapshot of all known entries for plan-id hashing.
    65	    fn snapshot_all(&self) -> Vec<(Pubkey, MailboxSnapshot)>;
    66	    /// Monotonic generation counter — advances on every accepted `put`.
    67	    fn generation(&self) -> u64;
    68	}
    69	
    70	/// Phase 1 stub: no mailbox data. All authors fall back to the indexer set.
    71	pub struct EmptyMailboxCache;
    72	
    73	impl MailboxCache for EmptyMailboxCache {
    74	    fn get(&self, _pubkey: &Pubkey) -> Option<MailboxSnapshot> {
    75	        None
    76	    }
    77	    fn snapshot_all(&self) -> Vec<(Pubkey, MailboxSnapshot)> {
    78	        Vec::new()
    79	    }
    80	    fn generation(&self) -> u64 {
    81	        0
    82	    }
    83	}
    84	
    85	/// Simple in-memory mailbox cache for tests and the planner harness.
    86	#[derive(Default)]
    87	pub struct InMemoryMailboxCache {
    88	    data: HashMap<Pubkey, MailboxSnapshot>,
    89	    generation: u64,
    90	}
    91	
    92	impl InMemoryMailboxCache {
    93	    pub fn new() -> Self {
    94	        Self::default()
    95	    }
    96	
    97	    pub fn put(&mut self, pubkey: Pubkey, snapshot: MailboxSnapshot) {
    98	        self.data.insert(pubkey, snapshot);
    99	        self.generation = self.generation.saturating_add(1);
   100	    }
   101	}
   102	
   103	impl MailboxCache for InMemoryMailboxCache {
   104	    fn get(&self, pubkey: &Pubkey) -> Option<MailboxSnapshot> {
   105	        self.data.get(pubkey).cloned()
   106	    }
   107	    fn snapshot_all(&self) -> Vec<(Pubkey, MailboxSnapshot)> {
   108	        self.data.iter().map(|(k, v)| (k.clone(), v.clone())).collect()
   109	    }
   110	    fn generation(&self) -> u64 {
   111	        self.generation
   112	    }
   113	}
   114	
   115	// ─── Internal relay-partitioned entry ────────────────────────────────────────
   116	
   117	/// A relay-partitioned slice of one logical interest.
   118	///
   119	/// When an interest has N authors, Stage 1 produces one `RelayEntry` per
   120	/// `(relay, interest_id)` pair, where `authors_for_relay` contains only the
   121	/// authors that declared this specific relay (not all N authors). This is the
   122	/// author-partitioning that lets the merge lattice produce per-relay author
   123	/// subsets.
   124	struct RelayEntry {
   125	    /// The interest's non-author fields (kinds, tags, since, until, etc.).
   126	    /// `authors` is intentionally left empty here; we merge `authors_for_relay`
   127	    /// in at Stage 3 merge time.
   128	    base_shape: InterestShape,
   129	    /// The subset of authors from this interest that declared this relay.
   130	    authors_for_relay: BTreeSet<Pubkey>,
   131	    /// Address-pointer coordinates from this interest (if relevant for routing).
   132	    addresses_for_relay: BTreeSet<NaddrCoord>,
   133	    lifecycle: InterestLifecycle,
   134	    source: RoutingSource,
   135	    interest_id: InterestId,
   136	}
   137	
   138	impl RelayEntry {
   139	    /// Construct the final `InterestShape` for this relay slice.
   140	    fn into_shape(mut self) -> (InterestShape, InterestLifecycle, RoutingSource, InterestId) {
   141	        self.base_shape.authors = self.authors_for_relay;
   142	        self.base_shape.addresses = self.addresses_for_relay;
   143	        (self.base_shape, self.lifecycle, self.source, self.interest_id)
   144	    }
   145	}
   146	
   147	// ─── SubscriptionCompiler ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   148	
   149	/// Version of the merge lattice — bump when Rule semantics change.
   150	/// Included in plan-id hash to ensure plan-ids invalidate on lattice changes.
   151	const MERGE_LATTICE_VERSION: u8 = 1;
   152	
   153	/// The subscription compiler.
   154	///
   155	/// Holds a reference to the mailbox cache and indexer relay set. Both may be
   156	/// updated between compilations (the compiler always reads the current state).
   157	pub struct SubscriptionCompiler<'a> {
   158	    mailbox_cache: &'a dyn MailboxCache,
   159	    indexer_relays: &'a [RelayUrl],
   160	}
   161	
   162	impl<'a> SubscriptionCompiler<'a> {
   163	    /// Construct a compiler bound to a mailbox cache and indexer set.
   164	    pub fn new(mailbox_cache: &'a dyn MailboxCache, indexer_relays: &'a [RelayUrl]) -> Self {
   165	        Self { mailbox_cache, indexer_relays }
   166	    }
   167	
   168	    /// Compile a set of logical interests into a `CompiledPlan`.
   169	    ///
   170	    /// ## Stages
   171	    /// 1. Resolve each interest's authors to relay URLs via the mailbox cache.
   172	    ///    Authors are **partitioned** per relay — only the authors that declared
   173	    ///    a relay appear in that relay's sub-shape.
   174	    /// 2. Fall back missing authors to the indexer set.
   175	    /// 3. Group relay entries by relay URL; merge compatible shapes per Rules 1–8.
   176	    /// 4. Compute `plan_id` via a stable deterministic hash.
   177	    ///
   178	    /// # EventStore coverage (phase 1 placeholder)
   179	    /// The compiler does not yet consult `EventStore::coverage()` for cache-aware
   180	    /// planning. The call-point is reserved here for the phase 2 / M3 wiring:
   181	    ///
   182	    /// ```text
   183	    /// // TODO(phase2): let coverage = event_store.coverage(&watermark_key)?;
   184	    /// // Use coverage to skip REQs whose time-range is fully cached locally.
   185	    /// ```
   186	    pub fn compile(
   187	        &self,
   188	        interests: &[LogicalInterest],
   189	    ) -> Result<CompiledPlan, PlannerError> {
   190	        // ── Stages 1 + 2: author-partitioned relay entry collection ──────────
   191	        // relay_url → Vec<RelayEntry>
   192	        let mut relay_entries: BTreeMap<RelayUrl, Vec<RelayEntry>> = BTreeMap::new();
   193	
   194	        for interest in interests {
   195	            self.partition_interest(interest, &mut relay_entries);
   196	        }
   197	
   198	        // ── Stage 3: Per-relay shape merge ──────────────────────────────────
   199	        let mut per_relay: BTreeMap<RelayUrl, RelayPlan> = BTreeMap::new();
   200	
   201	        for (relay_url, entries) in relay_entries {
   202	            let mut role_tags: BTreeSet<RoutingSource> = BTreeSet::new();
   203	
   204	            // Convert RelayEntry → (shape, lifecycle, source, id)
   205	            let mut resolved: Vec<(InterestShape, InterestLifecycle, RoutingSource, InterestId)> =
   206	                entries
   207	                    .into_iter()
   208	                    .map(|entry| {
   209	                        let source = entry.source.clone();
   210	                        role_tags.insert(source);
   211	                        entry.into_shape()
   212	                    })
   213	                    .collect();
   214	
   215	            // Greedy pairwise merge
   216	            let mut sub_shapes: Vec<(InterestShape, InterestLifecycle, Vec<InterestId>)> =
   217	                Vec::new();
   218	
   219	            for (shape, lifecycle, _source, interest_id) in resolved.drain(..) {
   220	                let mut merged = false;
   221	                for (existing_shape, existing_lifecycle, existing_ids) in sub_shapes.iter_mut() {
   222	                    if let MergeOutcome::Merged(new_shape) =
   223	                        merge(&existing_shape.clone(), &shape, existing_lifecycle, &lifecycle)
   224	                    {
   225	                        *existing_shape = new_shape;
   226	                        existing_ids.push(interest_id.clone());
   227	                        merged = true;
   228	                        break;
   229	                    }
   230	                }
   231	                if !merged {
   232	                    sub_shapes.push((shape, lifecycle, vec![interest_id]));
   233	                }
   234	            }
   235	
   236	            let relay_sub_shapes: Vec<SubShape> = sub_shapes
   237	                .into_iter()
   238	                .map(|(shape, _lifecycle, ids)| {
   239	                    let hash = simple_shape_hash(&shape);
   240	                    SubShape {
   241	                        shape,
   242	                        originating_interests: ids,
   243	                        canonical_filter_hash: hash,
   244	                    }
   245	                })
   246	                .collect();
   247	
   248	            per_relay.insert(
   249	                relay_url.clone(),
   250	                RelayPlan {
   251	                    relay_url,
   252	                    role_tags,
   253	                    sub_shapes: relay_sub_shapes,
   254	                },
   255	            );
   256	        }
   257	
   258	        // ── Stage 4: Plan-id binding ──────────────────────────────────────────
   259	        let plan_id = compute_plan_id(interests, self.mailbox_cache, MERGE_LATTICE_VERSION);
   260
