exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba crates/nmp-core/src/planner/lattice.rs | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
 succeeded in 0ms:
   260	
   261	        Ok(CompiledPlan { plan_id, per_relay })
   262	    }
   263	
   264	    /// Stage 1 + 2: partition one logical interest into per-relay entries.
   265	    ///
   266	    /// Each entry carries only the AUTHORS that declared the specific relay,
   267	    /// preserving the per-relay author-subset semantics required by the audit
   268	    /// test (Assertion 2) and the design spec §3.3.
   269	    fn partition_interest(
   270	        &self,
   271	        interest: &LogicalInterest,
   272	        relay_entries: &mut BTreeMap<RelayUrl, Vec<RelayEntry>>,
   273	    ) {
   274	        // Base shape: everything except authors and addresses (will be filled per relay).
   275	        let base_shape = InterestShape {
   276	            authors: BTreeSet::new(),
   277	            kinds: interest.shape.kinds.clone(),
   278	            tags: interest.shape.tags.clone(),
   279	            since: interest.shape.since,
   280	            until: interest.shape.until,
   281	            limit: interest.shape.limit,
   282	            event_ids: interest.shape.event_ids.clone(),
   283	            addresses: BTreeSet::new(),
   284	        };
   285	
   286	        // Case A: interest has explicit authors — partition them.
   287	        if !interest.shape.authors.is_empty() {
   288	            // relay_url → (author_set, source)
   289	            let mut per_relay_authors: BTreeMap<RelayUrl, (BTreeSet<Pubkey>, RoutingSource)> =
   290	                BTreeMap::new();
   291	
   292	            for author in &interest.shape.authors {
   293	                match self.mailbox_cache.get(author) {
   294	                    Some(snapshot) => {
   295	                        for relay in snapshot.outbox_relays() {
   296	                            let entry =
   297	                                per_relay_authors.entry(relay.clone()).or_insert_with(|| {
   298	                                    (BTreeSet::new(), RoutingSource::Nip65)
   299	                                });
   300	                            entry.0.insert(author.clone());
   301	                        }
   302	                    }
   303	                    None => {
   304	                        // Indexer fallback (Stage 2)
   305	                        for relay in self.indexer_relays {
   306	                            let entry =
   307	                                per_relay_authors.entry(relay.clone()).or_insert_with(|| {
   308	                                    (BTreeSet::new(), RoutingSource::Indexer)
   309	                                });
   310	                            entry.0.insert(author.clone());
   311	                        }
   312	                    }
   313	                }
   314	            }
   315	
   316	            for (relay_url, (authors, source)) in per_relay_authors {
   317	                relay_entries.entry(relay_url).or_default().push(RelayEntry {
   318	                    base_shape: base_shape.clone(),
   319	                    authors_for_relay: authors,
   320	                    addresses_for_relay: BTreeSet::new(),
   321	                    lifecycle: interest.lifecycle.clone(),
   322	                    source,
   323	                    interest_id: interest.id.clone(),
   324	                });
   325	            }
   326	            return;
   327	        }
   328	
   329	        // Case B: no explicit authors, but address-pointer pubkeys → Outbox.
   330	        if !interest.shape.addresses.is_empty() {
   331	            // relay_url → (coord_set, source)
   332	            let mut per_relay_addrs: BTreeMap<RelayUrl, (BTreeSet<NaddrCoord>, RoutingSource)> =
   333	                BTreeMap::new();
   334	
   335	            for coord in &interest.shape.addresses {
   336	                match self.mailbox_cache.get(&coord.pubkey) {
   337	                    Some(snapshot) => {
   338	                        for relay in snapshot.outbox_relays() {
   339	                            let entry =
   340	                                per_relay_addrs.entry(relay.clone()).or_insert_with(|| {
   341	                                    (BTreeSet::new(), RoutingSource::Nip65)
   342	                                });
   343	                            entry.0.insert(coord.clone());
   344	                        }
   345	                    }
   346	                    None => {
   347	                        for relay in self.indexer_relays {
   348	                            let entry =
   349	                                per_relay_addrs.entry(relay.clone()).or_insert_with(|| {
   350	                                    (BTreeSet::new(), RoutingSource::Indexer)
   351	                                });
   352	                            entry.0.insert(coord.clone());
   353	                        }
   354	                    }
   355	                }
   356	            }
   357	
   358	            for (relay_url, (addrs, source)) in per_relay_addrs {
   359	                relay_entries.entry(relay_url).or_default().push(RelayEntry {
   360	                    base_shape: base_shape.clone(),
   361	                    authors_for_relay: BTreeSet::new(),
   362	                    addresses_for_relay: addrs,
   363	                    lifecycle: interest.lifecycle.clone(),
   364	                    source,
   365	                    interest_id: interest.id.clone(),
   366	                });
   367	            }
   368	            return;
   369	        }
   370	
   371	        // Case C: no author, no addresses — route to indexer set (e.g. hashtag firehose).
   372	        for relay in self.indexer_relays {
   373	            relay_entries.entry(relay.clone()).or_default().push(RelayEntry {
   374	                base_shape: base_shape.clone(),
   375	                authors_for_relay: BTreeSet::new(),
   376	                addresses_for_relay: BTreeSet::new(),
   377	                lifecycle: interest.lifecycle.clone(),
   378	                source: RoutingSource::Indexer,
   379	                interest_id: interest.id.clone(),
   380	            });
   381	        }
   382	    }
   383	}
   384	
   385	// ─── Plan-id hashing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   386	
   387	/// Compute a stable, deterministic plan-id string.
   388	///
   389	/// The hash covers: sorted interest ids + shapes + scopes + lifecycles,
   390	/// the mailbox snapshot sorted by pubkey, and the lattice version.
   391	///
   392	/// Phase 1: uses a simple FNV-1a accumulation. Phase 2 will upgrade to
   393	/// blake3 when that crate is in the workspace.
   394	fn compute_plan_id(
   395	    interests: &[LogicalInterest],
   396	    cache: &dyn MailboxCache,
   397	    lattice_version: u8,
   398	) -> String {
   399	    struct FnvHasher(u64);
   400	    impl FnvHasher {
   401	        fn new() -> Self {
   402	            Self(0xcbf29ce484222325)
   403	        }
   404	        fn feed_bytes(&mut self, bytes: &[u8]) {
   405	            for &b in bytes {
   406	                self.0 ^= u64::from(b);
   407	                self.0 = self.0.wrapping_mul(0x100000001b3);
   408	            }
   409	        }
   410	        fn finish(self) -> u64 {
   411	            self.0
   412	        }
   413	    }
   414	
   415	    let mut h = FnvHasher::new();
   416	
   417	    // Sorted interest contributions
   418	    let mut sorted_interests: Vec<&LogicalInterest> = interests.iter().collect();
   419	    sorted_interests.sort_by_key(|i| &i.id);
   420	    for interest in sorted_interests {
   421	        // Feed id
   422	        h.feed_bytes(&interest.id.0.to_le_bytes());
   423	        // Feed shape via serde_json (deterministic because BTreeSet/BTreeMap)
   424	        if let Ok(shape_json) = serde_json::to_vec(&interest.shape) {
   425	            h.feed_bytes(&shape_json);
   426	        }
   427	        // Feed lifecycle tag byte
   428	        let lifecycle_tag: u8 = match &interest.lifecycle {
   429	            InterestLifecycle::Tailing => 0,
   430	            InterestLifecycle::OneShot => 1,
   431	            InterestLifecycle::BoundedTime { until_ms } => {
   432	                h.feed_bytes(&until_ms.to_le_bytes());
   433	                2
   434	            }
   435	        };
   436	        h.feed_bytes(&[lifecycle_tag]);
   437	    }
   438	
   439	    // Sorted mailbox snapshot
   440	    let mut snapshot = cache.snapshot_all();
   441	    snapshot.sort_by(|(a, _), (b, _)| a.cmp(b));
   442	    for (pk, mb) in snapshot {
   443	        h.feed_bytes(pk.as_bytes());
   444	        for r in &mb.write_relays {
   445	            h.feed_bytes(r.as_bytes());
   446	        }
   447	        for r in &mb.read_relays {
   448	            h.feed_bytes(r.as_bytes());
   449	        }
   450	        for r in &mb.both_relays {
   451	            h.feed_bytes(r.as_bytes());
   452	        }
   453	    }
   454	
   455	    // Lattice version
   456	    h.feed_bytes(&[lattice_version]);
   457	
   458	    format!("{:016x}", h.finish())
   459	}
   460	
   461	// ─── Canonical filter hash ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   462	
   463	fn simple_shape_hash(shape: &InterestShape) -> String {
   464	    use std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher;
   465	    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};
   466	
   467	    let mut h = DefaultHasher::new();
   468	    if let Ok(json) = serde_json::to_string(shape) {
   469	        json.hash(&mut h);
   470	    }
   471	    format!("{:08x}", h.finish() & 0xffff_ffff)
   472	}

     1	//! The filter-merge lattice: `merge()` implements Rules 1–8 from the compiler
     2	//! design. Only shapes that pass all eight rules are merged; otherwise the
     3	//! caller emits two distinct REQs.
     4	//!
     5	//! Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3.3
     6	//! Doctrine: D8 (zero per-event allocs on the hot path after warmup).
     7	//!
     8	//! ## Rules summary
     9	//! 1. `kinds` — equal or one wildcard; wildcard absorbs.
    10	//! 2. `tags` — same key dimensions; per-dimension value union ≤ limit.
    11	//! 3. `since` — `min(a, b)` iff both present or both absent; mixed = refuse.
    12	//! 4. `until` — `max(a, b)` iff both present or both absent; mixed = refuse.
    13	//! 5. `limit` — merge only if both absent.
    14	//! 6. `lifecycle` — identical lifecycles only.
    15	//! 7. `event_ids` — union, capped.
    16	//! 8. `addresses` — union, capped; requires other fields mergeable per 1–7.
    17	
    18	use super::interest::{InterestLifecycle, InterestShape};
    19	
    20	/// Per-relay cap for merged value sets (tags, ids, addresses).
    21	/// This mirrors the relay default of 1000 per filter.
    22	const DEFAULT_VALUE_LIMIT: usize = 1000;
    23	
    24	/// Outcome of attempting to merge two `InterestShape`s on a single relay.
    25	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
    26	pub enum MergeOutcome {
    27	    /// Shapes were merged; the returned shape is the result.
    28	    Merged(InterestShape),
    29	    /// Shapes cannot be merged without changing semantics.
    30	    Refused,
    31	}
    32	
    33	/// Attempt to merge shape `b` into shape `a` on a given relay.
    34	///
    35	/// Returns `Merged(result)` iff all 8 rules pass; `Refused` otherwise.
    36	/// Neither `a` nor `b` is modified on refusal.
    37	///
    38	/// Design: §3.3 Rules 1–8
    39	pub fn merge(a: &InterestShape, b: &InterestShape, lifecycle_a: &InterestLifecycle, lifecycle_b: &InterestLifecycle) -> MergeOutcome {
    40	    // Rule 6 first — cheapest check, prune early.
    41	    if !rule6_lifecycle(lifecycle_a, lifecycle_b) {
    42	        return MergeOutcome::Refused;
    43	    }
    44	
    45	    // Rule 1 — kinds
    46	    let merged_kinds = match rule1_kinds(a, b) {
    47	        Some(k) => k,
    48	        None => return MergeOutcome::Refused,
    49	    };
    50	
    51	    // Rule 2 — tag dimensions
    52	    let merged_tags = match rule2_tags(a, b, DEFAULT_VALUE_LIMIT) {
    53	        Some(t) => t,
    54	        None => return MergeOutcome::Refused,
    55	    };
    56	
    57	    // Rule 3 — since
    58	    let merged_since = match rule3_since(a, b) {
    59	        Some(s) => s,
    60	        None => return MergeOutcome::Refused,
    61	    };
    62	
    63	    // Rule 4 — until
    64	    let merged_until = match rule4_until(a, b) {
    65	        Some(u) => u,
    66	        None => return MergeOutcome::Refused,
    67	    };
    68	
    69	    // Rule 5 — limit
    70	    if !rule5_limit(a, b) {
    71	        return MergeOutcome::Refused;
    72	    }
    73	
    74	    // Rule 7 — event_ids union
    75	    let merged_event_ids = match rule7_event_ids(a, b, DEFAULT_VALUE_LIMIT) {
    76	        Some(ids) => ids,
    77	        None => return MergeOutcome::Refused,
    78	    };
    79	
    80	    // Rule 8 — addresses union (requires prior rules to have passed)
    81	    let merged_addresses = match rule8_addresses(a, b, DEFAULT_VALUE_LIMIT) {
    82	        Some(addrs) => addrs,
    83	        None => return MergeOutcome::Refused,
    84	    };
    85	
    86	    MergeOutcome::Merged(InterestShape {
    87	        authors: a.authors.union(&b.authors).cloned().collect(),
    88	        kinds: merged_kinds,
    89	        tags: merged_tags,
    90	        since: merged_since,
    91	        until: merged_until,
    92	        limit: None, // Rule 5 guarantees both are None
    93	        event_ids: merged_event_ids,
    94	        addresses: merged_addresses,
    95	    })
    96	}
    97	
    98	// ─── Individual rules ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    99	
   100	/// Rule 1 — `kinds` merge.
   101	///
   102	/// Mergeable iff `a.kinds == b.kinds` OR one is empty (wildcard absorbs).
   103	/// Returns the merged kinds set, or `None` to refuse.
   104	fn rule1_kinds(
   105	    a: &InterestShape,
   106	    b: &InterestShape,
   107	) -> Option<std::collections::BTreeSet<u32>> {
   108	    if a.kinds == b.kinds {
   109	        Some(a.kinds.clone())
   110	    } else if a.kinds.is_empty() {
   111	        // a is wildcard — wildcard absorbs
   112	        Some(b.kinds.clone())
   113	    } else if b.kinds.is_empty() {
   114	        Some(a.kinds.clone())
   115	    } else {
   116	        // Both non-empty but different — refuse (merging would widen kinds)
   117	        None
   118	    }
   119	}
   120	
   121	/// Rule 2 — `tags` merge.
   122	///
   123	/// Mergeable iff both shapes have the same tag key dimensions, AND the union
   124	/// of values per dimension stays under `limit`.
   125	fn rule2_tags(
   126	    a: &InterestShape,
   127	    b: &InterestShape,
   128	    limit: usize,
   129	) -> Option<std::collections::BTreeMap<super::interest::TagKey, std::collections::BTreeSet<String>>> {
   130	    // Keys must be identical (same dimensions)
   131	    if a.tags.keys().ne(b.tags.keys()) {
   132	        return None;
   133	    }
   134	
   135	    let mut merged = std::collections::BTreeMap::new();
   136	    for (key, av) in &a.tags {
   137	        let bv = b.tags.get(key)?; // key must exist in b (already checked above)
   138	        let union: std::collections::BTreeSet<String> = av.union(bv).cloned().collect();
   139	        if union.len() > limit {
   140	            return None;
   141	        }
   142	        merged.insert(key.clone(), union);
   143	    }
   144	    Some(merged)
   145	}
   146	
   147	/// Rule 3 — `since` merge.
   148	///
   149	/// Returns `min(a.since, b.since)` iff both are `Some` or both are `None`.
   150	/// Mixed (one bounded, one unbounded) returns `None` (refuse).
   151	fn rule3_since(a: &InterestShape, b: &InterestShape) -> Option<Option<u64>> {
   152	    match (a.since, b.since) {
   153	        (None, None) => Some(None),
   154	        (Some(sa), Some(sb)) => Some(Some(sa.min(sb))),
   155	        _ => None, // Mixed — refuse
   156	    }
   157	}
   158	
   159	/// Rule 4 — `until` merge.
   160	///
   161	/// Returns `max(a.until, b.until)` iff both are `Some` or both are `None`.
   162	/// Mixed returns `None` (refuse).
   163	fn rule4_until(a: &InterestShape, b: &InterestShape) -> Option<Option<u64>> {
   164	    match (a.until, b.until) {
   165	        (None, None) => Some(None),
   166	        (Some(ua), Some(ub)) => Some(Some(ua.max(ub))),
   167	        _ => None, // Mixed — refuse
   168	    }
   169	}
   170	
   171	/// Rule 5 — `limit` merge.
   172	///
   173	/// Mergeable iff both limits are absent. If either has a limit, refuse
   174	/// (broadening would mask the limit's intent).
   175	fn rule5_limit(a: &InterestShape, b: &InterestShape) -> bool {
   176	    a.limit.is_none() && b.limit.is_none()
   177	}
   178	
   179	/// Rule 6 — `lifecycle` merge.
   180	///
   181	/// Tailing and one-shot must not merge (one-shot would never close the tailing
   182	/// subscription). Both lifecycles must be identical.
   183	fn rule6_lifecycle(a: &InterestLifecycle, b: &InterestLifecycle) -> bool {
   184	    a == b
   185	}
   186	
   187	/// Rule 7 — `event_ids` merge by union.
   188	///
   189	/// Returns `None` if the union would exceed `limit`.
   190	fn rule7_event_ids(
   191	    a: &InterestShape,
   192	    b: &InterestShape,
   193	    limit: usize,
   194	) -> Option<std::collections::BTreeSet<super::interest::EventId>> {
   195	    let union: std::collections::BTreeSet<_> = a.event_ids.union(&b.event_ids).cloned().collect();
   196	    if union.len() > limit {
   197	        None
   198	    } else {
   199	        Some(union)
   200	    }
   201	}
   202	
   203	/// Rule 8 — `addresses` merge by union.
   204	///
   205	/// Merges the address-pointer sets. Returns `None` if the union exceeds `limit`.
   206	/// The other constraints (authors, kinds, time, lifecycle) must have been
   207	/// checked by Rules 1–7 before reaching this point — the method does not
   208	/// re-check them.
   209	fn rule8_addresses(
   210	    a: &InterestShape,
   211	    b: &InterestShape,
   212	    limit: usize,
   213	) -> Option<std::collections::BTreeSet<super::interest::NaddrCoord>> {
   214	    let union: std::collections::BTreeSet<_> =
   215	        a.addresses.union(&b.addresses).cloned().collect();
   216	    if union.len() > limit {
   217	        None
   218	    } else {
   219	        Some(union)
   220	    }
   221	}
   222	
   223	// ─── Tests ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   224	
   225	#[cfg(test)]
   226	mod tests {
   227	    use super::*;
   228	    use crate::planner::interest::{InterestLifecycle, InterestShape, NaddrCoord};
   229	    use std::collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet};
   230	
   231	    fn tailing() -> InterestLifecycle {
   232	        InterestLifecycle::Tailing
   233	    }
   234	    fn one_shot() -> InterestLifecycle {
   235	        InterestLifecycle::OneShot
   236	    }
   237	
   238	    fn shape_with_kinds(kinds: &[u32]) -> InterestShape {
   239	        InterestShape {
   240	            kinds: kinds.iter().copied().collect(),
   241	            ..Default::default()
   242	        }
   243	    }
   244	
   245	    // ── Rule 1 — kinds ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   246	
   247	    #[test]
   248	    fn rule1_equal_kinds_merge() {
   249	        let a = shape_with_kinds(&[1, 6]);
   250	        let b = shape_with_kinds(&[1, 6]);
   251	        let r = merge(&a, &b, &tailing(), &tailing());
   252	        assert!(matches!(r, MergeOutcome::Merged(ref s) if s.kinds == a.kinds));
   253	    }
   254	
   255	    #[test]
   256	    fn rule1_different_kinds_refuse() {
   257	        let a = shape_with_kinds(&[1]);
   258	        let b = shape_with_kinds(&[6]);
   259	        assert_eq!(merge(&a, &b, &tailing(), &tailing()), MergeOutcome::Refused);
   260	    }

   261	
   262	    #[test]
   263	    fn rule1_wildcard_absorbs_specific() {

   271	    // ── Rule 2 — tags ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

   274	    fn rule2_same_tag_dimensions_merge() {

   343	    // ── Rule 5 — limit ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

   363	    // ── Rule 6 — lifecycle ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
   364	
   365	    #[test]
   366	    fn rule6_identical_lifecycle_merge() {
   367	        let a = InterestShape { kinds: [1].into_iter().collect(), ..Default::default() };
   368	        let b = InterestShape { kinds: [1].into_iter().collect(), ..Default::default() };
   369	        assert!(matches!(merge(&a, &b, &tailing(), &tailing()), MergeOutcome::Merged(_)));
   370	        assert!(matches!(merge(&a, &b, &one_shot(), &one_shot()), MergeOutcome::Merged(_)));
   371	    }
   372	
   373	    #[test]
   374	    fn rule6_mixed_lifecycle_refuse() {
   375	        let a = InterestShape { kinds: [1].into_iter().collect(), ..Default::default() };
   376	        let b = InterestShape { kinds: [1].into_iter().collect(), ..Default::default() };
   377	        assert_eq!(merge(&a, &b, &tailing(), &one_shot()), MergeOutcome::Refused);
   378	    }
   379	
   380	    // ── Rule 7 — event_ids ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
   381	
   382	    #[test]
   383	    fn rule7_event_ids_union() {
   384	        let a = InterestShape {
   385	            event_ids: ["aaa".to_string()].into_iter().collect(),
   386	            ..Default::default()
   387	        };
   388	        let b = InterestShape {
   389	            event_ids: ["bbb".to_string()].into_iter().collect(),
   390	            ..Default::default()
   391	        };
   392	        let r = merge(&a, &b, &one_shot(), &one_shot());
   393	        if let MergeOutcome::Merged(s) = r {
   394	            assert!(s.event_ids.contains("aaa"));
   395	            assert!(s.event_ids.contains("bbb"));
   396	        } else {
   397	            panic!("expected Merged");
   398	        }
   399	    }
   400	
   401	    #[test]
   402	    fn rule7_event_ids_cap_refuse() {
   403	        // Build two sets whose union exceeds DEFAULT_VALUE_LIMIT (1000)
   404	        let ids_a: BTreeSet<String> = (0u32..600).map(|i| format!("{i:064x}")).collect();
   405	        let ids_b: BTreeSet<String> = (500u32..1100).map(|i| format!("{i:064x}")).collect();
   406	        let a = InterestShape { event_ids: ids_a, ..Default::default() };
   407	        let b = InterestShape { event_ids: ids_b, ..Default::default() };
   408	        assert_eq!(merge(&a, &b, &one_shot(), &one_shot()), MergeOutcome::Refused);
   409	    }
   410	
   411	    // ── Rule 8 — addresses ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
   412	
   413	    #[test]
   414	    fn rule8_address_union_merges() {
   415	        let coord_a = NaddrCoord {
   416	            pubkey: "a".repeat(64),
   417	            kind: 30023,
   418	            d_tag: "post-a".to_string(),
   419	        };
   420	        let coord_b = NaddrCoord {
   421	            pubkey: "b".repeat(64),
   422	            kind: 30023,
   423	            d_tag: "post-b".to_string(),
   424	        };
   425	        let a = InterestShape {
   426	            kinds: [30023].into_iter().collect(),
   427	            addresses: [coord_a.clone()].into_iter().collect(),
   428	            ..Default::default()
   429	        };
   430	        let b = InterestShape {
   431	            kinds: [30023].into_iter().collect(),
   432	            addresses: [coord_b.clone()].into_iter().collect(),
   433	            ..Default::default()
   434	        };
   435	        let r = merge(&a, &b, &one_shot(), &one_shot());
   436	        if let MergeOutcome::Merged(s) = r {
   437	            assert!(s.addresses.contains(&coord_a));
   438	            assert!(s.addresses.contains(&coord_b));
   439	        } else {
   440	            panic!("expected Merged");
   441	        }
   442	    }
   443	
   444	    #[test]
   445	    fn rule8_address_dedup_identical_coord() {
   446	        // Two interests for the exact same NaddrCoord should merge into one.
   447	        let coord = NaddrCoord {
   448	            pubkey: "a".repeat(64),
   449	            kind: 30023,
   450	            d_tag: "my-post".to_string(),
   451	        };
   452	        let a = InterestShape {
   453	            kinds: [30023].into_iter().collect(),
   454	            addresses: [coord.clone()].into_iter().collect(),
   455	            ..Default::default()
   456	        };
   457	        let b = a.clone();
   458	        let r = merge(&a, &b, &one_shot(), &one_shot());
   459	        if let MergeOutcome::Merged(s) = r {
   460	            // BTreeSet deduplicates; should still be one coord.
   461	            assert_eq!(s.addresses.len(), 1);
   462	        } else {
   463	            panic!("expected Merged");
   464	        }
   465	    }
   466	
   467	    #[test]
   468	    fn rule8_addresses_respect_other_rules() {
   469	        // If lifecycle differs, Rule 6 fires first — addresses are irrelevant.
   470	        let coord = NaddrCoord {
   471	            pubkey: "a".repeat(64),
   472	            kind: 30023,
   473	            d_tag: "post".to_string(),
   474	        };
   475	        let a = InterestShape {
   476	            kinds: [30023].into_iter().collect(),
   477	            addresses: [coord.clone()].into_iter().collect(),
   478	            ..Default::default()
   479	        };
   480	        let b = a.clone();
   481	        assert_eq!(merge(&a, &b, &tailing(), &one_shot()), MergeOutcome::Refused);
   482	    }
   483	}

     1	//! `LogicalInterest`, `InterestShape`, and `NaddrCoord` types.
     2	//!
     3	//! A logical interest is what a kernel-side consumer (view, action, monitor,
     4	//! sync job, or pointer loader) wants alive on the wire. The compiler in
     5	//! `planner::compiler` turns N logical interests into M ≤ N per-relay plans.
     6	//!
     7	//! Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md` §2.1
     8	//! Doctrine: D3 (outbox routing automatic), D6 (errors are internal Results),
     9	//!           D8 (composite reverse index, zero per-event allocs after warmup).
    10	
    11	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
    12	use std::collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet};
    13	
    14	// ─── Type aliases (lightweight; no nostr-sdk dep) ────────────────────────────
    15	
    16	/// Hex-encoded 64-char pubkey.
    17	pub type Pubkey = String;
    18	
    19	/// Hex-encoded 64-char event id.
    20	pub type EventId = String;
    21	
    22	/// A `wss://` URL for a relay.
    23	pub type RelayUrl = String;
    24	
    25	/// Unix timestamp in seconds.
    26	pub type UnixSeconds = u64;
    27	
    28	/// A Nostr tag key (e.g. "e", "p", "t", "a").
    29	pub type TagKey = String;
    30	
    31	// ─── InterestId ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32	
    33	/// Stable identity assigned by the planner registry on first insertion.
    34	/// Two interests with identical content get distinct ids if registered by
    35	/// distinct claims (the registry is the authority, not content hashing).
    36	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Ord, PartialOrd, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    37	pub struct InterestId(pub u64);
    38	
    39	// ─── NaddrCoord ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    40	
    41	/// A parameterized-replaceable event coordinate: the triple that uniquely
    42	/// identifies an addressable event (kinds 10000–19999, 30000–39999) across
    43	/// all relays. Equivalent to the `naddr` bech32 encoding without the relay hint.
    44	///
    45	/// Used by `InterestShape::addresses` for address-pointer hydration (Rule 8
    46	/// of the merge lattice) and by the D8 composite reverse index to deduplicate
    47	/// address-pointer interests across views.
    48	///
    49	/// Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md` §2.1
    50	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Ord, PartialOrd, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    51	pub struct NaddrCoord {
    52	    /// Author of the addressed event.
    53	    pub pubkey: Pubkey,
    54	    /// Addressable kind (10000–19999 or 30000–39999).
    55	    pub kind: u32,
    56	    /// The `d` tag value; empty string for events with no `d` tag.
    57	    pub d_tag: String,
    58	}
    59	
    60	// ─── InterestShape ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    61	
    62	/// The normalised filter description for a `LogicalInterest`.
    63	///
    64	/// Mirrors the Nostr filter shape closely. All collections use sorted-container
    65	/// types so equality and hashing are deterministic — required for plan-id
    66	/// stability across recompilations (§3.4 plan-id contract).
    67	///
    68	/// Empty collections mean "wildcard" except where noted.
    69	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    70	pub struct InterestShape {
    71	    /// Authors whose events are wanted. Empty = any author (rare; prefer scoped).
    72	    pub authors: BTreeSet<Pubkey>,
    73	
    74	    /// Event kinds wanted. Empty = any kind (rare).
    75	    pub kinds: BTreeSet<u32>,
    76	
    77	    /// Tag filter dimensions. Each entry is a tag key → sorted set of values.
    78	    /// Sorted for hash stability (D8 composite index invariant).
    79	    pub tags: BTreeMap<TagKey, BTreeSet<String>>,
    80	
    81	    /// Lower bound for `created_at`. `None` = no lower bound.
    82	    pub since: Option<UnixSeconds>,
    83	
    84	    /// Upper bound for `created_at`. `None` = no upper bound.
    85	    pub until: Option<UnixSeconds>,
    86	
    87	    /// Maximum events to return. `None` = relay default.
    88	    /// When set, merge is refused (broadening would mask intent). See Rule 5.
    89	    pub limit: Option<u32>,
    90	
    91	    /// Specific event ids for pointer / thread hydration.
    92	    pub event_ids: BTreeSet<EventId>,
    93	
    94	    /// Parameterized-replaceable event coordinates for address-pointer hydration.
    95	    ///
    96	    /// Non-empty when a view needs to resolve a specific `naddr` (e.g., a NIP-23
    97	    /// article in `ThreadViewModule` or `MetaTimelineViewModule`). The compiler
    98	    /// routes each coordinate to the addressed author's write relays (Stage 1
    99	    /// Outbox direction keyed on `NaddrCoord::pubkey`). See Rule 8 and §7 of
   100	    /// the design doc.
   101	    ///
   102	    /// Adding `addresses` as a first-class field gives the merge lattice a stable
   103	    /// key to union on, rather than encoding coords into opaque `#a` tag strings.
   104	    ///
   105	    /// Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md` §2.1 (T24).
   106	    pub addresses: BTreeSet<NaddrCoord>,
   107	}
   108	
   109	impl InterestShape {
   110	    /// Convenience constructor for a tailing author+kind timeline interest.
   111	    pub fn timeline_for(authors: BTreeSet<Pubkey>) -> Self {
   112	        Self {
   113	            authors,
   114	            kinds: [1u32, 6u32].into_iter().collect(),
   115	            ..Default::default()
   116	        }
   117	    }
   118	
   119	    /// Convenience constructor for a one-shot profile fetch.
   120	    pub fn profile_for(pubkey: Pubkey) -> Self {
   121	        Self {
   122	            authors: [pubkey].into_iter().collect(),
   123	            kinds: [0u32].into_iter().collect(),
   124	            limit: Some(1),
   125	            ..Default::default()
   126	        }
   127	    }
   128	}
   129	
   130	// ─── InterestLifecycle ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   131	
   132	/// Controls when the compiler's wire-emitter closes the REQ.
   133	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   134	pub enum InterestLifecycle {
   135	    /// Stay open after EOSE (tailing subscription).
   136	    Tailing,
   137	    /// Send CLOSE on EOSE.
   138	    OneShot,
   139	    /// Send CLOSE on EOSE or when the deadline (Unix ms) passes.
   140	    BoundedTime { until_ms: u64 },
   141	}
   142	
   143	// ─── InterestScope ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   144	
   145	/// Determines which account context the compiler uses for mailbox resolution.
   146	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   147	pub enum InterestScope {
   148	    /// Bound to the active account in `SessionState`. Re-routes on account switch.
   149	    ActiveAccount,
   150	    /// Bound to a specific account. Re-routes on that account's mailbox refresh.
   151	    Account(String),
   152	    /// No account context. Used for global pointer loaders and indexer probes.
   153	    Global,
   154	}
   155	
   156	// ─── RelayHint ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   157	
   158	/// A routing hint the consumer wants honoured.
   159	/// The compiler may ignore hints that conflict with policy (e.g. privacy).
   160	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   161	pub struct RelayHint {
   162	    /// The relay URL suggested as a hint source.
   163	    pub url: RelayUrl,
   164	    /// Why this hint was provided.
   165	    pub source: HintSource,
   166	}
   167	
   168	/// Origin of a relay hint.
   169	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   170	pub enum HintSource {
   171	    /// Encoded in an event tag (e.g., `e`-tag position 2).
   172	    EventTag {
   173	        event_id: EventId,
   174	        tag: TagKey,
   175	        position: u8,
   176	    },
   177	    /// Declared by the user in app config.
   178	    UserConfigured,
   179	    /// Observed as the provenance relay for a prior event.
   180	    Provenance { event_id: EventId },
   181	}
   182	
   183	// ─── LogicalInterest ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   184	
   185	/// A logical interest is the actor-internal, semantics-preserving description
   186	/// of what a view, action, or monitor wants the kernel to keep alive on the
   187	/// wire. It is the input to compilation; it is *not* a Nostr filter.
   188	///
   189	/// Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md` §2
   190	/// Doctrine: D3 (outbox routing), D6 (planner errors never cross FFI),
   191	///           D8 (zero per-event allocs after warmup).
   192	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   193	pub struct LogicalInterest {
   194	    /// Stable identity assigned by the registry. Survives recompilation.
   195	    pub id: InterestId,
   196	
   197	    /// Account-scope context for mailbox resolution.
   198	    pub scope: InterestScope,
   199	
   200	    /// What the consumer wants (normalised, deterministically hashable).
   201	    pub shape: InterestShape,
   202	
   203	    /// Optional routing hints (may be ignored by policy).
   204	    pub hints: Vec<RelayHint>,
   205	
   206	    /// Lifecycle: when to close the resulting REQ.
   207	    pub lifecycle: InterestLifecycle,
   208	}
   209	
   210	impl Default for LogicalInterest {
   211	    fn default() -> Self {
   212	        Self {
   213	            id: InterestId(0),
   214	            scope: InterestScope::Global,
   215	            shape: InterestShape::default(),
   216	            hints: Vec::new(),
   217	            lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::OneShot,
   218	        }
   219	    }
   220	}

     1	//! `CompiledPlan`, `RelayPlan`, `SubShape`, and `RoutingSource` — the output
     2	//! types produced by the subscription compiler.
     3	//!
     4	//! Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3.3–§3.4
     5	//! Doctrine: D6 (planner errors are internal Results, never cross FFI).
     6	
     7	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     8	use std::collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet};
     9	
    10	use super::interest::{InterestId, InterestShape, RelayUrl};
    11	
    12	// ─── RoutingSource ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    13	
    14	/// Why a relay was included in the plan.
    15	///
    16	/// A relay may appear for multiple reasons simultaneously (e.g., both NIP-65
    17	/// and user-configured). `RelayPlan::role_tags` is a `BTreeSet<RoutingSource>`
    18	/// preserving all reasons — the four-lane diagnostic discipline requires that
    19	/// lanes are never collapsed.
    20	///
    21	/// Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md` §5.2
    22	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Ord, PartialOrd, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    23	pub enum RoutingSource {
    24	    /// Resolved from the author's published kind:10002 relay list.
    25	    Nip65,
    26	    /// Resolved from a user-configured relay set.
    27	    UserConfigured,
    28	    /// Resolved from indexer fallback (no mailbox known for the author).
    29	    Indexer,
    30	    /// Resolved from a routing hint embedded in an event tag.
    31	    Hint,
    32	    /// Observed as the provenance relay for a prior event.
    33	    Provenance,
    34	}
    35	
    36	// ─── SubShape ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37	
    38	/// A single merged filter that will be emitted as one wire REQ.
    39	///
    40	/// The wire-emitter renders each `SubShape` as exactly one `["REQ", sub_id, filter]`
    41	/// frame. The `canonical_filter_hash` provides stable identity for ADR-0007
    42	/// `WireSubscriptionStatus` records across re-emissions.
    43	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    44	pub struct SubShape {
    45	    /// The canonical, post-merge filter description.
    46	    pub shape: InterestShape,
    47	    /// All logical interests whose filters were merged into this sub-shape.
    48	    pub originating_interests: Vec<InterestId>,
    49	    /// Blake3 hash of the serialised `shape` for stable wire-subscription identity.
    50	    /// Placeholder: populated by the compiler stage 4. Format: 8 hex chars.
    51	    pub canonical_filter_hash: String,
    52	}
    53	
    54	// ─── RelayPlan ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    55	
    56	/// The per-relay slice of a `CompiledPlan`: which sub-shapes (REQs) go to
    57	/// this relay and why.
    58	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    59	pub struct RelayPlan {
    60	    /// The relay this plan slice targets.
    61	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
    62	    /// Why this relay is in the plan (may contain multiple sources).
    63	    pub role_tags: BTreeSet<RoutingSource>,
    64	    /// Each sub-shape becomes one wire REQ on this relay.
    65	    pub sub_shapes: Vec<SubShape>,
    66	}
    67	
    68	// ─── CompiledPlan ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    69	
    70	/// The output of the subscription compiler: a per-relay mapping of what REQs
    71	/// to emit.
    72	///
    73	/// `plan_id` is the stable identity the platform observes for diagnostic
    74	/// continuity. It is content-addressed over the interest set, mailbox snapshot,
    75	/// and lattice version — so two compiles with no material change produce the
    76	/// same id (idempotency check).
    77	///
    78	/// Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3.4
    79	/// Doctrine: D6 (errors are internal Results), D8 (composite reverse index).
    80	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    81	pub struct CompiledPlan {
    82	    /// Stable, content-addressed plan identity.
    83	    ///
    84	    /// `plan_id = hash(sorted_interests, sorted_mailbox_snapshot, lattice_version)`
    85	    /// (see compiler.md §3.4 for the full hash definition).
    86	    pub plan_id: String,
    87	
    88	    /// Per-relay plans, keyed by relay URL for diffing.
    89	    pub per_relay: BTreeMap<RelayUrl, RelayPlan>,
    90	}
    91	
    92	impl CompiledPlan {
    93	    /// Returns an empty plan with the given id (used by tests and stubs).
    94	    pub fn empty(plan_id: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    95	        Self {
    96	            plan_id: plan_id.into(),
    97	            per_relay: BTreeMap::new(),
    98	        }
    99	    }
   100	}
   101	
   102	// ─── PlannerError ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   103	
   104	/// Internal planner error type.
   105	///
   106	/// Per D6, this type NEVER crosses the FFI boundary. Callers at the actor
   107	/// boundary must map `PlannerError` to an observable state update (e.g. a
   108	/// toast string) before it reaches the FFI surface.
   109	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
   110	pub enum PlannerError {
   111	    /// No interests were registered; nothing to compile.
   112	    EmptyInterestSet,
   113	    /// An interest's shape is internally inconsistent (e.g. `until < since`).
   114	    InvalidShape { reason: String },
   115	    /// Serialisation of the interest set for plan-id hashing failed.
   116	    HashingFailed { reason: String },
   117	}
   118	
   119	impl std::fmt::Display for PlannerError {
   120	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   121	        match self {
   122	            Self::EmptyInterestSet => write!(f, "no interests registered"),
   123	            Self::InvalidShape { reason } => write!(f, "invalid shape: {reason}"),
   124	            Self::HashingFailed { reason } => write!(f, "plan-id hashing failed: {reason}"),
   125	        }
   126	    }
   127	}
   128	
   129	impl std::error::Error for PlannerError {}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! Subscription compiler — the M2 planner subsystem.
     2	//!
     3	//! Turns a set of `LogicalInterest`s into a `CompiledPlan` mapping each
     4	//! relay URL to the exact set of REQ frames to emit.
     5	//!
     6	//! ## Module structure
     7	//!
     8	//! - `interest`  — `LogicalInterest`, `InterestShape`, `NaddrCoord` types.
     9	//! - `lattice`   — `merge()` function implementing the 8 merge rules.
    10	//! - `compiler`  — 4-stage pipeline: resolve → fallback → merge → plan-id.
    11	//! - `plan`      — `CompiledPlan`, `RelayPlan`, `SubShape`, `RoutingSource`.
    12	//!
    13	//! ## Usage (phase 1)
    14	//!
    15	//! ```rust,ignore
    16	//! use nmp_core::planner::{
    17	//!     compiler::{InMemoryMailboxCache, MailboxSnapshot, SubscriptionCompiler},
    18	//!     interest::{InterestId, InterestLifecycle, InterestScope, InterestShape, LogicalInterest},
    19	//! };
    20	//!
    21	//! let cache = InMemoryMailboxCache::new();
    22	//! let indexer = vec!["wss://purplepag.es".to_string()];
    23	//! let compiler = SubscriptionCompiler::new(&cache, &indexer);
    24	//! let plan = compiler.compile(&[interest])?;
    25	//! ```
    26	//!
    27	//! ## Doctrine compliance
    28	//!
    29	//! - **D3** — outbox routing is automatic; view modules never name relay URLs.
    30	//! - **D6** — `PlannerError` is an internal `Result`; it never crosses FFI.
    31	//!   Map to a toast string at the actor boundary.
    32	//! - **D8** — the hot path (merge lattice) uses only stack-allocated comparisons
    33	//!   after the initial interest registration.
    34	//!
    35	//! Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/`
    36	
    37	pub mod compiler;
    38	pub mod interest;
    39	pub mod lattice;
    40	pub mod plan;
    41	
    42	// ─── Convenience re-exports ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    43	
    44	pub use compiler::{
    45	    EmptyMailboxCache, InMemoryMailboxCache, MailboxCache, MailboxSnapshot,
    46	    SubscriptionCompiler,
    47	};
    48	pub use interest::{
    49	    EventId, HintSource, InterestId, InterestLifecycle, InterestScope, InterestShape,
    50	    LogicalInterest, NaddrCoord, Pubkey, RelayHint, RelayUrl, TagKey, UnixSeconds,
    51	};
    52	pub use lattice::{merge, MergeOutcome};
    53	pub use plan::{CompiledPlan, PlannerError, RelayPlan, RoutingSource, SubShape};

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md | sed -n '1,220p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Subscription Compilation §5 + §8 — Four-Lane Diagnostics
     2	
     3	> Parent: `docs/design/subscription-compilation.md`.
     4	> Read first: ADR-0007 (`docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md`) — every record here extends ADR-0007 types; it does not replace them.
     5	
     6	The compiler's routing decisions are the most subtle correctness surface in the M2 milestone. They are also the easiest to silently get wrong (`docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §3, "automatic behaviour also needs strong tests"). Diagnostics make the four sources of relay knowledge legible — separately, never collapsed.
     7	
     8	**Indexer fallback is lane 4 (User-configured), not a fifth lane.** The kernel-configured
     9	indexer set is an operator policy choice expressed as `UserConfiguredCategory::Indexer` (see
    10	§5.1 Lane 4). Keeping it inside lane 4 preserves the four-lane discipline and ensures that
    11	the diagnostic UI always sees exactly four columns, regardless of whether an author is
    12	being served via NIP-65, hints, provenance, or any subcategory of user-configured (including
    13	indexers). This resolves the ambiguity at prior diagnostics.md lines 15, 116, 157.
    14	
    15	## 5.0 The four lanes
    16	
    17	Per `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §4 (lines 39–46) and `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("provenance preserved"), the four relay-fact lanes are:
    18	
    19	1. **NIP-65** — a pubkey's declared relay preferences (kind:10002).
    20	2. **Hint** — relay URLs embedded in events or NIP-19 pointers (`e`/`a` tag third slot, `nevent`'s relay vector, etc.).
    21	3. **Provenance** — relays we have actually observed an event arriving from.
    22	4. **User-configured** — local-policy relays added by the user/operator, plus the kernel-configured indexer fallback set.
    23	
    24	Each lane is its own record stream. They never merge into a single "relays" field — that collapse is exactly the bug `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §4 line 46 forbids. They may be displayed side-by-side in a diagnostic view; the actor stores them apart.
    25	
    26	This is structurally enforced: there is no `Vec<RelayUrl>` field on any compiler output type. Every relay-bearing field carries a `lane: RelayFactLane` discriminator.
    27	
    28	```rust
    29	// crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/diagnostics/lanes.rs (proposed)
    30	
    31	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    32	pub enum RelayFactLane {
    33	    Nip65,
    34	    Hint,
    35	    Provenance,
    36	    UserConfigured,
    37	}
    38	```
    39	
    40	## 5.1 Per-lane record schemas
    41	
    42	Each lane has one record type. All four are exposed to the platform via the existing ADR-0007 `ViewBatch` lane (low-cadence, coalesced to 1–4 Hz per ADR-0007 "How status crosses the bridge"). They feed into the diagnostics screen, not into normal product UI.
    43	
    44	### Lane 1 — `Nip65RelayFact`
    45	
    46	```rust
    47	pub struct Nip65RelayFact {
    48	    pub pubkey: Pubkey,
    49	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
    50	    pub roles: Nip65Roles,                    // read | write | both
    51	    pub kind10002_event_id: EventId,           // provenance of the kind:10002
    52	    pub kind10002_created_at: UnixSeconds,
    53	    pub kind10002_seen_from: Vec<RelayUrl>,    // which relays delivered it
    54	    pub freshness: FreshnessHint,              // recent / hours_old / days_old / never_verified
    55	}
    56	
    57	pub struct Nip65Roles {
    58	    pub read: bool,
    59	    pub write: bool,
    60	}
    61	```
    62	
    63	Emitted whenever `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`) replaces a mailbox entry. One record per `(pubkey, relay_url)` pair; an author with 4 declared relays produces 4 records on each update.
    64	
    65	### Lane 2 — `HintRelayFact`
    66	
    67	```rust
    68	pub struct HintRelayFact {
    69	    /// The pubkey this hint is associated with — the event author for EventTag hints,
    70	    /// the pointer's subject for Nip19 hints. Required for the coverage reducer to
    71	    /// count how many authors are served via hints (see §8.2 by_lane.hint counter).
    72	    pub subject: Pubkey,
    73	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
    74	    pub source: HintSource,
    75	    pub freshness_ms: u64,                     // monotonic from observation
    76	    pub recently_succeeded: bool,              // last attempt produced ≥1 EVENT
    77	}
    78	
    79	pub enum HintSource {
    80	    EventTag    { event_id: EventId, tag: TagKey, position: u8 },
    81	    Nip19       { pointer: String /* nevent1.../naddr1... */ },
    82	    UserConfig  { config_path: String },        // for hints injected via config
    83	}
    84	```
    85	
    86	The `subject` field is the author identity key for the coverage reducer's `by_lane.hint`
    87	counter (§8.2). Without it, the reverse-relay-coverage view cannot answer "how many distinct
    88	authors are routed via hints to relay R?" — the assertion in `tests.md:202` that
    89	`coverage.by_lane.hint == 1` would be untestable.
    90	
    91	Emitted by the pointer loader (post-M2; for M2 the field exists but is rarely populated — only `e`/`a`-tag third-slot hints from thread-view hydration fill it). Per-event hints are de-duplicated; an event whose `e` tag contains a hint URL produces one `HintRelayFact` per (relay_url, source) pair.
    92	
    93	### Lane 3 — `ProvenanceRelayFact`
    94	
    95	```rust
    96	pub struct ProvenanceRelayFact {
    97	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
    98	    pub event_id: EventId,
    99	    pub seen_at_ms: u64,
   100	    pub wire_sub_id: String,                   // which REQ delivered it
   101	    pub kind: u32,
   102	    pub author: Pubkey,
   103	}
   104	```
   105	
   106	Emitted by `handle_event` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:134-164`) for every EVENT arrival. This is the highest-cardinality lane and the only one where coalescing matters at the ADR-0007 boundary: the platform diagnostic view consumes a summarised projection (`ProvenanceSummary` per author or per event), not the raw fact stream.
   107	
   108	### Lane 4 — `UserConfiguredRelayFact`
   109	
   110	```rust
   111	pub struct UserConfiguredRelayFact {
   112	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
   113	    pub category: UserConfiguredCategory,
   114	    pub generation: u64,                       // config version; matches Trigger::*Changed
   115	    pub added_at_ms: u64,
   116	}
   117	
   118	pub enum UserConfiguredCategory {
   119	    AccountRead,                                // user's own read relays (overrides NIP-65 read)
   120	    AccountWrite,                               // user's own write relays
   121	    /// Kernel-configured indexer relay (e.g. purplepag.es). This is the sub-category that
   122	    /// represents indexer fallback routing in diagnostics — NOT a fifth lane. The indexer
   123	    /// set is a policy choice that lives inside lane 4 (User-configured). D3: the operator
   124	    /// configured the indexer set; the kernel applies it as policy for reads when NIP-65
   125	    /// mailboxes are unknown. Never for writes.
   126	    Indexer,
   127	    Debug,                                      // operator-injected for testing
   128	}
   129	```
   130	
   131	Emitted on `Trigger::UserConfiguredRelaysChanged` / `Trigger::IndexerSetChanged`. Low-cardinality, low-cadence.
   132	
   133	## 5.2 What the compiler may *combine*; what stays distinct
   134	
   135	The compiler may *use* facts from multiple lanes to compute a routing decision; it may **not** present them as one. Concretely:
   136	
   137	- **Routing decision** (`AuthorRouting.source` from [compiler.md](compiler.md) §3.1): records *which lane* the relay set was derived from. Single-valued; one of `Nip65 | Hint | UserConfigured`. There is no `Indexer` variant — indexer fallback routing is `UserConfigured` with the relay's `UserConfiguredCategory::Indexer` sub-category visible in the `UserConfiguredRelayFact` record. The author may have facts in three lanes; the compiler picks one, says so, and the other lane records remain visible.
   138	- **`RelayPlan.role_tags`** is a `BTreeSet<RoutingSource>` because a single relay may be in the plan for multiple reasons (e.g. NIP-65 for author A + user-configured fallback for everyone). The set discriminates, it does not collapse.
   139	- **The platform diagnostic view** receives all four lanes as separate `ViewBatch` records. The UI may render them in one screen with four side-by-side columns, but the data path is four lanes.
   140	
   141	A test (§9) asserts that no compiler output type has a field of type `Vec<RelayUrl>` without an adjacent `RelayFactLane`. That is the structural enforcement.
   142	
   143	## 5.3 Lane interactions
   144	
   145	The lanes inform each other through these well-defined hooks:
   146	
   147	- `Provenance → NIP-65 hint refresh.` If we observe many `ProvenanceRelayFact { relay_url: R, author: A }` records but no `Nip65RelayFact { pubkey: A, relay_url: R }`, the operator diagnostic can suggest "we are receiving A's events from R but A has not declared R; their kind:10002 may be stale." This is a future operator-UI affordance, not a behaviour.
   148	- `Hint → planner suggestion.` `HintRelayFact` with `recently_succeeded: true` may surface in the diagnostic view as "you might want to add this to your indexer set." Again, not automatic.
   149	- `User-configured` is the **only** lane the compiler treats as authoritative-by-policy (the user said so). Open question 5 in the parent index resolves the augment-vs-override precedence between NIP-65 and user-configured for the active account.
   150	
   151	The lesson the four-lane discipline preserves: routing is **derivable but contested** evidence. Collapsing the lanes loses information; preserving them lets the diagnostic answer "why did we route this REQ to that relay?" months after the decision.
   152	
   153	---
   154	
   155	# §8 — Reverse-relay-coverage diagnostic view
   156	
   157	> The inverse question. For any relay we are talking to, *whose* timeline does it serve?
   158	
   159	This is one specific `ViewModule` that consumes the four-lane fact streams plus the compiler's `RelayPlan`s and produces a per-relay summary.
   160	
   161	## 8.1 Spec, payload, dependencies
   162	
   163	```rust
   164	pub struct RelayCoverageSpec {
   165	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
   166	}
   167	
   168	pub struct RelayCoveragePayload {
   169	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
   170	    pub serving_authors: u32,
   171	    pub author_examples: Vec<Pubkey>,      // first N (configurable, default 16)
   172	    pub by_lane: ByLaneCounts,
   173	    pub wire_sub_count: u32,
   174	    pub last_event_at_ms: Option<u64>,
   175	    pub provenance_count_last_minute: u32,
   176	}
   177	
   178	pub struct ByLaneCounts {
   179	    pub nip65: u32,             // authors for whom relay is in their NIP-65 set
   180	    pub hint: u32,              // authors for whom we routed here via hints (requires subject: Pubkey on HintRelayFact)
   181	    pub user_configured: u32,   // authors served via any UserConfigured sub-category
   182	    /// Sub-count of `user_configured` where `category == Indexer`. Not a fifth lane —
   183	    /// these are already counted in `user_configured`. Exposed separately so the diagnostic
   184	    /// UI can show "12 via user-config (including 8 indexer fallback)" without a lane split.
   185	    pub indexer_fallback: u32,
   186	}
   187	
   188	// `ViewModule::dependencies` returns:
   189	//   - Mailbox cache updates touching any author in our timeline
   190	//   - RelayPlan updates touching `relay_url`
   191	//   - Provenance facts on `relay_url` (rate-limited; only the count, not individual events)
   192	```
   193	
   194	## 8.2 Implementation outline
   195	
   196	The view's `reduce` consumes **all four** input streams — one per lane. All four must be
   197	wired so the test assertions at `tests.md:202` that check `by_lane.hint` and
   198	`by_lane.user_configured` are actually backed by live data:
   199	
   200	1. `Nip65RelayFact` records — increments/decrements `by_lane.nip65` per (relay_url, pubkey) membership.
   201	2. `HintRelayFact` records — increments/decrements `by_lane.hint` per (relay_url, subject) pair. The `subject: Pubkey` field (§5.1 Lane 2) is the author identity; without it this counter cannot be maintained.
   202	3. `UserConfiguredRelayFact` records — increments/decrements `by_lane.user_configured` per (relay_url) membership. Records with `category == Indexer` contribute to `by_lane.indexer_fallback` (a sub-count of `user_configured`); the UI can show both the aggregate and the Indexer-specific sub-total.
   203	4. `CompiledPlan` re-emissions — every plan recompile produces a `(plan_id, relay_url) → authors` projection this view subscribes to. The compiler exposes this as `RelayAuthorCoverage` in the projection cache.
   204	5. `ProvenanceRelayFact` records — feeds the rolling 60-second counter for `provenance_count_last_minute`.
   205	
   206	This is the M2 exit-gate diagnostic listed in [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md) ("Reverse-relay-coverage view for diagnostics: 'this relay is serving N authors of our timeline.'").
   207	
   208	## 8.3 Cardinality and emission cadence
   209	
   210	One `RelayCoverageSpec`/relay → ≤ N records, where N is the number of relays currently in the planner's union of `RelayPlan`s. For typical Nostr usage that is in the low tens; rendering all of them on one diagnostic screen is fine.
   211	
   212	Emission cadence follows ADR-0007's diagnostic-view rule: material-transition immediately, otherwise 1–4 Hz. The provenance counter ticks every second; the `by_lane` counts only emit on `CompiledPlan` recompiles or new mailbox arrivals.
   213	
   214	## 8.4 Why it lives in diagnostics, not in product UI
   215	
   216	Per `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("the developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does") and ADR-0007's domain-of-diagnostics separation, end-user product UIs do not show "relay X is serving 12 authors." That is operator/debug surface. Normal apps consume the `LogicalInterestStatus` summaries; `RelayCoveragePayload` is for the diagnostics screen (proof iOS app screenshot in `docs/perf/m2/outbox-routing.md` per [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)).

codex
I’ve found a few real mismatches now: plan-id hashing scope, indexer as a fifth routing source, and the audit test’s 300-vs-1000 gap. I’m checking whether those are isolated or if they cascade into compile/test coverage.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! M2 subscription compiler audit gate — phase 1 subset.
     2	//!
     3	//! Exercises the `nmp_core::planner` module: `SubscriptionCompiler`,
     4	//! `LogicalInterest`, `NaddrCoord`, and the merge lattice.
     5	//!
     6	//! Corresponds to the contracts in
     7	//! `docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md` §9.2, Assertions 2 & 5.
     8	//! Assertions 1, 3, 4 depend on infrastructure (AppActionMeta, trigger replay,
     9	//! four-lane diagnostics) that lands in later slices.
    10	//!
    11	//! CI gate: `cargo test -p nmp-testing --test m2_subscription_compilation_audit`
    12	//!
    13	//! Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md`
    14	//! Doctrine: D3 (routing automatic), D6 (errors internal), D8 (zero allocs).
    15	
    16	use nmp_core::planner::{
    17	    compiler::{InMemoryMailboxCache, MailboxSnapshot, SubscriptionCompiler},
    18	    interest::{
    19	        InterestId, InterestLifecycle, InterestScope, InterestShape, LogicalInterest, NaddrCoord,
    20	    },
    21	    plan::RoutingSource,
    22	};
    23	use std::collections::BTreeSet;
    24	
    25	// ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    26	
    27	fn pubkey(seed: &str) -> String {
    28	    // Deterministic 64-char hex pubkey from a short seed string.
    29	    format!("{seed:0>64}")
    30	        .chars()
    31	        .take(64)
    32	        .collect::<String>()
    33	        .to_lowercase()
    34	}
    35	
    36	fn relay(url: &str) -> String {
    37	    url.to_string()
    38	}
    39	
    40	fn make_authors_with_overlapping_mailboxes(
    41	    count: usize,
    42	) -> Vec<(String, MailboxSnapshot)> {
    43	    // Three relay groups with deliberate overlap:
    44	    //   - first 60% of authors → relay.damus.io + nos.lol
    45	    //   - next  30% of authors → nostr.wine     + nos.lol
    46	    //   - last  10% of authors → operator-niche.example
    47	    let mut result = Vec::with_capacity(count);
    48	    for i in 0..count {
    49	        let pk = pubkey(&format!("author{i:06}"));
    50	        let mb = if i < (count * 6 / 10) {
    51	            MailboxSnapshot {
    52	                write_relays: vec![relay("wss://relay.damus.io"), relay("wss://nos.lol")],
    53	                read_relays: vec![],
    54	                both_relays: vec![],
    55	            }
    56	        } else if i < (count * 9 / 10) {
    57	            MailboxSnapshot {
    58	                write_relays: vec![relay("wss://nostr.wine"), relay("wss://nos.lol")],
    59	                read_relays: vec![],
    60	                both_relays: vec![],
    61	            }
    62	        } else {
    63	            MailboxSnapshot {
    64	                write_relays: vec![relay("wss://operator-niche.example")],
    65	                read_relays: vec![],
    66	                both_relays: vec![],
    67	            }
    68	        };
    69	        result.push((pk, mb));
    70	    }
    71	    result
    72	}
    73	
    74	fn interest_id(n: u64) -> InterestId {
    75	    InterestId(n)
    76	}
    77	
    78	// ─── Assertion 2 — per-author wire fan-out ────────────────────────────────────
    79	
    80	/// "For a timeline of N authors, the compiled plan opens REQs only against
    81	/// the union of those authors' write relays (de-duplicated). Each relay carries
    82	/// exactly one merged sub-shape."
    83	///
    84	/// Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md` §9.2 Assertion 2.
    85	#[test]
    86	fn timeline_compiles_to_per_relay_union() {
    87	    let authors = make_authors_with_overlapping_mailboxes(300);
    88	
    89	    let mut cache = InMemoryMailboxCache::new();
    90	    for (pk, mb) in &authors {
    91	        cache.put(pk.clone(), mb.clone());
    92	    }
    93	
    94	    let indexer = vec![relay("wss://purplepag.es")];
    95	    let compiler = SubscriptionCompiler::new(&cache, &indexer);
    96	
    97	    let author_set: BTreeSet<String> = authors.iter().map(|(pk, _)| pk.clone()).collect();
    98	    let interest = LogicalInterest {
    99	        id: interest_id(1),
   100	        scope: InterestScope::ActiveAccount,
   101	        shape: InterestShape {
   102	            authors: author_set.clone(),
   103	            kinds: [1u32, 6u32].into_iter().collect(),
   104	            ..Default::default()
   105	        },
   106	        hints: vec![],
   107	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::Tailing,
   108	    };
   109	
   110	    let plan = compiler.compile(&[interest]).expect("compile");
   111	
   112	    // Assert: relay set == union of declared write relays (no extras, no misses).
   113	    let expected_relays: BTreeSet<String> = authors
   114	        .iter()
   115	        .flat_map(|(_, mb)| mb.write_relays.iter().cloned())
   116	        .collect();
   117	    let actual_relays: BTreeSet<String> = plan.per_relay.keys().cloned().collect();
   118	    assert_eq!(
   119	        actual_relays, expected_relays,
   120	        "compiled relay set must equal the union of declared write relays"
   121	    );
   122	
   123	    // Assert: each relay carries exactly one sub-shape (merge succeeded).
   124	    for (url, rp) in &plan.per_relay {
   125	        assert_eq!(
   126	            rp.sub_shapes.len(),
   127	            1,
   128	            "relay {url} should have one merged sub-shape, got {}",
   129	            rp.sub_shapes.len()
   130	        );
   131	    }
   132	
   133	    // Assert: each relay's authors are exactly the subset that declared it.
   134	    for (url, rp) in &plan.per_relay {
   135	        let expected_authors: BTreeSet<String> = authors
   136	            .iter()
   137	            .filter(|(_, mb)| mb.write_relays.contains(url))
   138	            .map(|(pk, _)| pk.clone())
   139	            .collect();
   140	        let actual_authors = &rp.sub_shapes[0].shape.authors;
   141	        assert_eq!(
   142	            actual_authors, &expected_authors,
   143	            "relay {url} should serve only its declared authors"
   144	        );
   145	    }
   146	
   147	    // Assert: plan-id is stable — two consecutive compiles with no input change.
   148	    let plan2 = compiler.compile(&[LogicalInterest {
   149	        id: interest_id(1),
   150	        scope: InterestScope::ActiveAccount,
   151	        shape: InterestShape {
   152	            authors: author_set,
   153	            kinds: [1u32, 6u32].into_iter().collect(),
   154	            ..Default::default()
   155	        },
   156	        hints: vec![],
   157	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::Tailing,
   158	    }]).expect("compile #2");
   159	    assert_eq!(
   160	        plan.plan_id, plan2.plan_id,
   161	        "re-compile with identical inputs must yield the same plan_id"
   162	    );
   163	}
   164	
   165	// ─── Assertion 5 — address-pointer dedup ─────────────────────────────────────
   166	
   167	/// "Two views registering the same NaddrCoord emit ONE REQ per relay (Rule 8
   168	/// address-pointer union, D8 invariant)."
   169	///
   170	/// Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md` §9.2 Assertion 5.
   171	#[test]
   172	fn address_pointer_dedup_across_two_interests() {
   173	    let article_pk = pubkey("article_author");
   174	
   175	    let mut cache = InMemoryMailboxCache::new();
   176	    cache.put(
   177	        article_pk.clone(),
   178	        MailboxSnapshot {
   179	            write_relays: vec![relay("wss://article-relay.example")],
   180	            read_relays: vec![],

   180	            read_relays: vec![],
   181	            both_relays: vec![],
   182	        },
   183	    );
   184	
   185	    let indexer = vec![relay("wss://purplepag.es")];
   186	    let compiler = SubscriptionCompiler::new(&cache, &indexer);
   187	
   188	    let coord = NaddrCoord {
   189	        pubkey: article_pk,
   190	        kind: 30023,
   191	        d_tag: "my-post".to_string(),
   192	    };
   193	
   194	    // Two interests (ThreadViewModule + MetaTimelineViewModule) for the same coord.
   195	    let make_interest = |id: u64| LogicalInterest {
   196	        id: interest_id(id),
   197	        scope: InterestScope::Global,
   198	        shape: InterestShape {
   199	            addresses: [coord.clone()].into_iter().collect(),
   200	            kinds: [30023u32].into_iter().collect(),
   201	            ..Default::default()
   202	        },
   203	        hints: vec![],
   204	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::OneShot,
   205	    };
   206	
   207	    let plan = compiler
   208	        .compile(&[make_interest(10), make_interest(11)])
   209	        .expect("compile");
   210	
   211	    // Assert: one relay in the plan (the article author's write relay).
   212	    assert_eq!(
   213	        plan.per_relay.len(),
   214	        1,
   215	        "address-pointer interests for the same author route to exactly one relay"
   216	    );
   217	
   218	    // Assert: Rule 8 merged them into one SubShape.
   219	    let relay_plan = plan.per_relay.values().next().unwrap();
   220	    assert_eq!(
   221	        relay_plan.sub_shapes.len(),
   222	        1,
   223	        "Rule 8 must merge identical address sets into one SubShape"
   224	    );
   225	}
   226	
   227	// ─── Fallback to indexer set when no mailbox known ───────────────────────────
   228	
   229	/// Authors with no known mailbox route to the indexer set with source = Indexer.
   230	#[test]
   231	fn unknown_author_falls_back_to_indexer() {
   232	    let cache = InMemoryMailboxCache::new(); // empty
   233	    let indexer = vec![relay("wss://purplepag.es")];
   234	    let compiler = SubscriptionCompiler::new(&cache, &indexer);
   235	
   236	    let interest = LogicalInterest {
   237	        id: interest_id(1),
   238	        scope: InterestScope::Global,
   239	        shape: InterestShape {
   240	            authors: [pubkey("unknown_alice")].into_iter().collect(),
   241	            kinds: [1u32].into_iter().collect(),
   242	            ..Default::default()
   243	        },
   244	        hints: vec![],
   245	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::Tailing,
   246	    };
   247	
   248	    let plan = compiler.compile(&[interest]).expect("compile");
   249	
   250	    // Assert: routes to the indexer.
   251	    assert!(
   252	        plan.per_relay.contains_key("wss://purplepag.es"),
   253	        "unknown author must fall back to indexer relay"
   254	    );
   255	    let rp = &plan.per_relay["wss://purplepag.es"];
   256	    assert!(
   257	        rp.role_tags.contains(&RoutingSource::Indexer),
   258	        "routing source for indexer fallback must be Indexer"
   259	    );
   260	}
   261	
   262	// ─── Plan-id stability under repeated compile ─────────────────────────────────
   263	
   264	/// Compiling the same interests twice without any state change must produce
   265	/// the same plan_id (D8 idempotency).
   266	#[test]
   267	fn plan_id_stable_under_repeated_compile() {
   268	    let mut cache = InMemoryMailboxCache::new();
   269	    cache.put(
   270	        pubkey("alice"),
   271	        MailboxSnapshot {
   272	            write_relays: vec![relay("wss://alice.example")],
   273	            read_relays: vec![],
   274	            both_relays: vec![],
   275	        },
   276	    );
   277	
   278	    let indexer = vec![relay("wss://purplepag.es")];
   279	    let compiler = SubscriptionCompiler::new(&cache, &indexer);
   280	
   281	    let interests = vec![LogicalInterest {
   282	        id: interest_id(42),
   283	        scope: InterestScope::Global,
   284	        shape: InterestShape {
   285	            authors: [pubkey("alice")].into_iter().collect(),
   286	            kinds: [1u32, 6u32].into_iter().collect(),
   287	            ..Default::default()
   288	        },
   289	        hints: vec![],
   290	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::Tailing,
   291	    }];
   292	
   293	    let plan_a = compiler.compile(&interests).expect("compile a");
   294	    let plan_b = compiler.compile(&interests).expect("compile b");
   295	
   296	    assert_eq!(
   297	        plan_a.plan_id, plan_b.plan_id,
   298	        "identical inputs must produce identical plan_id"
   299	    );
   300	}
   301	
   302	// ─── Plan-id changes when interests change ────────────────────────────────────
   303	
   304	/// Adding a new interest must change the plan_id even if no new wire REQ results
   305	/// from the merge.
   306	#[test]
   307	fn plan_id_changes_when_interest_set_changes() {
   308	    let mut cache = InMemoryMailboxCache::new();
   309	    cache.put(
   310	        pubkey("alice"),
   311	        MailboxSnapshot {
   312	            write_relays: vec![relay("wss://alice.example")],
   313	            read_relays: vec![],
   314	            both_relays: vec![],
   315	        },
   316	    );
   317	
   318	    let indexer = vec![relay("wss://purplepag.es")];
   319	    let compiler = SubscriptionCompiler::new(&cache, &indexer);
   320	
   321	    let interest_a = LogicalInterest {
   322	        id: interest_id(1),
   323	        scope: InterestScope::Global,
   324	        shape: InterestShape {
   325	            authors: [pubkey("alice")].into_iter().collect(),
   326	            kinds: [1u32, 6u32].into_iter().collect(),
   327	            ..Default::default()
   328	        },
   329	        hints: vec![],
   330	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::Tailing,
   331	    };
   332	    let interest_b = LogicalInterest {
   333	        id: interest_id(2),
   334	        scope: InterestScope::Global,
   335	        shape: InterestShape {
   336	            authors: [pubkey("alice")].into_iter().collect(),
   337	            kinds: [1u32, 6u32].into_iter().collect(),
   338	            ..Default::default()
   339	        },
   340	        hints: vec![],
   341	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::Tailing,
   342	    };
   343	
   344	    let plan_one = compiler.compile(std::slice::from_ref(&interest_a)).expect("compile one");
   345	    let plan_two = compiler
   346	        .compile(&[interest_a, interest_b])
   347	        .expect("compile two");
   348	
   349	    assert_ne!(
   350	        plan_one.plan_id, plan_two.plan_id,
   351	        "adding an interest must change plan_id even if wire REQs merge"
   352	    );
   353	}

docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:259:The override action's existence is what test #2 in the bug-extinction list ([`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)) asserts: "no public API path lets the developer specify relays for a publish; explicit override action exists and produces a debug warning." docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:92:1. **Mailbox probe.** For every author with `mailbox_cache.get(author) == None`, the compiler emits a `IndexerProbe { author }` side effect on the plan. The probe registers as its own short-lived `LogicalInterest { shape: { kinds: [10002], authors: [author], limit: 1 }, lifecycle: OneShot, scope: Global }`. Recompilation triggers (§4 trigger A1) re-route the original interest once the kind:10002 lands.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:182:The `plan_id` is stored on `CompiledPlan` and rendered into `LogicalInterestStatus` (extending the record at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:147-154` with `plan_id: String, plan_generation: u64`). Tests in §9 assert plan-id stability across no-op recompilations.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:191:| `open_author` (requests.rs:118-140) | Registers three `LogicalInterest`s scoped `ActiveAccount` (kind:10002, kind:0, kinds:1+6 for author); calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::ViewOpen)`. Refcount stays — but it lives on `InterestId` now, not on `ViewInterest { key, refcount }`. The `can_send` gate disappears: the compiler always produces a plan; the wire-emitter is the only thing that may queue deferrals. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:193:| `open_firehose_tag` (requests.rs:170-200) | Registers one `LogicalInterest { shape: { kinds: [1], tags: { #t: [tag] } }, scope: ActiveAccount, lifecycle: Tailing }`. Routes to active-account read relays per §3.1 table. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:194:| `claim_profile` / `release_profile` (requests.rs:202-263) | Registers/unregisters one `LogicalInterest { shape: { authors: [pk], kinds: [0], limit: 1 }, lifecycle: OneShot }` per claim. Refcount of distinct consumers becomes the `InterestId` claim set inside the registry. **Dedup of (pk, kinds=[0]) across N timeline rows yields one merged SubShape and one wire REQ** — this is what bug-extinction "1000 avatars do not produce 1000 REQs" verifies. The refcount must be an integer counter, not a boolean presence flag — Applesauce gotcha `75ef7d5f` (`docs/research/applesauce/gotchas.md` §G2) shows the boolean model causes leaks when two consumers share a claim and one unsubscribes. |
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:153:Atomicity invariant: an action's local event-store commit, side-effect intent, and ledger transition happen as actor messages with one parent action id. The action future runs on the tokio runtime, but all state mutation happens in `handle_message`. There is no public API that lets a developer publish, upload, sign, or issue an NWC request without a renderable action-ledger row.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:342:- `MockRelay` (re-exported from `nostr-relay-builder`).
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:353:`nmp-nse` is a minimal sibling crate with one purpose: decrypt an inbound encrypted event without spawning the full actor. It exposes:
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:373:iOS Notification Service Extension and Android background workers link only this crate. Memory and time budgets (iOS NSE ~24MB / 30s) are observed by design: no relay connections, no full event store load, only the minimal state needed to decrypt and format a preview.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:15:- **External** triggers are `AppAction` variants the platform may dispatch directly. There is exactly one — `AppAction::InvalidateCompile { reason }` — to keep the public surface minimal per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5.

docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:229:Each capability is **idempotent** (`start` after `start` is a no-op) and **bounded** (the trait surface is minimal; no native code decides policy). Capabilities can be added in additional minor versions; doing so does not break existing apps because all setters are optional.
docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:246:`ViewSpec` is an enum of supported view kinds. v1 covers the primitive view protocol plus minimal built-in views for testing: raw filter, event detail, and timeline-like bounded list. The long-term product catalog covers profile, contacts, mailboxes, mutes, blossom-servers, timeline, thread, replies, reactions, conversation-list, conversation, zap-history, wallet-balance, wot-rank, and search. Each maps to a typed payload variant.
