docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:100:- Theming systems: visual identity is the consumer's job. The starter app ships a minimal neutral theme.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:120:| 6. v1 release | Publish primitive kernel crates, minimal examples, migration notes, explicit future extension points | A developer can build a small event viewer without writing cleanup, dedup, replaceable handling, or FFI batching |
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:139:| 15. CLI + starter app + docs | `nmp init`, `nmp doctor`, `nmp gen *`, minimal starter app polish, recipe book, NIP support matrix, generated examples metadata | §3 success criteria reproducible from published docs alone |
docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md:20:- Bug-extinction test #3 (publish to wrong relays): no public API path lets the developer specify relays for a publish; explicit override action exists and produces a debug warning.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:19:The summary diagnosis: **the planner is a string formatter, not a compiler.** Every REQ is a per-call-site decision; routing is one of two literals; recompilation is impossible because nothing is compiled. The diagnostics in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:117-154` already type `RelayStatus` / `WireSubscriptionStatus` / `LogicalInterestStatus` per ADR-0007 — but the planner currently emits at most one `LogicalInterestStatus` per view kind because there is no logical-interest object to scope it against.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:34:pub struct LogicalInterest {
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:132:        -> Vec<LogicalInterest>;
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:140:- `TimelineView { authors: [pablof7z, fiatjaf, jb55, ...follows] }` returns one `LogicalInterest { shape: { authors, kinds: {1, 6}, limit: 200 }, lifecycle: Tailing }`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:163:The seed-bootstrap path (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:50-106`) becomes one `LogicalInterest` per concern, registered at actor `Start` rather than emitted as raw REQs. The compiler produces the wire artifacts.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:216:Per `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("the developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does") and ADR-0007's domain-of-diagnostics separation, end-user product UIs do not show "relay X is serving 12 authors." That is operator/debug surface. Normal apps consume the `LogicalInterestStatus` summaries; `RelayCoveragePayload` is for the diagnostics screen (proof iOS app screenshot in `docs/perf/m2/outbox-routing.md` per [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)).
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:23:The framework treats common Nostr-correctness failures (stale replaceable events, lost subscriptions, mis-routed publishes, double-publication, multi-account desync, leaked secrets across FFI, naive cache invalidation, withheld cached data, blocking-on-fetch UI patterns) as **product defects in the framework** rather than as developer mistakes. The public API is designed so that the wrong thing is hard to type.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:104:Each of these classes must be structurally impossible to introduce through the safe framework public API. Lower-level Rust escape hatches used for tests or internal policy modules must be named, instrumented, and regression-tested. Each bug class below is paired with a regression test in `crates/nmp-testing`.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:180:A kitchen-sink stress-test app, built using the framework, on all four platforms. It is **not** the starter app — the starter stays minimal so newcomers can read it. The proof app exists to validate the framework at scale and to gate v1 release.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:55:The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.

 succeeded in 0ms:
   126	}
   127	```
   128	
   129	The view exists so platform code can render "alice@example uses these relays" using the same path as any other view (`useMailboxes(pubkey)`); it is *not* the compiler's source of truth. The compiler reads `MailboxCache` directly.
   130	
   131	## 6.3 Public surface (compiler-facing, not FFI-facing)
   132	
   133	The `MailboxCache` trait and its associated types live in **`nmp-core::substrate::mailbox`**
   134	to break the dependency cycle (see §6.1 intro). `nmp-nip65` provides the `InMemoryMailboxCache`
   135	implementation; `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes only the trait, never the concrete type.
   136	
   137	```rust
   138	// crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/mailbox.rs  ← trait lives here, not in nmp-nip65
   139	
   140	pub trait MailboxCache: Send + Sync {
   141	    fn get(&self, pubkey: &Pubkey) -> Option<MailboxSnapshot>;
   142	    fn put(&mut self, pubkey: Pubkey, snapshot: MailboxSnapshot)
   143	        -> CachePutResult;
   144	    fn snapshot_all(&self) -> Vec<(Pubkey, MailboxSnapshot)>;
   145	    fn generation(&self) -> u64;           // monotonic; advances on every accepted put
   146	}
   147	
   148	pub enum CachePutResult {
   149	    Inserted,
   150	    ReplacedNewer { prior_created_at: UnixSeconds },
   151	    /// NIP-01 tie-break: equal `created_at` keeps the event with the lexicographically
   152	    /// lower event id, per Applesauce gotcha `90d525af`
   153	    /// (`docs/research/applesauce/gotchas.md` §G1). Two clients producing kind:10002
   154	    /// in the same second must not silently disagree on the stored version.
   155	    RejectedStale { current_created_at: UnixSeconds },
   156	}
   157	
   158	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
   159	pub struct MailboxSnapshot {
   160	    pub kind10002_event_id: EventId,
   161	    pub created_at: UnixSeconds,
   162	    pub read:  Vec<RelayUrl>,
   163	    pub write: Vec<RelayUrl>,
   164	    pub both:  Vec<RelayUrl>,
   165	    pub seen_from: Vec<RelayUrl>,          // ProvenanceRelayFact seed
   166	}
   167	```
   168	
   169	```rust
   170	// crates/nmp-nip65/src/cache.rs  ← concrete impl lives here
   171	
   172	pub struct InMemoryMailboxCache { /* HashMap<Pubkey, MailboxSnapshot> */ }
   173	impl MailboxCache for InMemoryMailboxCache { /* ... */ }
   174	```
   175	
   176	The `MailboxCache` trait is the seam M3 (LMDB persistence) replaces with a backing-store-aware implementation. The compiler does not know which backend it is reading.
   177	
   178	```rust
   179	// crates/nmp-nip65/src/routing.rs
   180	
   181	pub fn resolve_author_outbox(
   182	    cache: &dyn MailboxCache,
   183	    user_configured: &UserConfiguredRelays,
   184	    indexer_set: &[RelayUrl],
   185	    author: &Pubkey,
   186	) -> AuthorRouting { /* ... */ }
   187	
   188	pub fn resolve_author_inbox(
   189	    cache: &dyn MailboxCache,
   190	    user_configured: &UserConfiguredRelays,

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# M2 — Subscription compilation + outbox routing
     2	
     3	> Part of the [Build & Validation Plan](../plan.md). Arc 1 — Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack.
     4	
     5	**Demo product:** Same iOS app as [M1](m1-twitter-slice.md), but timeline subscriptions are routed per-author to those authors' write relays per NIP-65, not to the hardcoded primal/purplepag.es pair. Diagnostics screen visibly shows the per-relay fan-out and which authors each relay covers.
     6	
     7	**Scope.** The planner becomes a **subscription compilation stage** per the NDK/Applesauce lessons doc. Logical interests get compiled into per-relay plans; recompilation happens when relay metadata arrives late. NIP-65 routing is the default for both reads and writes. Provenance / NIP-65 / relay hints / user-configured relays are four distinct facts that inform each other but never collapse. Also includes **kind:3 auto-tracking** for the active account's follow list per the 2026-05-18 scope adjustments.
     8	
     9	**Subsystem deliverables.**
    10	
    11	- `nmp-nip65` protocol module: Mailboxes view module (parsed kind:10002); outbox routing as a planner subsystem; recompilation triggers (kind:10002 arrival, view open, relay reconnect).
    12	- Planner refactored from "hardcoded relay set" to "compiler of logical interests → per-relay plans." See `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7 (subscription compilation lessons).
    13	- Per-pubkey relay-list cache (durable, even before LMDB lands — keep it in-memory until [M3](m3-persistence.md), but the data model is correct).
    14	- Indexer fallback when a pubkey's kind:10002 is unknown: opportunistic discovery from a configurable indexer relay set.
    15	- Reverse-relay-coverage view for diagnostics: "this relay is serving N authors of our timeline."
    16	- **Kind:3 auto-tracking**: when a kind:3 (follow list) lands for the active account, the planner automatically refreshes the author-set of every subscription that depends on "the current user's follows" — recompiling transparently without the app re-issuing the REQ. Reference: NDK auto-follow kind:3 changes + Applesauce event-store query builder auto-tracking.
    17	
    18	**Exit gate.**
    19	
    20	- Bug-extinction test #3 (publish to wrong relays): no public API path lets the developer specify relays for a publish; explicit override action exists and produces a debug warning.
    21	- Subscription compilation correctness: for a timeline of 1000 authors, the compiled plan opens REQs only against the union of those authors' write relays (de-duplicated). Test asserts on the wire frame count.
    22	- Late-arriving kind:10002 triggers recompilation: an author whose mailbox was unknown gets re-routed once their kind:10002 lands, without the platform observing protocol churn.
    23	- Distinct-source visibility: the diagnostics screen shows the four relay-fact lanes (NIP-65 / hint / provenance / user-configured) separately.
    24	
    25	**Runnable artifact.** iOS app with measurably different relay-fan-out behavior; demo screenshot in `docs/perf/m2/outbox-routing.md` showing per-relay coverage.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:115:Compiler effect: every `InterestScope::ActiveAccount` interest is re-resolved as if newly opened. `InterestScope::Account(specific)` interests are untouched. `InterestScope::Global` interests are untouched.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:794:++- `Trigger::ActiveAccountChanged { from, to }` (`subscription-compilation/recompilation.md` §4.2) fires as a consequence of the state change. The planner re-runs `interests()` on every `ViewModule` whose registered interest carries `InterestScope::ActiveAccount` (`subscription-compilation/intro.md` §2.1 line 60 + §2.3); `InterestScope::Account(specific)` and `InterestScope::Global` interests are untouched.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:811:++7. **Assert specific-scoped views untouched:** before step 3, also open `ProfileView { pubkey: charlie_pk }` (an `InterestScope::Account(charlie)`-equivalent — actually Global since it names an explicit author). Assert this view's payload is not re-emitted after the switch; its underlying REQ stays alive on the same relay; no delta frames touch it. This is the symmetric assertion: the switch affects *only* `ActiveAccount`-scoped interests, per `subscription-compilation/recompilation.md` §4.2 line 113.

codex
**Findings**

1. [lattice.rs:108](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-core/src/planner/lattice.rs:108) implements Rule 1 backwards for wildcard kinds. Empty `kinds` means wildcard, so wildcard + `{1,6}` must merge to empty, not `{1,6}`. Fix `rule1_kinds` to return `BTreeSet::new()` when either side is empty, and update [lattice.rs:263](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-core/src/planner/lattice.rs:263).

2. [compiler.rs:417](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-core/src/planner/compiler.rs:417) plan-id binding does not match §3.4. It hashes the whole mailbox cache, omits `scope`, does not sort relay vectors, and does not include indexer/user-config versions. Fix to hash only referenced pubkeys (`authors`, `#p`, `addresses.pubkey`), include scope + indexer/config versions, and add tests for unrelated mailbox churn and indexer-set changes.

3. [plan.rs:23](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-core/src/planner/plan.rs:23) adds `RoutingSource::Indexer`, but the design says indexer fallback is UserConfigured lane 4, not a fifth lane. **Findings**

1. [lattice.rs:108](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-core/src/planner/lattice.rs:108) implements Rule 1 backwards for wildcard kinds. Empty `kinds` means wildcard, so wildcard + `{1,6}` must merge to empty, not `{1,6}`. Fix `rule1_kinds` to return `BTreeSet::new()` when either side is empty, and update [lattice.rs:263](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-core/src/planner/lattice.rs:263).

2. [compiler.rs:417](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-core/src/planner/compiler.rs:417) plan-id binding does not match §3.4. It hashes the whole mailbox cache, omits `scope`, does not sort relay vectors, and does not include indexer/user-config versions. Fix to hash only referenced pubkeys (`authors`, `#p`, `addresses.pubkey`), include scope + indexer/config versions, and add tests for unrelated mailbox churn and indexer-set changes.
